title: Dispatch architecture v2: /dispatch-middle test-author→implementer pipeline (central writes nothing)
description:
Goal: Get loom to push ALL subagent-able work to subagents, keeping the MAIN thread for planning + executive function with the user. Closes 3 problems with central doing work in-thread (Frank, 2026-06-07): (1) eats context needed for planning/executive function; (2) test-writer and code-writer become the SAME agent — a demonstrated anti-pattern (no independent verification; the test becomes a tautology shaped by the implementation); (3) central accumulates random unrelated context. Extends loom-yb5 (dispatch posture nudge+fan-out+worker-default).

REFRAME: loom-yb5 was a PUSH (nudge pressures toward dispatch); the deeper fix is a PULL — make dispatch LOWER-FRICTION than inline so the right thing is the easy thing. And the NEW piece loom-yb5 never addressed: SPLIT the variable middle's test-author and implementer into DIFFERENT agents.

Architecture: /dispatch-middle <bead> = a command+skill scripting central's 2-(or-3-)agent pipeline; central invokes once and WRITES NOTHING in the middle. One shared worktree (frank/<bead>), sequential INDEPENDENT agents: TEST-AUTHOR (briefed with ONLY the locked contract + interface -> RED test, commit, return failure verbatim) -> IMPLEMENTER (briefed with ONLY the RED-test-as-FILE + code area; 'make it pass, do NOT modify the test, stop-and-report if the test looks wrong' -> GREEN, commit; NEVER sees the author's reasoning, inherits the test as an ARTIFACT not a shared mind) -> optional VERIFIER (existing requesting-code-review) -> hand back to central for verify+merge+close+capture. Clean line: central = conversation + contract-lock (M1=user dialogue) + integration; workers = RED-test + GREEN-code + research + review, each with minimal context.

COMPOSITION: /dispatch-middle owns the WITHIN-bead test/code split; loom-yb5 fan-out detector owns ACROSS-bead parallelism (multiple beads each via /dispatch-middle). The dispatch-nudge hook is UPDATED to point at /dispatch-middle (push+pull). INTERLOCK with loom-tdua (design-phase epic): the test-author's contract IS the bead's RED: line (design emits contracts -> dispatch consumes them).

Children: T1 /dispatch-middle command+skill [foundation]; T2 bead-lifecycle-shell rewrite [dep T1]; T3 dispatch-nudge hook update [dep T1]; T4 bugfix/feature recipe M-step updates [dep T1]; T5 dogfood [dep T1-T4]. T1 -> {T2,T3,T4} 3-wide file-disjoint wave -> T5. DEFERRED (not v1): Workflow-tool multi-bead fan-out version, mandatory-verifier, context-scoping lint.
close_reason: Dispatch architecture v2 COMPLETE. T1 loom-0k68 (/dispatch-middle cmd+skill), T2 loom-6bv1 (bead-lifecycle-shell central-writes-nothing), T3 loom-l95f (dispatch-nudge→/dispatch-middle + test-file trigger), T4 loom-8crd (bugfix/feature M3/M4 RED-author vs GREEN-implementer split), T5 loom-t0l3 (dogfood: /dispatch-middle run on loom-9ng, independence verified). The friction-inversion pull primitive is live + dogfood-validated.

title: T5: dogfood — run /design-a-cycle on its own design (precipitate research drawers into the layered substrate)
description:
Goal: DOGFOOD — the first real use of /design-a-cycle is to capture ITS OWN design properly. Precipitate the three prose research drawers (loom-l0f 010d480b, loom-5w6 ed1f4600, loom-dwn d673e1e5) + the build-brainstorm checkpoint (957e6a40) into the layered substrate the system prescribes: an L1 KG spine (design-predicate triples linking the decisions) + a proper L2 DESIGN DOC drawer (with the state-header + locked-decisions + grounding) for the design-phase-system itself. This validates the substrate is buildable + inverts the prose-heavy/KG-thin imbalance the research flagged.
Files: (MemPalace only — no source change; drawer + KG)
Steps: 1. Run /design-a-cycle on topic "loom design-phase system" pointed at the existing drawers. 2. Precipitate the locked decisions (two-tier soundness, layered substrate, above-bead orchestrator, etc.) into KG triples + a consolidated L2 design-doc drawer with the state header. 3. Confirm the substrate round-trips (kg_query + drawer get) and that session-startup discovery (T3) surfaces it.
Acceptance: a layered substrate for the design-phase-system exists (L1 KG + L2 design-doc drawer); round-trips; surfaced by T3. First /design-a-cycle dogfood findings captured.
DEPENDS ON T1, T2, T3, T4 (needs the template + skill + discovery + conventions). Capstone.
close_reason: DOGFOOD COMPLETE — first real /design-a-cycle run. Precipitated the design-phase system's own design (research chain loom-l0f/5w6/dwn + build brainstorm 957e6a40) into the layered substrate it prescribes: L2 design-doc drawer f3206a700fc3ba086580b581 (STATE HEADER green/complete, 8 locked decisions w/ grounding+options+L3 specs) + 6 L1 KG design-predicate triples (grounded_in/emits_bead/soundness_tier/depends_on_invariant/is_design_drawer_for). Round-trips (kg_query + drawer get verified). Findings captured in-drawer (incl. T3 discovery filter correctly excludes complete cycles; worktree-sharing gap loom-fx9m from sibling loom-t0l3).

title: T4: CLAUDE.md design-cycle conventions (KG predicates + RED: line + above-bead note)
description:
Goal: Document the design-cycle CONVENTIONS in CLAUDE.md so the orchestrator + downstream projects share them.
Files: CLAUDE.md
Add a Conventions bullet cluster: (1) the SOFT recommended KG design-predicate set for the L1 spine (supersedes_design_of, grounded_in, emits_bead, soundness_tier, depends_on_invariant) — recommended, not a locked schema; composes with the open KG. (2) the RED: spec-line convention on beads spawned from a Tier-1 design decision (carries the decision's Given-When-Then or INVARIANT), parallel to the loom-asr Files: line; the implementation bead inherits its RED test from this. (3) the design-cycle-is-an-above-bead-orchestrator note: a design cycle is NOT a bead/epic; its state lives in the L2 design-doc drawer header + L1 KG; reason-in-prose, precipitate-into-structure; loom is opinionated about the structured destination + cadence, permissive about the prose reasoning surface, generative about human Diataxis.
Steps: 1. Edit CLAUDE.md Conventions section. 2. Full loom suite (no doc test breaks).
Acceptance: CLAUDE.md documents the 3 conventions; suite green.
File-disjoint from T1/T2/T3 -> parallel-wave member.
close_reason: Merged --no-ff. loom-sbth: CLAUDE.md design-cycle conventions. loom-l95f: dispatch-nudge→/dispatch-middle + test-file trigger (RED 12/7→GREEN 19/0). loom-9s7x: session-startup step 1d active-design-cycle discovery. Suite green.

title: T3: session-startup discovery of active design cycles
description:
Goal: session-startup discovers ACTIVE design cycles, since the orchestrator is not a bead and bd ready will not show it.
Files: skills/session-startup/SKILL.md
Add a step (near the in-progress-beads RESUME header): scan for active design cycles = open DESIGN DOC drawers (mempalace_search / list for the project wing) whose state header has unresolved [CLARIFICATION] markers or soundness != green. Surface a block: "ACTIVE DESIGN CYCLE: <topic> - cycle N, soundness <status>, M open markers, K research beads open" so a cold-start session resumes the design, not just bead work. Degrade-safe (mempalace offline / none found -> silent or one-line skip). INFO-only, never fails the skill.
Steps: 1. Edit session-startup SKILL.md to add the active-design-cycle discovery step + the surfacing block + degrade-safety. 2. Verify against the full loom suite (docs-strict etc. unaffected).
Acceptance: session-startup surfaces active design cycles alongside in-progress beads; degrade-safe; no regression.
File-disjoint from T1/T2/T4 -> parallel-wave member. Soft-references T1's state-header shape.
close_reason: Merged --no-ff. loom-sbth: CLAUDE.md design-cycle conventions. loom-l95f: dispatch-nudge→/dispatch-middle + test-file trigger (RED 12/7→GREEN 19/0). loom-9s7x: session-startup step 1d active-design-cycle discovery. Suite green.

title: T1: templates/design-doc/ skeleton (L2 living-design-document)
description:
Goal: Ship the L2 living-design-document template — the working-surface artifact a /design-a-cycle orchestrator maintains. Mirrors templates/diataxis/ (sed-substitution skeleton).
Files: templates/design-doc/, lib/tests/design-doc-template.test.sh
The skeleton: a DESIGN DOC drawer template with (a) a structured STATE HEADER section the orchestrator reads/updates each cycle — cycle-number, locked-decisions (list), open [CLARIFICATION] markers (list), soundness-status (red/amber/green), spawned research-bead IDs, target implementation-epic ID; (b) reasoning sections — Question/Scope, Decisions-locked (each cites grounding + options/why-not + optional L3 spec block: Given-When-Then or INVARIANT:), Grounding-checklist, Lineage. Variable tokens {{ topic }} / {{ wing }} substituted by sed (match templates/diataxis README mechanism).
Steps: 1. RED test (lib/tests/design-doc-template.test.sh, house fixture style mirroring lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh): substituting tokens + copying the template yields a well-formed design-doc with the state-header fields present + the decision/spec block shape; no surviving {{ }} placeholders. 2. Author templates/design-doc/. 3. GREEN + full loom suite.
Acceptance: template substitutes clean; state-header + reasoning sections present; test green; no regression.
NOTE: foundation. T2 (the skill) references this template. File-disjoint from T3/T4 -> parallel-wave member.
close_reason: Merged to main (--no-ff). loom-dhra: templates/design-doc L2 template, 28/28. loom-0k68: /dispatch-middle cmd+skill+contract test 29/29, installed+invocable. Full suite 60 files 0 failing.

title: T2: /design-a-cycle orchestrator skill + command
description:
Goal: The /design-a-cycle orchestrator skill + command — the above-bead campaign/arc primitive that drives the Plan->Research->Architect cadence over the layered substrate and hands off to create-beads. NOT a bead-lifecycle recipe; it ORCHESTRATES (spawns research-a-beads + an implementation epic) and is itself stateless beyond the substrate.
Files: skills/design-a-cycle/SKILL.md, commands/design-a-cycle.md
The skill: on invocation, READ the design-substrate STATE (the L2 design-doc drawer header + L1 KG) for <topic>; if none, scaffold one from templates/design-doc/ into the project wing. Then drive ONE cadence step (or loop), proposing the next action from state: Plan (invoke brainstorming to set/refine direction) -> Research (for each open [CLARIFICATION] marker that needs grounding, spawn a research-a-bead and record its ID in the header) -> Architect (precipitate each newly-locked decision into L1 KG triples [soft recommended design-predicate set] + the L2 Decisions-locked section + optional L3 executable spec) -> SOUNDNESS check (Tier-0: no unresolved markers + every locked decision cites grounding + constitution-consistent; Tier-1 optional) -> loop until green -> HANDOFF: run create-beads against the locked decisions to spawn the implementation epic; each bead from a Tier-1 decision carries a RED: line (its Given-When-Then / invariant) parallel to the Files: line. command = disable-model-invocation user door.
Steps: 1. Author SKILL.md (cadence + soundness + precipitate + handoff prose) + command. 2. Light contract/grep test that the skill documents the cadence steps + soundness gate + RED:-handoff (prose-skill test pattern). 3. install.sh re-run + verify invocable. 4. Full loom suite.
Acceptance: skill drives the cadence from substrate-state; soundness gate prose present; handoff emits RED: lines; installed + invocable.
DEPENDS ON T1 (references templates/design-doc/). File-disjoint from T3/T4.
close_reason: Merged --no-ff (a77ffd1). /design-a-cycle orchestrator skill + command + 42-assertion contract test. Above-bead campaign primitive: Plan→Research→Architect cadence over L1/L2/L3 substrate, two-tier soundness, create-beads handoff w/ RED: lines. Installed+invocable. Suite 61 green.

title: Design-phase system: /design-a-cycle above-bead orchestrator + layered substrate (v1)
description:
Goal: A loom design-phase system — an above-bead /design-a-cycle ORCHESTRATOR (a new campaign/arc unit, NOT a bead) that drives a Plan->Research->Architect cadence over a LAYERED living-design-substrate, with two-tier soundness, handing off to create-beads. Closes the long-identified gap: loom is implementation-anchored (bead = verified change, RED->GREEN), but the design phase produces decisions/understanding with no red-green test and had no loom home. GENERAL loom use, not just dreamer-engine.

Design source: brainstorm checkpoint drawer_loom_decisions_957e6a400c44af2cdc654670; research chain 010d480b82d00234d1e953b9 (loom-l0f survey) -> ed1f460072b7ede5711a3e21 (loom-5w6 two-tier soundness) -> d673e1e52ae1696f6d8e955f (loom-dwn layered KG-spine substrate).

Architecture (v1 = recipe + convention + 1 template): SUBSTRATE 3 layers (all MemPalace/existing) — L1 KG spine (source-of-truth; soft recommended design-predicate set: supersedes_design_of/grounded_in/emits_bead/soundness_tier/depends_on_invariant) + L2 design-doc drawer (templates/design-doc/ skeleton with a structured STATE HEADER above prose) + L3 optional executable specs (Given-When-Then / invariant-as-prose -> bead RED test). CADENCE: read-state -> Plan (brainstorming) -> Research (spawn research-a-beads) -> Architect (precipitate locked decisions into L1+L2+optional L3) -> soundness (Tier-0 coherence: no-open-markers + grounding + constitution-consistent; Tier-1 optional) -> loop until green -> handoff (create-beads implementation epic; Tier-1 decisions' beads carry a RED: line). STATE = substrate-as-state (no new bd entity); DISCOVERY = session-startup surfaces active design cycles. POSTURE: opinionated about the structured destination (L1/L3) + cadence; permissive about the prose reasoning surface (L2); generative about human Diataxis (deferred).

DEFERRED follow-ups (NOT v1, dogfood-gated by T5): scripts/loom-render-design (L1->L2 projection), hooks/design-drift-check.sh (drawer<->KG reconciliation), Diataxis-scaffold-from-KG generator.

Children: T1 templates/design-doc/ skeleton [foundation]; T2 /design-a-cycle skill+command [dep T1]; T3 session-startup discovery; T4 CLAUDE.md conventions; T5 dogfood-recapture-own-design [dep T1-T4]. T1/T3/T4 file-disjoint -> parallel wave.
close_reason: Design-phase system v1 COMPLETE. T1 loom-dhra (templates/design-doc L2 template), T2 loom-k0s8 (/design-a-cycle orchestrator skill+command), T3 loom-9s7x (session-startup active-cycle discovery), T4 loom-sbth (CLAUDE.md design-cycle conventions), T5 loom-70ye (dogfood: recaptured its own design into the layered substrate). The above-bead campaign/arc primitive + layered KG-spine substrate is live, installed, and dogfood-validated. Interlocks loom-5m94 (dispatch-v2) via the RED: line contract.

title: Research: agent-optimal vs human-optimal representation for design specs/research docs (structured/KG/compressed/formal vs prose) + the agent-substrate→Diátaxis generation model
description:
Extends loom-l0f + loom-5w6 (design-phase system research; drawers 010d480b + ed1f460072). Frank's reframe: this design-phase capability is for GENERAL loom use, not just dreamer-engine. KEY INSIGHT he raised: the prior two research passes surveyed only HUMAN-readable formats (Spec-Kit, OpenSpec, ADR, Gherkin, TLA+ — all markdown/prose for a person). But "optimal for a HUMAN to read" != "optimal for an AGENT to read and MAINTAIN." Loom needs BOTH: (1) human-facing Diataxis docs (all 4 quadrants), generated + kept in sync with every software change, optimized for human reading; (2) an AGENT-FACING working substrate — the research docs + design specs the AGENT reads and maintains for its OWN use — optimized for the agent, NOT human-optimal. The popular tools won't answer this. Goal: ground what representation is optimal for an agent to work from.

Operational question: What does current research/practice say about the representation an LLM/coding-agent reads and MAINTAINS most effectively for design specs + research docs + living architecture — as distinct from human-readable docs? And what does that imply for loom's two-representation model (agent-substrate as source-of-truth + Diataxis as a generated human projection), and for WHERE on loom's existing spectrum (prose-drawer -> AAAK-compressed -> KG-triples) the agent-optimal spec should sit?

FRAMING: loom is opinionated-on-process. The proposal forming: loom should be OPINIONATED about the agent-substrate format (a specific structured representation the agent maintains) and GENERATIVE about the human docs (render Diataxis FROM the substrate). The agent-substrate must be: token-efficient, structured/unambiguous, queryable/retrievable (not a monolithic doc), and cheap for an agent to UPDATE-IN-PLACE (vs re-weaving prose). Diataxis (human) is downstream-generated. This deepens the loom-l0f/5w6 "drawer-home, Diataxis-downstream" resolution with the real reason: agent-optimal != human-optimal -> they must be different artifacts with a generation relationship.

DIRECTIONS TO RESEARCH (M3, current-year filter; for each: what it is + source + does it favor STRUCTURED over PROSE for agent consumption + does it support cheap agent MAINTENANCE/update + retrieval/queryability):
1. Structured vs unstructured/prose context for LLMs: does the research show LLMs read+reason better from structured (JSON/YAML/typed-schema/tables) vs narrative prose? Token cost, ambiguity, instruction-following. (e.g. structured prompting, schema-guided generation, any empirical comparisons.)
2. Knowledge-graph grounding for agents: GraphRAG (Microsoft), KG-RAG, structured retrieval; KG-as-spec. Does a graph representation beat a doc for agent retrieval + maintenance? loom already has the MemPalace KG — is the SPEC better as graph than prose?
3. Agent memory / context-engineering architectures: how do agent frameworks store + retrieve + UPDATE their own working knowledge (vs human docs)? MemGPT/Letta, generative-agents memory, "context engineering" (the shift from prompt-eng), reflection/maintenance loops. What's the state of the art for an agent maintaining its OWN evolving knowledge artifact?
4. Spec formats for code-generation AGENTS specifically: what spec representation makes agentic code-gen most reliable? (spec-driven-dev for agents, intent specs, formal-ish specs as agent input, "specs as the new code" current-year discourse.)
5. Emerging agent-doc CONVENTIONS: llms.txt (llmstxt.org), AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md/cursor-rules, "docs for agents" — are these just human-markdown-relabeled, or genuinely agent-optimized? What do they get right/wrong about agent-vs-human representation?
6. Token-efficient / semantic compression for agent consumption: AAAK-style entity-coded compression, prompt compression (LLMLingua etc.), summarization-for-recall. Is a compressed/encoded spec more agent-optimal than full prose, and at what readability cost?
7. Formal/unambiguous-for-agents: does giving an agent a more FORMAL spec (typed contracts, invariants, state machines) reduce hallucination/re-derivation vs prose? Ties to loom-5w6 Tier-1 (executable specs). Is formality MORE agent-optimal even though humans find it harder?

M2 (internal): loom/HAW prior art on agent-vs-human representation — AAAK spec (the diary compression), the KG, the drawer format, any prior decision on "agent-optimal docs"; CLAUDE.md/rules as agent-facing; does any sibling project already separate agent-substrate from human-docs?

SYNTHESIZE (M4, opinionated): (a) what the research says is agent-OPTIMAL for a maintained spec/design artifact (structured? graph? compressed? formal? hybrid?) and WHY, distinct from human-optimal; (b) the recommended SHAPE of loom's agent-substrate (where on prose-drawer->AAAK->KG spectrum; one format or layered?); (c) the generation relationship (how the human Diataxis is rendered from the agent-substrate, and whether that's realistic to keep in sync); (d) does this CHANGE the loom-l0f/5w6 conclusions (e.g. is the living-design-doc actually a KG/structured artifact rather than a prose status-drawer?); (e) the product/process implication (what loom should be OPINIONATED about vs GENERATIVE about). Relate to loom-l0f composite + loom-5w6 two-tier soundness.

DELIVERABLE (M5, draft for central): decision-drawer body (extends the loom-l0f/5w6 chain) + 3-5 KG triples + follow-up bead candidates. Keep source URLs inline. NO code. Be opinionated; flag unverified; explicitly call out where the popular human-doc tools are the WRONG model for the agent-substrate.
close_reason: Research output (extends loom-l0f/5w6). CONFIRMS the layered hypothesis + sharpens it. The living-design-document is a 3-LAYER substrate: L1 MemPalace KG = structured SPINE / source-of-truth (queryable, update-in-place; already carries design predicates); L2 prose drawer = working-surface/rendering (DEMOTED from source-of-truth); L3 = loom-5w6 Tier-1 executable specs. Diátaxis renders downstream (scaffold+drift-signals from L1, prose authored-into — NOT full auto-render). KEY GUARDRAIL: structured-beats-prose holds for READ/RETRIEVE/UPDATE but HURTS constrained GENERATION (Let Me Speak Freely, arXiv 2408.02442) → reason-in-prose(L2), precipitate-into-structure(L1/L3); loom OPINIONATED about the structured destination, PERMISSIVE about the reasoning surface. SHARPEST NEGATIVE: AGENTS.md/llms.txt/cursor-rules are the WRONG model (minimal operational context, exclude architecture). REVISES loom-l0f #2 (living-doc != single prose drawer). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_d673e1e52ae1696f6d8e955f + 5 KG + 5 follow-up candidates.

title: Research: spec↔test↔docs convergence (BDD, property/model-based, TLA+, Diátaxis-as-spec) — refine loom design-phase soundness + living-doc home
description:
Extends loom-l0f (design-phase system research, drawer_loom_decisions_010d480b82d00234d1e953b9). Frank pushed on the SOUNDNESS model: the first pass concluded "fitness functions = BUILD layer; design soundness = coherence checklist." Frank surfaces three test<->spec / design<->docs boundary patterns that may REFINE that. Research each + synthesize the implications for the loom design-phase system.

Operational question: Do the spec-as-test patterns (BDD, property-based, model-based/formal) provide a richer DESIGN-phase soundness model than the coherence-checklist -- specifically by being specs that are ALSO executable, authored during design and verified during build (bridging design->build) -- and does Diataxis-as-spec (doc-first) answer where the living design doc should live (MemPalace drawer vs repo file)?

FRAMING (Frank's): the three thoughts are the three boundaries around the design phase -- spec-cap-test (behavioral)=BDD; spec-cap-test (structural/high-level)=property/model-based; spec-cap-docs=Diataxis-as-spec. The loom design-phase system sits in the middle; the question is which boundaries to collapse.

DIRECTIONS (M3 -- for each: what it is + canonical source + how it fits loom's 5 ethos axes + at what ALTITUDE it operates):
1. BDD as test-AND-spec: Gherkin / Given-When-Then, Cucumber "living documentation", Specification by Example (Gojko Adzic). Behavioral-contract specs authored during design that become executable acceptance tests at build. ASSESS: does the design artifact EMIT the RED acceptance tests the implementation beads turn GREEN? Altitude = behavior/use-cases, NOT architecture structure -- name that limit.
2. Property-based + model-based + formal specification at HIGH level: QuickCheck (Claessen/Hughes) + Hypothesis (properties-as-specs); model-based testing (abstract model as spec+oracle); TLA+ (Lamport) and Alloy (Jackson) -- formal DESIGN specs that are model-checkable BEFORE building. These specify invariants/models/laws = the architecture altitude. ASSESS: do these REVISE loom-l0f's "fitness functions = build only"? I.e. is the architectural invariant/model a DESIGN artifact authored at design time that is also checkable? Where do TLA+/Alloy fit loom (too heavyweight for a markdown+bash ethos? or the formal end of a spectrum loom can gesture at without adopting)?
3. Diataxis-as-spec / doc-first: README-Driven Development (Tom Preston-Werner), doc-driven development, design-docs-as-spec. In some orgs Diataxis Reference/Explanation is written FIRST as a spec; in others pure downstream. ASSESS: should the loom living-design-document BE a doc-first Diataxis Explanation page (a repo file that matures into shipped docs) vs a MemPalace status-drawer -- this answers loom-l0f fork-2 (living-doc home). Loom currently treats Diataxis as downstream (docs-scaffold, loom-9z1).

Also (M2 internal): does loom/HAW already touch BDD, property-based testing, TLA+/formal methods, or doc-first? (HAW is a live system -- check both wings + diary.)

SYNTHESIZE (M4): (a) how each refines the loom design-phase soundness model; (b) whether design soundness should include EXECUTABLE specs at multiple altitudes -- BDD (behavioral) + property/model (structural) -- that bridge design->build by emitting the bead RED tests; (c) the REVISED view on fitness-functions-at-design-time; (d) the living-doc-home recommendation (MemPalace drawer vs doc-first Diataxis vs both, with the drawer-vs-repo precedence rule in mind); (e) a one-paragraph spec<->test<->docs boundary map for loom. Relate everything back to the loom-l0f composite (Spec-Kit shape + ADR-as-drawers + create-beads handoff + constitution-gate).

DELIVERABLE (M5, draft for central to file): decision-drawer body (extends 010d480b; question / answer-up-front / per-direction fit + altitude / revised-soundness-model / living-doc-home recommendation / WHY-NOT / OPEN / lineage) + 3-5 KG triple proposals + follow-up bead candidates (title + one-line scope). Keep source URLs inline. NO code, no worktree. Be opinionated; flag anything unverified.
close_reason: Research output (extends loom-l0f). Changes TWO loom-l0f conclusions: (1) design soundness is TWO-TIER — Tier-0 coherence (floor, always) + Tier-1 OPTIONAL executable-spec emission at behavioral (BDD Given-When-Then) + structural (invariant/property) altitudes that bridge design->build by emitting the bead RED test; (2) fitness functions DO have a design-time role — the INVARIANT is a design artifact, only the CHECK is build-time (loom-l0f conflated authoring-time with runtime). Living-doc home RESOLVED: MemPalace status-drawer authoritative-on-intent, doc-first repo file rejected as home / downstream-render-only (RDD anti-waterfall). MAJOR internal finding: the 3 patterns are ALREADY LIVE in sibling HAW projects (OpenSpec malleus/e2e, rapid malleus, TLA+/Apalache sir/liza/tla_puzzles). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_ed1f460072b7ede5711a3e21 + 4 KG + 5 follow-up candidates.

title: Research: design-phase system that fits loom's tools + ethos (ADR/RFC/Shape-Up/arc42/fitness-functions survey)
description:
Goal: Determine what design/architecture-PHASE system best fits loom's tools (Claude Code + beads + MemPalace + recipes/hooks) and ethos (decision-capture, hooks-enforce, bead=verified-change, living MemPalace substrate), for the Plan->Research->Architect loop whose Architect step is an EVOLVING DESIGN DOCUMENT (Frank's framing, drawer_loom_decisions_af817d22ba51f9a69be86703). Loom is implementation-anchored (bead = verified change, RED->GREEN); the design phase produces understanding/decisions with NO red-green test and currently has no loom home. Survey external prior art, assess fit against the loom ethos, and synthesize the minimal loom-native shape.

Operational question: Which existing design-phase methodology/artifact-pattern(s) map cleanly onto MemPalace-as-living-substrate + bead-handoff, and what is the minimal loom-native shape (a recipe + a capture primitive + a soundness/verification analog) for the Plan->Research->Architect loop?

INTERNAL prior art to check (M2): loom/decisions (design-phase? constitution loom-8jz/ld4? Diataxis loom-9z1 Explanation quadrant?), hundred_acre_woods/decisions, the loom retrospective-dig (loom-z3m), bd memories, the claude-opus diary. NOTE: A1 quick scan found NO prior loom decision on a design-phase system -> treat as fresh territory, but confirm.

EXTERNAL prior art to survey + assess (M3) -- for EACH, what it is, and how well it fits loom (MemPalace-native? bead-handoff? lightweight? soundness model?):
- ADRs (Michael Nygard Architecture Decision Records) -- maps onto MemPalace decision drawers directly?
- RFC processes (Rust RFC, IETF, company RFC) -- the review-gated evolving design doc
- Design docs / narrative memos (Amazon 6-pager / PRFAQ, Google design docs)
- Shape Up "shaping" (Basecamp) -- a NAMED structured pre-build design phase that hands off to "building"; assess the shaping->betting->building handoff vs design->create-beads
- arc42 + C4 model (architecture documentation templates/structure)
- Evolutionary architecture + FITNESS FUNCTIONS (Neal Ford / Building Evolutionary Architectures) -- candidate analog for the missing RED->GREEN "soundness" check in the design phase
- Diataxis (already in loom -- Explanation quadrant)
- AGENT-NATIVE / spec-driven-development: any recent (current-year) tooling or methodology for LLM/agent-driven design phases, spec-first / intent-doc workflows, "living design doc" tools. Use a year filter.

Assess each against loom's ethos explicitly: (1) MemPalace-native (living, revisable, provenance-tracked) (2) bead-handoff (design -> create-beads) (3) capture-discipline (4) lightweight-not-heavyweight (5) a soundness/verification model that fits design (coherence/grounding, not RED->GREEN).

DELIVERABLE (M5, draft for central to file): a decision drawer synthesizing (a) which external pattern(s) fit + WHY NOT the others, (b) the minimal loom-native design-phase shape it suggests (recipe cadence + the living-design-document capture primitive + how it hands off to create-beads), (c) the "soundness verification" analog for the design phase, (d) OPEN questions. Plus 3-5 KG triples for the design-family relationships. NO code. Round-trippable.

Files: (none -- MemPalace drawer deliverable; no source change)
research-a-bead. Lineage: drawer af817d22 (design-phase gap, 2026-06-06), loom-yb5 (dispatch posture), loom-9z1 (Diataxis), constitution loom-8jz/ld4.
close_reason: Research output: design-phase system survey. Recommendation = 3-part composite — GitHub Spec-Kit SHAPE as backbone (living spec + immutable constitution + checklist/clarification-marker soundness; agent-native, Claude-Code, current-year) + ADR/decision-drawers as capture leaf (loom already has) + create-beads handoff (Shape Up shaping→building). Soundness analog = no-open-markers + grounding-checklist + constitution-gate (NOT fitness functions = build-layer). Loom constitution epic loom-6f8 CONVERGED with Spec-Kit's constitution.md. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_010d480b82d00234d1e953b9 + 5 KG triples. 4 follow-up bead candidates surfaced (not filed).

title: skill-redirect hook: superpowers:brainstorming → beadpowers:brainstorming in beads projects
description:
Goal: When the model spontaneously invokes the Skill tool with superpowers:brainstorming in a beads-tracked project, redirect it to beadpowers:brainstorming. The two skills have WORD-FOR-WORD identical descriptions, so on auto-trigger the model has no distinguishing signal and defaults to superpowers; loom wants beadpowers (design lands as beads). We do NOT control the plugin descriptions (plugin-owned, overwritten on upgrade), so a loom-owned PreToolUse hook is the robust fix. Bites every beads project, not just dreamer-engine.

Confirmed feasibility (2026-06-06, via claude-code-guide + this session transcript): Skill IS a matchable PreToolUse tool_name (the Agent SDK adds the Skill tool to allowedTools). Empirically the PreToolUse payload is tool_name=Skill and tool_input is {skill: "<name>", args: "<args>"} -- the skill name lives in tool_input.skill. exit 2 blocks tool calls and the hook stderr is fed back to the model on block (same as other loom hooks).

Design:
- hooks/skill-redirect.sh: PreToolUse. Parse tool_name + tool_input.skill (jq with no-jq sed fallback; mirror hooks/edit-write-pwd-guard.sh input-parse). Only act when tool_name=Skill. Gate on a .beads/ directory present in the project (walk up from cwd; skip silently otherwise). Redirect table (config-as-code, currently ONE entry): superpowers:brainstorming -> beadpowers:brainstorming. On a mapped skill in a beads project: exit 2 + stderr naming the replacement (e.g. "beads-tracked project: use beadpowers:brainstorming (design lands as beads), not superpowers:brainstorming"). Bypass LOOM_SKILL_REDIRECT_SKIP=1 (literal-1 match, per loom-b1l convention). Non-mapped skills / non-Skill tools / non-beads projects: exit 0 silently.
- Register the PreToolUse Skill matcher in settings.snippet.json (install.sh merges it into live settings.json; hooks/*.sh are auto-symlinked at install.sh ~line 185, so no separate install.sh edit needed).

WHY BLOCK, NOT NUDGE: a non-blocking nudge would let superpowers:brainstorming load and the wrong brainstorm starts -- only exit 2 prevents the load so the model re-picks. This is a legitimate block case (differs from the loom-yb5 dispatch-nudge by design); the env bypass keeps it escapable and recovery is trivial (re-invoke the right skill).

TDD (lib/tests/skill-redirect.test.sh, house fixture style -- feed PreToolUse JSON on stdin, assert exit code + stderr):
(a) {tool_name:Skill, tool_input:{skill:"superpowers:brainstorming"}} + .beads present -> exit 2, stderr names beadpowers
(b) {skill:"beadpowers:brainstorming"} -> exit 0 (no redirect loop)
(c) {skill:"feature-a-bead"} (unmapped) -> exit 0
(d) superpowers:brainstorming but NO .beads in the project -> exit 0 (gate)
(e) LOOM_SKILL_REDIRECT_SKIP=1 set -> exit 0 (bypass)
(f) non-Skill tool (e.g. tool_name:Bash) -> exit 0 (passthrough)

Acceptance: redirect fires only for the mapped skill in a beads project; blocks with a beadpowers-naming stderr; bypass + .beads-gate + unmapped-skills + non-Skill-tools all pass through exit 0; registered in settings.snippet.json. Full loom suite green.

POST-MERGE (central, the one undocumented behavior): verify the LIVE blocked-Skill UX -- confirm that exit 2 on a Skill call actually tells the model to pick beadpowers (vs failing oddly). If the UX is poor, file a follow-up.

Files: hooks/skill-redirect.sh, lib/tests/skill-redirect.test.sh, settings.snippet.json
bugfix-a-bead.
close_reason: skill-redirect PreToolUse hook: superpowers:brainstorming → beadpowers:brainstorming in .beads/ projects (identical descriptions → model coin-flipped to superpowers). exit-2 block (required — nudge would let wrong skill load), config-as-code redirect table, LOOM_SKILL_REDIRECT_SKIP=1 bypass. 7/7 tests; suite 58/0. LIVE-VERIFIED: invoking superpowers:brainstorming in loom was blocked + redirected end-to-end (confirms Skill is harness-hookable + exit 2 steers the model). Worker-dispatched. Merged.

title: history-mine full e2e run still 0 drafts post-loom-bzl (foreground-validated 1/5, background full-run 0/787)
description:
Goal: explain + fix why the FULL background mine of e2e-api-tests still emits 0 drafts AFTER loom-bzl fixed the parse (which was foreground-validated at 5 gated -> 1 salient draft).

Observations (2026-06-06): post-bzl full background run (out=~/.loom/mine-e2e) = 1833 harvested -> 787 gated -> .processed has 787 entries (all ran) -> 0 drafts, 0 triples. The capped FOREGROUND validation (--since=2026-05-15 --max-units=5) produced 1 draft with non-empty verbatim/synthesis/decision-triple. So the parser works in the foreground; the full background run yields nothing.

Hypotheses to test (systematic-debugging):
1. Background/headless claude invocation differs from foreground (auth / TTY / model resolution) so every reply comes back empty -> salient != true -> 0 drafts. Capture a raw reply from inside a backgrounded run (tee the claude output to a file) to confirm.
2. Rate-limiting across 787 sequential calls -> replies start erroring/empty partway; but that would still leave SOME early drafts, not 0.
3. Genuinely-low salience: e2e-api-tests history is dominated by test-infra churn (fix test / bump / update packages) that legitimately states no rationale -> high salient=false. But 0 from 787 including ~200 merged PRs (which have bodies/rationale) is implausibly low.
4. Interaction with the loom-mec hard-drop dropping more than intended, or the loom-bzl fence/format change behaving differently in bulk.

Repro: run the wrapper full (background) -> 0 drafts; same with a small --since + --max-units in the foreground -> >=1 draft. Diff the two contexts.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (likely the claude-invocation or a context-sensitive branch), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh; manifest evidence preserved at ~/.loom/mine-e2e/ (candidates.jsonl populated, drafts.jsonl empty, .processed=787).

bugfix-a-bead / systematic-debugging. Blocks productive e2e (and any real) mining. Lineage: loom-bzl (fixed the parse), e2e dogfood retrospective drawer_loom_decisions_4c31458cd8eabbe4e200fb90.
close_reason: Merged --no-ff. loom-6bv1: bead-lifecycle-shell dispatch-discipline→/dispatch-middle default, central-writes-nothing (41/0). loom-8crd: bugfix/feature M3/M4 RED-author vs GREEN-implementer split (17/0). loom-b10: ROOT CAUSE bash read truncates multi-line commit body at first newline→model saw no rationale→0 drafts; fix awk collapses newlines pre-join (RED→GREEN). Suite green.

title: T4: recipe prose — worker-default + mechanical inline threshold (bead-lifecycle-shell)
description:
Goal: Flip the recipe's stated default to worker-dispatch and replace the vague 'trivial/contained → inline' escape with a MECHANICAL threshold the nudge (T2) can reference.
Files: skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md (Dispatch discipline section); cross-ref from CLAUDE.md if needed.
Steps: 1. Rewrite Dispatch discipline: worker-dispatch is the DEFAULT for any bead with a RED->GREEN cycle; inline is the explicit, justified exception. 2. Define the mechanical inline escape: inline without justification ONLY for <=~15 lines AND single non-test file AND no new test (docs/config/prose edits qualify); anything with a RED->GREEN cycle defaults to worker. 3. Cross-reference T2's nudge + the dispatch field. 4. Verify prose-contract grep if applicable.
Acceptance: bead-lifecycle-shell states worker-default + the mechanical threshold; the inline escape is checkable (line/file/test based), not vibes.
Independent file (skill md) → parallel-safe. SOFT-PRECEDES T2.
close_reason: Epic loom-yb5 parallel wave (3 workers, worktree-isolated, dogfooding the dispatch posture being built). T1: workflow-state dispatch field + inline/dispatched counters + statusline (13/13). T3: scripts/loom-fanout-detect + session-startup step 6a + Files: convention (10/10). T4: bead-lifecycle-shell worker-default + mechanical inline threshold. Integrated 9b35297/94ef389/12e845b; suite 56/0. T2 (loom-h5s) now unblocked.

title: T3: fan-out detector at selection + Files: declaration convention (Component B)
description:
Goal: At bead selection, proactively PROPOSE a parallel worker wave when ready beads are independent — so central parallelizes without being told.
Files: skills/session-startup/SKILL.md, commands/working-a-bead.md (router) [or skills/*router*], optional scripts/loom-fanout-detect, lib/tests/loom-fanout-detect.test.sh
Steps: 1. RED (if helper): given a set of ready beads with dep edges + Files: lines, the detector groups beads that have NO dep edge between them AND NO overlapping Files:; a bead with no Files: declared → 'unknown, not safe to auto-parallelize'. 2. Implement detector (parse bd ready --json + each bead's Files: line + dep graph). 3. Wire into session-startup/router: when >=2 independent ready beads, surface 'loom-X/Y/Z are independent — dispatch N parallel workers? [y/edit/serial]' as the DEFAULT proposal (propose, not auto-dispatch). 4. Document the Files: declaration convention (CLAUDE.md / bead-lifecycle-shell): beads should declare Files: so the detector works; degrades conservative without it.
Acceptance: independent ready beads (no dep + no Files: overlap) are proposed as a wave; file-colliding-but-dep-independent beads (bn7.2/bn7.3 case) are NOT proposed parallel; missing Files: → conservative.
Independent of T1/T2/T4 (disjoint files) → parallel-safe wave member.

T1: workflow-state dispatch field + inline/dispatched counters + statusline (13/13). T3: scripts/loom-fanout-detect + session-startup step 6a + Files: convention (10/10). T4: bead-lifecycle-shell worker-default + mechanical inline threshold. Integrated 9b35297/94ef389/12e845b; suite 56/0. T2 (loom-h5s) now unblocked.

title: T1: workflow-state dispatch field + drift counter + statusline render
description:
Goal: Foundation for the dispatch-posture epic — a per-bead dispatch decision field + session drift counter, both surfaced on the statusline. Subsumes Component C.
Files: scripts/workflow-state, scripts/statusline.sh, lib/tests/workflow-state.test.sh (or a new lib/tests/dispatch-state.test.sh)
Steps: 1. RED: workflow-state set/get 'dispatch' accepts worker | inline:<reason> | (unset); setting dispatch=worker increments a session 'dispatched' counter, dispatch=inline increments 'inline'; statusline renders 'dispatch:<v>' + 'inline:N/dispatched:M'. 2. Implement in workflow-state (new field + two counters, auto-increment on set) + statusline render. 3. Verify round-trip + counters + render.
Acceptance: dispatch field round-trips; counters increment correctly on set; statusline shows both; existing workflow-state/statusline tests still green.
NOTE: foundation — blocks T2 (nudge hook reads/sets this field). Independent of T3/T4 (disjoint files) → parallel-safe with them.

T1: workflow-state dispatch field + inline/dispatched counters + statusline (13/13). T3: scripts/loom-fanout-detect + session-startup step 6a + Files: convention (10/10). T4: bead-lifecycle-shell worker-default + mechanical inline threshold. Integrated 9b35297/94ef389/12e845b; suite 56/0. T2 (loom-h5s) now unblocked.

title: T2: per-bead dispatch-nudge hook (Component A)
description:
Goal: Non-blocking forcing function nudging central toward worker-dispatch as the default, once per bead at the decision point.
Files: hooks/dispatch-nudge.sh, install.sh (wire PreToolUse Edit/Write), lib/tests/dispatch-nudge.test.sh
Steps: 1. RED: hook fires ONCE per bead when bead in_progress AND workflow-state dispatch UNSET AND target is a source file (not docs/config/test) → emits non-blocking system-reminder 'Default is dispatch a worker for the variable middle; set dispatch=worker (then dispatch) or dispatch=inline:<reason>'. Once dispatch set → silent. If dispatch=worker but central still Edits source → softer 'declared-worker-but-editing-inline' reminder. Non-blocking (exit 0 always; reminder via stderr/JSON). 2. Implement + wire into install.sh. 3. Verify: unset→nudge once, inline:<reason>→silent, worker+edit→mismatch reminder, docs/test target→silent.
Acceptance: nudge fires once/bead on unset+source-edit; silent after dispatch set; never blocks; install.sh links it; tests green.
DEPENDS ON T1 (reads/sets the dispatch field). SOFT-PRECEDED BY T4 (the nudge message references the worker-default + inline-threshold prose). Files disjoint from T3/T4.
close_reason: Component A: non-blocking per-bead dispatch-nudge PreToolUse hook (hooks/dispatch-nudge.sh). Fires once/bead when in_progress + workflow-state dispatch unset + source-file edit → reminder that worker-dispatch is the default; dispatch=worker+source→mismatch reminder; inline/docs/test→silent. Bypass LOOM_DISPATCH_NUDGE_SKIP=1. 16/16 tests; suite 57/0; registered live. Worker-dispatched (dogfood). Merged.

title: Restore central-agent dispatch posture (worker-default + parallel-by-default)
description:
Goal: Fix the observed drift where the main/central agent does most work INLINE and SERIAL instead of dispatching workers by default and parallelizing — the user had to explicitly tell it to dispatch/parallelize most of the time (named 2026-06-06; the 7-bead bn7 session is exhibit A: all inline, all serial).

Root causes (from that session's retrospective): (1) the inline escape is a ratchet — each inline bead builds context that makes the next feel more justified; (2) dispatch discipline lives only as PROSE in the recipes, and per loom's own 'hooks enforce, prose slips' lesson it slips; (3) bd ready is popped as a serial queue — independence is computed at bead CREATION (splitting heuristic) but never re-surfaced at WORK time.

Design (brainstorm 2026-06-06, beadpowers): make worker-dispatch + parallelism the VISIBLE DEFAULT via nudges + visibility, NOT hard blocks (blocks become bypass-magnets — edit-after-failure-guard was marker-bypassed 4x in one session). Three components over a shared workflow-state 'dispatch' field:
- A (T2): per-bead non-blocking dispatch-nudge hook.
- B (T3): fan-out detector at selection (dep-graph + Files: overlap).
- C (folded into T1): inline/dispatched drift counter on statusline.
Plus T4: recipe prose flipping the default to worker + a mechanical inline threshold.

Honest scope: tooling makes the right behavior low-friction + makes drift visible; it does not GUARANTEE compliance (nudge-not-block by design). The rest is central's posture.

Children: T1 (foundation: dispatch field + counter + statusline), T2 (nudge hook, dep T1), T3 (fan-out detector + Files: convention), T4 (recipe prose). T1/T3/T4 are file-disjoint → a parallel-safe 3-wide wave; T2 depends on T1; T4 soft-precedes T2. Eat-our-own-dogfood: build T1/T3/T4 as a parallel worker wave.
close_reason: Epic complete: central-agent dispatch posture restored. T1 dispatch field+counter+statusline, T2 nudge hook (live), T3 fan-out detector+Files: convention, T4 worker-default+mechanical inline threshold. Built largely via parallel worker waves (dogfood). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_f7301fa96b87c3b0e8c71f92.

title: edit-after-failure-guard false-positives on 'N failed' substring in GREEN test output
description:
hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh scans recent Bash output for test-failure markers; its marker detection matches the bare substring 'failed', so GREEN summaries like 'Tests: 15 passed, 0 failed' trip it. Since the test RUN is the last action (no test-file edit follows a run), it then blocks the next legitimate source edit. Recurred 4x in the 2026-06-03 bn7 session (loom-6ah, bn7.5, loom-kx2/mec) — each time worked around via the loom-n1q marker bypass (touch .claude/no-edit-after-failure-guard), which is friction + risks leaving the guard disabled.

Fix: the failure-marker regex must NOT match '0 failed' / 'N failed' / '0 failures' in an otherwise-passing summary. Match real failure signatures instead — e.g. a nonzero count ('[1-9][0-9]* failed', 'FAILED', 'FAIL:', non-zero exit markers) and explicitly exclude '0 failed'. RED test: feed a transcript-tail fixture containing 'Tests: N passed, 0 failed' + a subsequent source edit; assert the guard does NOT block (exit 0). Sibling-class to loom-6iy (both naive-substring bugs), but a different file. Files: hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh + its test. bugfix-a-bead.
close_reason: loom-9ng: edit-after-failure-guard FAIL_RE count-alternatives tightened to [1-9]\d* so '0 failed'/'N passed, 0 failed' GREEN summaries no longer block; non-zero failures + FAIL: still block (target 50/0, suite 62 green). loom-t0l3: DOGFOOD of /dispatch-middle SUCCEEDED — ran test-author→implementer split on loom-9ng in one shared worktree; implementer made GREEN from the RED-test-as-artifact alone, never seeing the author's reasoning; independence verified by construction.

title: history-mine gate over-admits routine commits (upgrade/lint/chore/update-packages) — expand junk-drop
description:
Surfaced by the e2e-api-tests dogfood (loom-bn7.5 recon, 2026-06-03): a bounded dry-run gated 36/337, of which ~1/3 were routine non-decisions — 'upgrade ck go packages', 'update ck go packages', lint fixes, 'fix readings.facility test', '(FOR NOW) make ttls longer'. The heuristic gate's hard-drop list in _lmh_score_commit (lib/loom-mine-history.sh) covers wip|fixup|squash|typo|bump|merge|revert but NOT upgrade|update.*packages|lint|chore — so dependency bumps and lint/test-fix churn survive to the (paid) LLM pass. The LLM trust-gate drops most (no rationale) but it inflates the gated count = cost.

Fix: extend the junk-subject hard-drop case in _lmh_score_commit with: upgrade*, *upgrade*, 'update * packages'/'update * pkgs', lint*, *lint, chore*, and possibly 'fix * test'/'de-flake'. Keep conservative — only DROP subjects that are clearly routine; don't drop substantive commits that merely mention these words in a rationale.
RED test: lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh — add junk subjects 'upgrade ck go packages', 'fix lint errors', 'chore: bump deps' to the gate fixture; assert dropped.
Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh. bugfix-a-bead.
close_reason: Dogfood-driven (first live /loom-mine-history dry-run on e2e-api-tests). loom-kx2: wrapper default wing → verbatim basename (e2e-api-tests not e2e_api_tests), agrees with audit-project. loom-mec: gate unconditionally hard-drops routine churn (upgrade/lint/chore/update-deps) — e2e gated 36→30. Engine 87/0, cmd 24/0, suite 54/0. Merged 17e9f9a.

title: loom-mine-history default-wing uses -→_ (wrong for dash wings like golden-path)
description:
Surfaced reconciling loom-6ah. scripts/loom-mine-history derives the default MemPalace wing from the repo basename with -→_ (dash→underscore). That is wrong for dash-style wings: a repo 'golden-path' → wing 'golden_path', but the actual wing is 'golden-path'. loom-mine-history FILES mined drawers into this wing; audit-project (loom-6ah, now via scripts/loom-audit-resolve) SEARCHES the VERBATIM wing. For a dash-named repo they DIVERGE → audit would miss the mined drawers.

Fix: change scripts/loom-mine-history default wing to VERBATIM basename (no _↔- substitution), matching scripts/loom-audit-resolve + skills/audit-project/SKILL.md. Verbatim is the only rule correct for both underscore (liza_base) and dash (golden-path) wings.
RED test: extend lib/tests/loom-mine-history-cmd.test.sh — a repo dir 'golden-path' resolves wing 'golden-path' (verbatim), not 'golden_path'.
Files: scripts/loom-mine-history, lib/tests/loom-mine-history-cmd.test.sh. bugfix-a-bead.
NOTE: the bn7.4 close drawer claimed -→_ 'mirrors audit-project --wing' — it did not; audit uses verbatim. This reconciles the cross-primitive convention.
close_reason: Dogfood-driven (first live /loom-mine-history dry-run on e2e-api-tests). loom-kx2: wrapper default wing → verbatim basename (e2e-api-tests not e2e_api_tests), agrees with audit-project. loom-mec: gate unconditionally hard-drops routine churn (upgrade/lint/chore/update-deps) — e2e gated 36→30. Engine 87/0, cmd 24/0, suite 54/0. Merged 17e9f9a.

title: history-mine tier-2: skill-side arc filing + anchor->drawer_id cross-link upgrade
description:
Follow-up to loom-bn7.2 (which shipped the lib half: --synthesize emits <out>/arcs.jsonl). Wire arc filing into skills/loom-mine-history/SKILL.md:
1. After the per-unit drafts.jsonl filing loop, read <out>/arcs.jsonl; for each arc: mempalace_check_duplicate(drawer_body) -> if not dup, mempalace_add_drawer(wing, room=decisions, content=drawer_body) -> mempalace_tag_drawer(id, provenance, mined) + tag synthesis=arc.
2. UPGRADE the constituent links: the lib emits arcs linking constituents by source ANCHOR (PR#/SHA) because it has no drawer_ids. The skill files per-unit drawers FIRST (collecting their returned drawer_ids keyed by source_id), THEN files arcs, rewriting each constituent anchor -> the real drawer_id (or mp:// URI) so the arc drawer cross-references filed drawers, not just anchors.
3. Surface 'filed K arc drawer(s) linking N per-unit drawers' in the adoption summary.
4. Pass --synthesize through commands/loom-mine-history.md.

Files: skills/loom-mine-history/SKILL.md, commands/loom-mine-history.md.
NOTE: Claude-executed MCP prose, NOT bash-testable; verified at first live dogfood (like bn7.4 filing loop + loom-zcv watermark round-trip). The lib half (--synthesize clustering + arc narration + arcs.jsonl emit) is DONE in bn7.2.

title: history-mine watermark: skill-side KG round-trip (read --since-sha, write history_mined_through)
description:
Follow-up to loom-bn7.3 (which shipped the lib half). Wire the skill-side watermark round-trip into skills/loom-mine-history/SKILL.md:
1. BEFORE invoking the wrapper: mempalace_kg_query(<repo-entity>) for an existing 'history_mined_through' fact; if present, pass --since-sha=<SHA> to mine only post-watermark units.
2. AFTER the filing loop completes: read <out>/watermark (the HEAD the lib mined through) and file mempalace_kg_add(<repo>, 'history_mined_through', <SHA>) so the next run is incremental. If a prior fact exists, invalidate/replace it.
3. Surface 'resuming from watermark <short-sha> (N new units since last mine)' in the cost-preview beat.
Also wire --resume into the skill's interrupted-run recovery story.

description:
Goal: Make audit-project aware of unmined decision history. Default audit (no flag) only FLAGS the gap; the --mine-history flag delegates to /loom-mine-history.

title: /loom-adopt orchestrator: full-treatment brownfield onboarding
description:
Goal: One-shot 'make this repo fully loom-standard' orchestrator composing existing primitives + history-mine.

Files: commands/loom-adopt.md, skills/loom-adopt/SKILL.md
Phases (dependency-ordered): P1 workflow-infra (DELEGATE to audit-project --apply — DRY, do NOT re-enumerate the checklist) -> P2 scripts/ scaffold [skip if loom-oxs not landed] -> P3 docs-scaffold -> P4 history-mine (needs wing from P1) -> P5 constitution + onboarding beads + wing tunnels [skip if constitution not landed]. P2/P3 order-independent.
Behavior: PER-PHASE CHECKPOINT interactivity (announce->confirm->run->show->proceed; not one-shot autonomous, not per-file nag); history-mine phase nests its own cost-preview gate. GRACEFUL DEGRADATION: unbuilt-primitive phases skipped with logged reason; phase list enumerated dynamically from what's installed. IDEMPOTENT+RESUMABLE: re-run = re-audit skip-satisfied + incremental mine via watermark; interrupted -> resume unfinished phase. Doubles as 'refresh to current loom standards'. OUTPUT: adoption report (installed/scaffolded/mined/skipped-why/beads-filed).
Steps: 1. SKILL.md phase machine + checkpoint logic. 2. command wrapper. 3. Tests: phase sequencing, graceful-skip of unbuilt primitives, idempotent re-run, mid-phase resume. 4. install.sh re-run + verify invocable.
Acceptance: composes the live primitives; skips unbuilt ones with reason; re-run is idempotent; resumable.
Soft-depends loom-oxs (scripts/) + loom-8jz/ld4 (constitution) — degrades gracefully, does NOT block.
close_reason: wave-2 parallel: DRY hook-helpers (13 hooks refactored, behavior-preserved), capture-fanout default at D3, /loom-adopt orchestrator, history-mine watermark round-trip. All merged --no-ff, 70/70 suite green.

title: /loom-mine-history command + skill (standalone primitive entry)
description:
Goal: The standalone user entry point that wraps the history-mine engine. Thin orchestration over lib/loom-mine-history.sh.

Files: commands/loom-mine-history.md, skills/loom-mine-history/SKILL.md
Steps: 1. Write SKILL.md: invoke the engine against a target repo (default cwd git root, --root override), surface the cost-preview gate, present the adoption-of-history summary. 2. Write commands/loom-mine-history.md (disable-model-invocation, pass-through flags --root/--since/--since-release/--max-units/--dry-run). 3. Verify install.sh --check shows the new symlinks WOULD link (globs skills/*/SKILL.md + commands/*.md); run install.sh; confirm /loom-mine-history invocable.
Acceptance: command + skill installed and invocable; flags pass through to the engine; cost-preview gate surfaces to the user.
NOTE: per the loom-k2g.7 finding this session — RUN install.sh after creating, recipe is not invocable until symlinked.

title: history-mine incremental: watermark + range flags + resumability
description:
Goal: Make history-mine cheap to re-run and bounded on huge repos. Additive over the core engine.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (extends core engine — SEQUENTIAL, shares file), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh
Features:
- Watermark: store last-mined HEAD SHA as KG fact <repo>->history_mined_through-><SHA>; re-runs harvest only units AFTER the watermark.
- Range flags: --since=<date>, --since-release=<tag>, --max-units=N (cap, most-recent-first). Default remains full-history.
- Resumability: checkpoint filed drawers; interrupted run resumes from last filed unit; mempalace_check_duplicate keeps re-files idempotent.
Steps: 1. RED tests: re-run after watermark harvests only new units; --since/--max-units bound the candidate set; interrupted+resumed run does not re-file. 2. Implement. 3. Verify watermark advances + reads back; re-run is incremental + idempotent.
Acceptance: second run mines only post-watermark units; range flags bound correctly; resume is idempotent.
NOTE: sequential after core engine (shared file).
close_reason: Lib half shipped: --since-sha watermark-consume bound (precedence over --since-release), <out>/watermark emit on real pass, --resume no-re-spend checkpoint via <out>/.processed; pinned --since/--since-release range bounding. 68/68 tests (was 49). Merged f307a98. Skill-side KG round-trip = follow-up loom-zcv.

title: history-mine core engine: harvest + heuristic gate + LLM salience/draft + cost-preview gate
description:
Goal: The minimum SAFE history-mine primitive — tier-1 per-unit decision archaeology with the mandatory cost gate. Independently useful before synthesis/incremental land.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh
Pipeline (stages 1-3+5 tier-1):
1. HARVEST — detect gh access (gh auth status + gh repo view); enumerate merged PRs (gh pr list --state merged --json + review threads via gh api); ALWAYS git log merge/squash commits + release tags. Unified candidate list tagged by source (PR# or SHA).
2. HEURISTIC GATE (no LLM) — score candidates: PR=review-discussion/body-len/breaking-design-RFC-labels/closes-issue/decision-shaped-files(schema,migrations,*.proto,interfaces,config)/merge-not-revert; commit=substantial-msg/not-{wip,fixup,typo,bump,merge main}/decision-files/release-tag. Above-threshold survive.
3. LLM SALIENCE+DRAFT (per survivor) — TRUST GATE: skip if source text does NOT state a rationale; else draft drawer = verbatim quote block (commit msg/PR body/key review comments) + clearly-separated synthesis + source anchor (SHA/PR#/URL/date/author).
5. FILE — mempalace_check_duplicate first; add_drawer room=decisions + provenance:mined tag; KG triples (PR#->decided->X, ->mined_from->repo, ->authored_by->Z).
COST-PREVIEW GATE (MANDATORY): real dry-run BEFORE the LLM pass — 'N harvested -> M gated -> ~M LLM reads, est cost/time' -> confirm/abort. Plus a --dry-run mode that emits drafts WITHOUT filing (makes the whole thing testable without a live palace).

Steps: 1. RED tests (gh stubbed via PATH-prepended fake binary echoing canned JSON, per upstream-a-bead + bd-stub pattern; git-fixture repo with known commits/PRs/tags). 2. Implement stages. 3. Verify gate filters, salience SKIPS no-rationale units, drawer carries verbatim+provenance tag+anchor, --dry-run files nothing, cost-preview prints before any LLM call.
Acceptance: tests pass; no-rationale units produce no drawer; --dry-run is side-effect-free; cost-preview gate cannot be bypassed silently.
close_reason: history-mine core engine shipped: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (HARVEST+GATE+cost-preview+LLM-draft, lib-drafts/skill-files seam, 49 fixture tests). Merged --no-ff. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_490284f114c5a773ee3456cf.

title: history-mine tier-2 synthesis: cluster salient units into narrative arc drawers
description:
Goal: Add the second tier — synthesize a HANDFUL of narrative arc drawers ('how auth evolved', 'the great DB migration') from the salient per-unit results. Additive over tier-1; ships after the core engine.

Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh (extends core engine — SEQUENTIAL after core, shares the file), lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh
Steps: 1. RED test: given a set of salient units clustered by theme/file-area/time, assert a narrative arc drawer is produced that LINKS to the per-unit drawers (cross-references their drawer_ids/anchors). 2. Implement clustering + arc-narrative drafting. 3. Verify arc drawers link back; verbatim discipline preserved.
Acceptance: tier-2 produces few high-value arc drawers, each linking its constituent per-unit drawers; no duplication of tier-1 content.
NOTE: shares lib/loom-mine-history.sh with the core engine bead — sequential, NOT parallel-dispatchable.
close_reason: Lib half shipped: --synthesize opt-in tier-2 — bash-cluster salient units by decision-file-area (_lmh_cluster_key), one LLM call per >=2-unit cluster narrates an arc drawer linking constituents by anchor, no verbatim duplication, <out>/arcs.jsonl. Fixed latent grep-c 0\n0 count bug. 82/82 tests. Merged bd6e6e6. Skill-side arc filing = follow-up loom-68r.

title: [epic] Brownfield adoption: decision-archaeology history mining + /loom-adopt orchestrator
description:
Close the brownfield asymmetry: a greenfield loom project accretes MemPalace memory naturally; a brownfield repo starts with an empty palace but a rich decision record already sitting in git + GitHub PRs. Mine that history into 'decision archaeology' (provenance-tagged decision drawers + KG triples) so loom's recall loop (bug-family-search, design-moment recall) works on day one.

Full design locked in MemPalace drawer drawer_loom_decisions_e43e3693c8ee82e3bc6e34c6 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-29 brainstorm).

Two new surfaces:
- /loom-mine-history (NEW primitive) — 5-stage pipeline: HARVEST (gh PRs+reviews where access; git log merge/squash/tags always) -> HEURISTIC GATE (no LLM) -> LLM SALIENCE+DRAFT (trust gate: skip if source doesn't STATE a rationale; verbatim quotes + separated synthesis + source anchor) -> SYNTHESIS tier-2 (narrative arc drawers) -> FILE (check_duplicate, add_drawer decisions+provenance:mined tag, KG triples). Full-history default + flags, MANDATORY cost-preview dry-run gate, watermark for incremental, resumable.
- /loom-adopt (orchestrator 'full treatment') — composes audit-project --apply (infra, DELEGATED/DRY) + scripts/ scaffold + docs-scaffold + history-mine + constitution; per-phase checkpoint, graceful degradation, idempotent+resumable.

Plus /audit-project --mine-history flag (informational gap, not autofix) + docs.

Dependency: history-mine engine blocks the command, audit flag, and orchestrator. /loom-adopt soft-depends on in-flight loom-oxs (scripts/) + loom-8jz/ld4 (constitution) but degrades gracefully.

title: audit-project portability: --root/--wing flags + docs/.no-diataxis opt-out + primitive autodetect (orphan WIP rescue)

title: cwd-drift-guard hook (loom-d2o) false-positive on workers in agent-worktrees blocks bd update --claim
description:
## Symptom

Workers dispatched via Agent + isolation: worktree run in .claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/. The cwd-drift-guard hook (hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh, loom-d2o) intercepts bd update/close, git merge/push, bd dolt push when cwd matches .claude/worktrees/agent-*/ — designed for CENTRAL drift detection but matches ANY cwd in that pattern, including legitimate worker contexts.

## Reproduction (3x observed in loom-k2g wave 1, 2026-05-27)

- Worker loom-k2g.2 first dispatch: burned 14 tool calls trying to bypass the hook with LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP=1 prefix. Hook reads env at scan time (before bash parses the assignment), so prefix-style bypass doesn't work. Worker returned without code; central had to cleanup + re-dispatch.
- Worker loom-k2g.1: apparently DID get it to work via env-var bypass (unclear exact mechanism — contradicts worker .2's experience).
- Worker loom-k2g.6: skipped claim, recommended central handle it.

## Hypothesis

cwd-drift-guard's match pattern '.claude/worktrees/agent-*/' is too broad. Designed for CENTRAL's persistent-bash drifting into a worker's worktree post-dispatch (per loom-d2o drawer drawer_loom_decisions_*). Worker agents legitimately operate FROM that path and should be exempted.

## Candidate fixes

- (a) Detect worker context — e.g., AGENT_ID env var, parent process inspection, or sentinel file dropped by Agent harness. Skip hook when worker context detected.
- (b) Make LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP work at exec-time (post-Bash-tool-call), not scan-time. Then workers can prefix.
- (c) Document the workaround pattern (central pre-claims, brief skips claim step) as canonical worker discipline — codify in .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md and bead-lifecycle-shell.

## Acceptance

- Workers can run bd update --claim from their own worktree without bypass ceremony OR docs explicitly capture the pre-claim+skip-brief pattern as canonical.
- Regression test: dispatched-agent simulation in lib/tests/ that exercises bd update --claim from a fake .claude/worktrees/agent-*/ path.

## Prior art

- loom-d2o (original hook design, 2026-05-27 same day) — central-drift focus, no worker-context handling.
- loom-k2g wave 1 (2026-05-27) — 3 workers, 3 different experiences with the hook.
- drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604 'Friction surfaced' section captures the in-flight observations.
- drawer (filing now after this bead) loom-k2g WAVE 1 SHIPPED — full friction writeup.

## Execution

bugfix-a-bead recipe. Worktree on frank/<this-bead-id>. RED test reproduces hook block on simulated worker context. GREEN fix per the chosen option above (a/b/c).
close_reason: cwd-drift-guard worker-context exemption shipped in merge 56c12c444fccad5c2362a25b870541e9239d9172 (worker commit 905cdf4). hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh now checks PreToolUse Bash payload for top-level agent_id / agent_type (CC's documented subagent markers — different schema from SessionStart payloads, so NOT reused via lib/subagent-detect.sh). Workers in .claude/worktrees/agent-*/ paths legitimately allowed; central-drift detection regression-guarded. 8 new fixture cases (29→37/37 GREEN), including regression guard for original loom-d2o behavior. Eliminates the wave-2 workaround (pre-claim centrally + skip-claim brief); env-var bypass still works.

description:

3. Fixture tests: cleanly stub bd + git status

Acceptance: never removes dir with watch-bead pointing at it; never removes dir with uncommitted work; user assent required for every removal.

close_reason: /loom-upstream-gc slash command shipped in merge f1cd2a492e580b81bcc86e79d2ee2e0788b0df57 (worker commit 4011db1). Interactive prune of stale ~/.loom/upstream clones. title: audit-project Item 12: encode additive-hook-layering + add real AUTOFIX paths (skip-worktree + commit-removal)
description:
## Why this exists

Item 12 of /audit-project (duplicate hook detection via find-hook-dups.sh) WARNs on duplicates but its resolution guidance has no AUTOFIX path. The currently-implied mitigation (empty-arrays in settings.local.json to cancel inherited hook registrations) was VERIFIED INERT on 2026-05-27 in e2e-api-tests: Claude Code's hook layering is **additive across all layers** (plugin + user-global ~/.claude/settings.json + project-tracked .claude/settings.json + project-local .claude/settings.local.json). Empty arrays don't cancel; they just add zero entries to the union. After applying the override, bd prime still fired 3 times in a fresh SessionStart.

Frank's session captured this as KG fact: `Claude Code settings hooks → merge_semantics_are → additive across all layers` — durable. Resolution that actually worked was `git update-index --skip-worktree .claude/settings.json` + clean the hooks locally. Manual, brittle on upstream changes (any future pull touching the file errors with 'would be overwritten').

## What this delivers

Three sub-deliverables under audit-project's Item 12 lineage:

### A. Skip-worktree AUTOFIX path

Tag: `[AUTOFIX:dedup-hook-skip-worktree]`. When find-hook-dups.sh detects a project-tracked SessionStart/PreToolUse hook that's ALSO registered by the plugin or user-global layer, /audit-project --apply-onboarding offers to:
1. `git update-index --skip-worktree <project>/.claude/settings.json`
2. Strip the duplicate hook from the local copy
3. Log the recovery snippet to .claude/loom-audit-state.json (or a sidecar) for the inevitable next upstream change to settings.json

Recovery snippet baked into AUTOFIX log:
```bash
git update-index --no-skip-worktree .claude/settings.json
git stash
git pull
git stash pop
# then re-apply skip-worktree + strip via /audit-project --apply-onboarding
```

Per-user, reversible. The default AUTOFIX path for this Item.

### B. Commit-removal AUTOFIX path

Tag: `[AUTOFIX:dedup-hook-commit]`. Same detection, different resolution: remove the duplicate hook from the tracked file and commit. Requires a confirmation prompt because it changes shared content — non-loom devs touching the repo would lose the hook registration.

Confirmation prompt explicitly names: 'This commits a change to .claude/settings.json that assumes loom adoption for all devs on this repo. Non-loom devs lose <hook-name> registration. Proceed? (y/N)'.

Behind the confirmation, the AUTOFIX writes the commit with subject `audit: dedup <hook-name> SessionStart hook (loom-managed; plugin + user-global handle registration)`.

### C. docs/reference/claude-code-hook-layering.md

New Diataxis reference page. Documents the additive-layering reality with:
- The four layers (plugin → user-global ~/.claude/settings.json → project-tracked .claude/settings.json → project-local .claude/settings.local.json)
- Verified-empirical anchor: 3 fires in a fresh e2e-api-tests session (2026-05-27) when project + user-global + plugin all registered bd prime
- Why empty-arrays-in-local-don't-cancel: layering is union, not override
- Pointer to /audit-project Item 12 + the two AUTOFIX paths above
- Pointer to docs/reference/loom-env-vars.md for the env-block sibling pattern (loom-7ro lineage)

Doubles as the discovery surface for future Claude Code hook-layering findings.

## Acceptance

- /audit-project Item 12 documents both AUTOFIX paths in agents/project-onboarder.md
- skills/audit-project/SKILL.md --apply-onboarding walk handles both tags
- skip-worktree AUTOFIX is the default offer; commit-removal is behind explicit confirmation
- docs/reference/claude-code-hook-layering.md registered in mkdocs.yml
- find-hook-dups.sh output remains the canonical detection mechanism (no script changes needed for this bead — already enumerates the tuples correctly)
- Tests under lib/tests/ cover the skip-worktree AUTOFIX (fixture: project with duplicated hook in tracked settings.json + plugin manifest → apply-onboarding produces the right git state + sidecar log)
- audit-apply-flags.test.sh AUTOFIX-inventory guards bumped to accommodate the two new tags

## Anti-scope

- Do NOT touch find-hook-dups.sh — detection works; only resolution is the gap.
- Do NOT add a third AUTOFIX path (e.g., 'modify the plugin's manifest') — out of loom's reach.
- Do NOT auto-commit the commit-removal AUTOFIX without explicit user y/N confirmation; the AUTOFIX inventory's binary 'apply' shape doesn't fit this case, so plumb a prompt through.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-27 by Frank during e2e-api-tests /audit-project session immediately after loom-7ro shipped. Empirically verified: fresh-session SessionStart fires 3 bd prime registrations from the three sources (plugin + user-global + project-tracked) regardless of settings.local.json overrides. KG triple from that session: `Claude Code settings hooks → merge_semantics_are → additive across all layers` (2026-05-27, e2e-api-tests/decisions wing).

## Sibling beads

- loom-7ro (closed) — env-block opt-out in project-level settings.json. Pure-additive shape works for ENV merge but not for HOOKS layering (loom-7ro's keys are scalars that overwrite; hooks are arrays that union).
- loom-1tq, loom-8hg, loom-a29 — audit-project + --apply-onboarding pattern (this bead extends it)
- loom-9pr — audit-for-hardcoded-loom/main references — orthogonal but the AUTOFIX inventory updates will touch the same test surfaces.

title: script/ convention: design + lock canonical set + workflow.json overlap
description:
## Goal

Lock the canonical script/ set for the loom-managed-repos convention (parent: loom-oxs). Decide which scripts are in the canonical set, what each is contractually required to do (exit conventions, no-op behavior for projects that don't need them), and how the convention interacts with the existing workflow.json `.deploy` field.

## Open design questions

1. **Canonical set.** Tentative list in loom-oxs (bootstrap, setup, update, server, test, lint, cibuild, deploy). Confirm or revise. Add/drop based on what loom-installed primitives actually consume + what 'predictability for a new agent walking in' demands.
2. **No-op behavior.** Should missing scripts be 'no-op exit 0' (script exists but does nothing) or 'absent entirely' (caller checks existence)? Tradeoff: the absent-entirely shape forces audit-project to surface gaps; the always-present-exit-0 shape lets callers blindly invoke.
3. **workflow.json overlap.** workflow.json today has `.deploy` (string command). Options:
   - Drop `.deploy` — `script/deploy` is the new convention; workflow.json keeps mode/v but not deploy.
   - Keep `.deploy` as an override — projects that want a one-off deploy command can stay declarative without a script file.
   - Have `.deploy` default to `script/deploy` — gives a uniform fallback.
4. **Project-type templates.** Should templates/scripts/ ship one canonical skeleton, or skeletons per project type (Go, Python, bash-only, Node)? Single-skeleton is simpler; per-type catches more out-of-box.
5. **Where the scripts live.** `script/` (GitHub blog convention, singular) vs `scripts/` (loom and many projects' current convention, plural). Loom-the-repo uses `scripts/`. Pick one and document.

## Deliverables

- A decision drawer in loom/decisions locking the canonical set + answers to the open questions.
- Update to loom-oxs description with the final list (replaces 'tentative').
- File T2-T6 children (templates skeleton, audit-project extension, /wrap-up migration, docs page, primitives audit) with concrete scope each, once the design is locked.

## Acceptance

- Locked canonical set published in a decision drawer.
- All five design questions answered with rationale.
- Children filed for the implementation work.

## Recipe

research-a-bead (design dialog with user; no code changes in this bead).

title: Standardize GitHub-style script/ convention for loom-managed repos
description:
## Why this exists

Loom-managed repos today have no project-agnostic entry points for common ops — every project ships its own ad-hoc `scripts/build`, `Makefile`, `./test.sh`, or nothing. Loom-installed skills/commands that need to 'run the tests' have to either hardcode a per-project command or punt the question to the user. The deploy-hint mechanism (workflow.json `.deploy`) is the smallest version of this; a full convention generalizes it.

Frank's framing 2026-05-27: the value isn't 'how many loom primitives consume the convention today' — it's that a human or agent walking into an unfamiliar loom-managed repo *knows what to expect*. Same logic as loom standardizing on Diataxis + GitHub Pages docs: adoption cost is real, but predictability for the outsider is the deliverable. Walk in → `script/test`, `script/setup`, `script/server` do what they say. No archaeology.

## Canonical set (locked at T1)

Tentative set, to be finalized at the design bead:
- `script/bootstrap` — one-time machine setup (install system deps, etc.)
- `script/setup` — get a fresh checkout to a runnable state (deps + initial migrate / build)
- `script/update` — pull + re-setup (after a git pull / branch switch)
- `script/server` — start the dev server / run the app locally (or no-op for libs)
- `script/test` — run the project's tests; exit 0 = green, non-zero = red
- `script/lint` — run linters; same exit convention
- `script/cibuild` — what CI runs (often = lint + test, but project's call)
- `script/deploy` — deploy step if any; otherwise no-op

(Modeled on GitHub's 'Scripts To Rule Them All' from the explain-yourself blog post; loom adds `lint` and `deploy` for its own purposes.)

## Children (filed after T1 design locks)

- T1: design + lock canonical set + decide workflow.json overlap (the .deploy field is the existing minimal version)
- T2: templates/scripts/ skeleton (mirrors templates/diataxis/ — placeholder scripts with comments explaining each, project-type-aware where possible)
- T3: /audit-project — extend project-onboarder subagent + audit-project skill to check for the convention + offer scaffolding from templates/
- T4: /wrap-up step 2 — migrate from hardcoded `python3 -m pytest -q` example to `script/test` (with fallback if absent)
- T5: docs/reference/script-convention.md (Diataxis reference page)
- T6: any other loom-installed primitives that today hardcode test/lint/build commands migrate (audit pass surfaces these — see also loom-9pr)

## Acceptance

- Canonical set documented in loom/decisions drawer + reference docs page.
- templates/scripts/ skeleton exists; /audit-project can scaffold into a fresh repo.
- /wrap-up step 2 uses script/test when present; falls back to current behavior otherwise.
- A new loom-managed repo can adopt the convention in one /audit-project pass.

## Provenance

Conversation between Frank and Claude 2026-05-27 during loom-6p6 wrap-up. Frank quote: 'it ends up being like loom standardizing on diataxis and publishing it via github pages, if you walk into a loom managed repo, you know what to expect.'

## Related

- loom-9pr (P3 audit for hardcoded loom-/main references) — orthogonal but adjacent; audit-project will likely surface call-sites that both this epic AND loom-9pr need to touch.
- Diataxis docs convention + docs-scaffold skill (loom-d50 lineage) — same shape of standardization.

title: /wrap-up: mechanical discovery of merged-but-open beads in step 1
description:
## Why this exists

`commands/wrap-up.md` step 1 asks the user to enumerate which bead(s) are wrapping up. That puts the user's memory in the critical path: if a session merged 5 beads in parallel-dispatch and the user invokes a bare `/wrap-up`, the command has nothing to bind to. Surfaced 2026-05-27 when loom-7p6.2-.6 were merged to main 2026-05-26 but never closed — the next session caught it via state reconciliation, not via the wrap-up flow.

## What this delivers

Enhance `/wrap-up` step 1 with a discovery sub-step BEFORE the user-enumerate prompt:

1. Grep `git log main` for `Merge loom-XXX:` patterns (and bare `loom-XXX:` subject lines for fast-forward / squash merges) since the last bd close timestamp (or a sensible default like 'since 1 week ago').
2. Extract bead IDs (regex `loom-[a-z0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*`).
3. For each, call `bd show <id>` and check status; collect any that are still open or in_progress.
4. If non-empty, present as the default close-set: 'Found N beads merged-to-main but still open: ... Close all?' — user can accept, edit, or override.
5. If user enumerates manually anyway, fall through to current behavior.

## Acceptance

- A bare `/wrap-up` after a session that merged N beads surfaces all N as the proposed close-set.
- User still confirms (no auto-close).
