- No regression for the single-bead case (the discovery just returns one ID, prompts as before).
- Idempotent: re-running /wrap-up after a successful close finds zero merged-but-open beads.

## Files

- commands/wrap-up.md (primary)
- possibly a small helper script under scripts/ if the grep+cross-check logic is non-trivial enough to extract

## Provenance

Conversation between Frank and Claude 2026-05-27 during loom-7p6 state-reconciliation session. See diary entry diary_wing_claude-opus_20260526_220031711485_4ff3fed1e79b.
close_reason: /wrap-up step 1 now mechanically discovers merged-but-open beads BEFORE asking the user. Shipped in merge ad-hoc (commit 1d5b24d after merge of frank/loom-6p6 + zsh-portability polish). Prefix-agnostic (project-symlinked), inline bash bounded by # DISCOVERY:START/:END markers, 15-case fixture test including zsh portability regression. Dogfooded self-referentially: the new mechanism correctly identifies loom-6p6 itself as merged-but-open. Follow-up loom-9pr tracks audit for other hardcoded 'loom-' / 'main' references in installed primitives.

title: wrap-up section 6 hard-codes loom-only install.sh; should hint at project's own deploy via workflow.json
description:
## Symptom

`/wrap-up` invoked in non-loom projects has been telling users to run loom's `./install.sh` as part of the close ritual. install.sh is loom-specific (symlinks loom files into ~/.claude/); running it from another project is nonsensical.

## Root cause

`commands/wrap-up.md` is symlinked into every project's `~/.claude/commands/`. Section 6 of that file ("(loom only) Re-deploy via install.sh") guards loom-detection with two conditions (lines 75–78):

- `pwd` repo root contains `install.sh` AND `settings.snippet.json`
- `install.sh` header first line is `#!/usr/bin/env bash` AND contains literal `loom install.sh` in its comment

The literal IS still present in loom's install.sh:2 — but the guard is convention-fragile. Claude reading wrap-up.md in a non-loom project often elides the literal-check ("install.sh exists, looks like a deploy script → run it"), surfacing loom-specific guidance to the wrong audience. `install.sh` is a common filename in unrelated projects.

Deeper problem: project-specific deploy guidance is hard-coded into a globally-shared command file. Detection-by-literal-match is brittle.

## Design (decided 2026-05-26 with frank)

**Mechanism:** Add `.deploy` field (string command, optional) to `<project>/.claude/workflow.json` schema. Document in `lib/workflow-config.sh` header alongside `.v`, `.mode`, `.guest`. wrap-up reads it via a new `workflow_resolve_deploy` resolver; if absent or empty → section 6 silently skips.

**Behavior:** Surface only. wrap-up prints `Next step (project deploy): <command>` after section 5 and stops. User decides when to run. No auto-exec.

**Loom's own config:** Set `.deploy: "./install.sh"` in loom's `.claude/workflow.json`. Loom user reading wrap-up output sees the hint and runs install.sh themselves (one keystroke vs current auto-run).

## Scope

In-scope:
- (1) `lib/workflow-config.sh`: add `.deploy` to the documented schema; add `workflow_resolve_deploy` reader (prints command string or empty).
- (2) `commands/wrap-up.md`: replace section 6 entirely. New section: "Surface project deploy hint (if configured)". No loom-specific detection.
- (3) `.claude/workflow.json` (loom's own): set `.deploy: "./install.sh"`.
- (4) `lib/tests/`: add `workflow-config-deploy.test.sh` covering: absent field → empty output, present field → string returned, malformed workflow.json → empty (graceful).

Follow-up beads (not in-scope for this bead):
- (5) `/audit-project` integration: detect projects with no `.deploy` set, prompt user to set or explicitly skip.
- (6) `docs/reference/workflow-json.md`: document the schema if not already documented.

## Acceptance

- `/wrap-up` in loom prints `Next step (project deploy): ./install.sh` and stops; does not auto-run.
- `/wrap-up` in a project without `.deploy` set skips section 6 silently.
- `workflow-config-deploy.test.sh` passes (3 cases: absent, present, malformed).
- No remaining mention of `install.sh` literal-detection in `commands/wrap-up.md`.
- Manual smoke: `/wrap-up` in a non-loom project (e.g., HAW or any other repo with no `.deploy` set) does NOT mention install.sh or loom.

## Cross-references

- Sibling/companion to loom-cuk (filed earlier 2026-05-26): both are about loom-isms leaking into / surviving past their proper scope. loom-cuk is install-time (worktree-path baked into symlinks); this bead is wrap-up-time (loom guidance leaking into other projects' wrap-up).
- workflow.json schema lives in `lib/workflow-config.sh`; existing test coverage in `lib/tests/workflow-config-guest.test.sh`.
close_reason: surface project deploy via workflow.json .deploy field (drops loom-only install.sh literal-match auto-run). Worktree commit 7763c28, merged --no-ff into main. workflow_resolve_deploy + scripts/loom-print-deploy-hint + section 6 rewrite + loom's own workflow.json. 11/11 deploy tests, 21/21 guest sibling no-regression. See drawer drawer_loom_decisions_9fb2868e288751d22c6dd7ec.

title: install.sh from a worktree bakes worktree-path symlinks; worktree cleanup breaks all loom primitives
description:
## Failure mode

`install.sh:17` resolves `LOOM_ROOT` via `cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd`. When install.sh is invoked from inside a worktree path like `.worktrees/loom-yjo/install.sh`, every symlink it creates in `~/.claude/{hooks,agents,commands,lib,scripts}/` bakes that worktree path as the target. The symlinks survive past worktree cleanup → become dangling en masse → every loom primitive silently breaks at once.

## Observed

Session start on 2026-05-26 after the loom-yjo worktree was removed post-merge (commits 6c7fc3d + d4ab97e). Two SessionStart:clear hooks surfaced as visible errors:

- `~/.claude/hooks/workflow-mode-onboarding.sh` → dangling
- `~/.claude/hooks/bd-prime-wrapper.sh` → dangling

A `find ~/.claude -maxdepth 4 -type l ! -exec test -e {} \; -print` showed **95 dangling symlinks** total: hooks (12), agents (4), lib (9), commands (9), scripts (5), plus per-skill/lib/test files.

## Immediate fix applied 2026-05-26

Re-ran `bash /home/frank/repos/loom/install.sh` from the main checkout. install.sh's "Pruning dangling loom-owned symlinks" step (line 98) plus re-linking from $LOOM_ROOT healed all 95 in one pass. Verified 0 dangling remain; both SessionStart hooks smoke-tested exit=0.

## Prevention options

1. **Refuse from worktree.** install.sh detects `git rev-parse --git-dir` returning `.git/worktrees/...` (or `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` not matching the main worktree) and refuses to run, instructing the user to invoke from the main checkout. Simplest, no behavior surprises.

2. **Resolve to main worktree.** install.sh uses `git worktree list --porcelain | awk '/^worktree/ && !seen{print $2; seen=1}'` to find the main worktree, and re-derives `LOOM_ROOT` from there even when invoked from a worktree path. Most ergonomic but couples install.sh to git internals.

3. **Doctor check.** Add a `bd doctor`-adjacent or stand-alone check (`scripts/loom-doctor` or a hook) that scans `find ~/.claude -maxdepth 4 -type l ! -exec test -e {} \;` and flags any dangling symlinks. Complementary to (1) or (2); catches the failure even if it slips through prevention.

Recommended scope: (1) + (3). (1) prevents the root cause cheaply; (3) catches any pre-existing damage and any future variant.

## Acceptance

- install.sh refuses (with a clear error) when invoked from a linked worktree path, OR resolves LOOM_ROOT through the main worktree
- A test under `lib/tests/` exercises the refusal/resolution path
- A doctor-style check exists that reports dangling `~/.claude/` symlinks
- A regression smoke battery (worktree → run install → cleanup worktree → check ~/.claude) is captured somewhere durable
close_reason: install.sh refuse-from-worktree (M4 prevention) + scripts/loom-doctor (M5 dangling-symlink surface) shipped; bug-family lineage to loom-x4m + loom-4um confirmed; reused TOPLEVEL==dirname(COMMON_DIR) idiom from loom-rebase-worktree/loom-worktree-python convention; 17 new tests GREEN; live doctor reports clean; install.sh dry-run from main passes through cleanly; mkdocs --strict clean.

title: bd-state rebase-replay drift: git pull --rebase=merges lands stale issues.jsonl in HEAD (loom-4um follow-up)
description:
Loom-4um (2026-05-15) shipped the .gitattributes merge=bd-export driver to prevent line-based three-way merge from silently reverting closed beads to in_progress. That structural fix covers MERGE but NOT REBASE-REPLAY.

SYMPTOM:
After bd close + git commit + git pull --rebase=merges + git push, the working tree shows .beads/issues.jsonl modified again. Diff shows recently-closed beads reverted to in_progress state in HEAD. Dolt (authoritative) has them correctly CLOSED. Working tree's bd export (regenerated from dolt) is correct.

REPRO (canonical):
1. bd close <id>  (auto-export regenerates issues.jsonl with status:closed)
2. git add .beads/issues.jsonl && git commit
3. git pull --rebase=merges  (rebase replays merges; merge driver fires again during replay)
4. git status shows .beads/issues.jsonl modified
5. git diff shows status reverted to in_progress in HEAD; working tree (re-exported from dolt) has closed

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
git's rebase machinery replays the merge driver against the ORIGINAL pre-merge tree state, not the post-bd-close state. The driver runs bd export, but the dolt state inside that replay context may be the pre-close snapshot. Need to verify by reading scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh + adding diagnostic logging.

HITS OBSERVED (2 in 2 consecutive days):
- 2026-05-25 -> commit db8fc74 'bd: post-close issues.jsonl re-export for loom-x2q' (yesterday)
- 2026-05-26 -> commit c8ebc6b 'bd: post-rebase issues.jsonl re-export (loom-ad1 + loom-tww closed)' (this session)

Both hits resolved by post-rebase re-export commit (working tree's bd export is authoritative; just commit it).

CURRENT WORKAROUND (works but noisy):
After git pull --rebase=merges, if git status shows .beads/issues.jsonl modified:
  git add .beads/issues.jsonl
  git commit -m 'bd: post-rebase issues.jsonl re-export'
  git push

CANDIDATE STRUCTURAL FIX (next session investigates):
Install a 'post-rewrite' git hook that runs 'bd export' after rebase completes. Hook fires on rebase + commit --amend; resyncs issues.jsonl from dolt unconditionally. Composes with the existing merge-driver fix.

Hook file would go in hooks/post-rewrite.sh, wired via install.sh into .git/hooks/post-rewrite (similar to how install.sh wires hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh).

LINEAGE:
- Parent: loom-4um (2026-05-15) — original three-way-merge structural fix via .gitattributes + merge driver
- This is a follow-up for the same bug class (bd-state silent drift) but a DIFFERENT git operation (rebase vs merge)
- Related: pull.rebase=merges is a loom convention (see CLAUDE.md 'Session Completion' section) — preserves --no-ff bead-merge bubbles. Switching to plain merge-pull is NOT a fix; it loses bubble preservation.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Diagnostic logging added to scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh confirms which code path fires during rebase replay vs initial merge.
2. Structural fix shipped (likely hooks/post-rewrite.sh + install.sh wiring + regression test).
3. lib/tests/ test fixture reproduces the rebase-replay scenario and asserts post-rewrite hook restores correct state.
4. CLAUDE.md 'Session Completion' section updated: the post-rebase re-export workaround line gets struck through or removed.

NOTES:
- N=2 in 2 days qualified this for P2 not P3.
- Plain git merge (no rebase) does NOT exhibit this — only --rebase=merges. Suggests the bug is in the replay path, not the merge-driver itself.
- The drift is COSMETIC: dolt is always correct, so 'git push' after the workaround commit yields the right state on origin. Risk is human confusion + commit noise, not data loss.
close_reason: Shipped hooks/post-rewrite.sh + install.sh wiring + lib/tests/bd-post-rewrite.test.sh (25 fixture cases including end-to-end real git rebase). Composes orthogonally with loom-4um bd-merge-driver (merge) and loom-x4m bd-worktree-preseed (fresh worktree). Hook re-exports from dolt after rebase/amend and auto-commits the delta if jsonl diverges from HEAD; degrades to no-op when bd unavailable, detached HEAD, staged changes, or .beads absent. Env bypasses: LOOM_BD_POST_REWRITE_SKIP=1, LOOM_BD_POST_REWRITE_NO_COMMIT=1. Full loom suite 40/41 (1 pre-existing loom-n1q FAIL). Merged via 58cb2e6 (bd-claim) + merge bubble. Acceptance criterion 1 (diagnostic logging in bd-merge-driver) deferred to closing drawer — structural fix is symptom-targeted.

title: docs-scaffold: DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME sentinel convention for unreplaced placeholders (loom-vca gap 3)
description:
Closes the third + final gap under loom-vca umbrella. mforth (downstream loom-managed project) shipped templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/install.md's placeholder prose verbatim because the scaffold provides no signal that the file is unreplaced. Fix: introduce a DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME broken-link sentinel that mkdocs build --strict catches via its built-in link checker (zero new infrastructure). See design drawer drawer_loom_decisions_56bfe66ecf7d9db3a3c555b0 for the locked design.

INVENTORY: 4 sentinels across 3 template files:
- templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/install.md.template (×2: install-cmd, install-verify)
- templates/diataxis/docs/tutorials/getting-started.md.template (×1: quickstart-invocation)
- templates/diataxis/docs/explanation/mental-model.md.template (×1: mental-model, page-level)

SENTINEL SHAPE:
[!! DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME: replace this section before publish !!](docs-scaffold-fixme-<token>)

Own line, directly above each placeholder block. Per-token diagnostic targets so mkdocs error names the specific instance.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Four sentinel lines added per inventory above.
2. lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh extended with RED + GREEN assertions: scaffold without replacement -> mkdocs build --strict fails AND each of the 4 tokens appears in stderr; sed-replace all four -> mkdocs build --strict passes.
3. New docs/reference/docs-scaffold-fixme.md reference page (~100 lines): what the sentinel is, why hard-fail, how to replace, inventory of tokens, grep self-audit instructions.
4. /docs-scaffold command's M6 summary mentions the sentinels + the grep -r DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME docs/ self-audit.
5. README.docs-pointer.md.template gets one sentence pointing at the grep self-audit.
6. mkdocs.yml.template gets a one-line comment referencing the convention.

OUT OF SCOPE (YAGNI):
- Dedicated /docs-doctor slash command. Bare grep is sufficient.
- mkdocs Python plugin / hooks. Broken-link weaponization gives the same forcing function with zero new infra.

LINEAGE:
- Parent: loom-vca umbrella (this is gap 3; on merge umbrella auto-closes).
- Wave 1 ship drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_682c0af9c299538551e0b026 (loom-ad1 + loom-tww, 2026-05-26).
- Design lock drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_56bfe66ecf7d9db3a3c555b0 (2026-05-26).
- mforth downstream ref (the EDIT this MECHANISM prevents recurrence of): commit 3cef9df.

EXECUTION:
Activity recipe feature-a-bead. Worktree on frank/<this-bead-id>. TDD: write RED test first (assert mkdocs --strict fails + names all 4 tokens), then add sentinels (going GREEN), then GREEN regression assertion.

PARKED for fresh-session execution per /wrap-up brainstorm 2026-05-26.
close_reason: Shipped DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME sentinel convention (4 sentinels across 3 templates) + lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh RED+GREEN extension (24/0 post-impl) + docs/reference/docs-scaffold-fixme.md reference page + skill/template/mkdocs.yml.template surfaces. Merged at this commit. Closes loom-vca umbrella gap 3.

title: edit-after-failure-guard: false positives on git merge CONFLICT text + bypass env var can't propagate from tool calls
description:
The hook trips on benign signals that don't represent test/build failures, blocking edits the agent legitimately needs to make.

REPRODUCTION (observed in mforth session 2026-05-24, 3/3 docs subagents + main orchestrator hit it)

Symptom 1 — git merge conflict output as 'failure':
A parallel-dispatch wave's serial-merge step runs:
    git merge --no-ff <branch>
which on the second + later merges produces:
    Auto-merging .beads/issues.jsonl
    CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .beads/issues.jsonl
    Auto-merging mkdocs.yml
    CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in mkdocs.yml
    Automatic merge failed; fix conflicts and then commit the result.
plus the Bash tool wrapping it with 'Exit code 1'.

The hook then refuses the very next Edit/Write — which is the conflict-resolution edit the workflow REQUIRES. The agent is being blocked from doing the thing the previous command literally instructed it to do.

Likely FAIL_RE match (hook's pattern after loom-7j5 hardening):
    r'\bexit code:\s*[1-9]'
matches against the Bash tool's 'Exit code 1' framing of the merge-with-conflicts exit, OR
    one of the failed-test patterns is matching 'merge failed' or similar.

Symptom 2 — Python traceback from benign exploration:
A subagent ran a one-shot 'python3 -c …' for a docs spot-check, forgot PYTHONPATH=src, got an ImportError traceback. The traceback was followed immediately by the successful spot-check (with PYTHONPATH set), and dozens of passing operations after that. Hook stayed in 'failure observed' state for the rest of the session.

The pattern r'\bTraceback \(most recent call last\)' fires on ANY traceback, including ones the agent recovered from in the next command. There's no notion of 'recovered' or 'TTL' on the failure signal.

Symptom 3 — bypass env var doesn't propagate:
LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP=1 only works if set in the harness's process environment. Setting it via a Bash tool call (export VAR=1) doesn't affect the hook's env because the hook is spawned by the harness, not by the agent's shell.

This means the documented bypass is effectively unavailable to agents at runtime — only available to the human running 'claude code' if they remembered to export it at launch.

IMPACT
- Forced workarounds: agents route Write/Edit through Bash heredocs (cat > file <<EOF) and Python string-replace one-liners (python3 -c). Works, but introduces noise and bypasses the Edit tool's diff-display.
- In a parallel-dispatch wave, the orchestrator hits this on every merge after the first (the merge conflict signal latches).
- Doc-shape beads spend disproportionate time fighting the hook for what should be one-line nav edits.

SUGGESTED FIXES (in increasing scope)

1. Tighten FAIL_RE to NOT match git-merge CONFLICT/conflict-resolution text. Specifically, exclude:
   - 'Auto-merging ' or 'CONFLICT (content):' framing → these are merge-state indicators, not failure markers
   - The 'Exit code 1' framing when the preceding stdout mentions 'CONFLICT' — git uses exit 1 to signal 'conflicts present, fix and commit', which IS the resolution opportunity

2. Add a TTL or 'recovered' notion: if the last Bash result was successful AND it touched files mentioned in the prior failure trace, treat the failure as recovered. Or: only count failures from the last 1-2 Bash calls, not arbitrary tail history.

3. Surface the bypass to agents at runtime. Options:
   - Read bypass from a per-project file (e.g., .loom/no-edit-after-failure-guard) that agents CAN create
   - Read bypass from a header in the tool_input ({"file_path": ..., "_loom_skip_guard": true}) — agents can include it when intentionally bypassing
   - Read bypass from a sentinel in the file path or commit message

ACCEPTANCE
- Repro 1 (parallel-dispatch wave with 3+ merges) no longer trips the hook on the merge-resolution edits.
- Repro 2 (single Python traceback followed by recovery) no longer latches for the rest of the session.
- Either bypass is reachable from agent tool calls, OR (preferred) the false-positive patterns above are tightened so bypass isn't needed for these cases.
notes:
Surfaced empirically during the mforth docs gap survey session 2026-05-24.

Cross-references in the mforth wing (mempalace_search 'mforth docs survey' OR 'parallel-dispatch'):
- drawer_mforth_decisions_3ebc7434c1efc22b93b428a1 — how-to wave ship report, includes 'edit-after-failure-guard friction' section with the full reproduction context and the suggested fix shape
- drawer_mforth_decisions_3b6d475201c08f5fbd03f0b7 — reference wave ship report (first wave that hit symptom 1)
- KG triple: 'loom edit-after-failure-guard hook → false_positive_triggers → git merge CONFLICT text + Python tracebacks from benign spot-checks; bypass env var doesn't propagate from tool calls'

Successor to loom-7j5 (which hardened FAIL_RE the first time after a similar prose-false-positive incident).

Hook source: /home/frank/repos/loom/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh (FAIL_RE is at lines ~115-127)

Test scaffolding already exists: /home/frank/repos/loom/lib/tests/edit-after-failure-guard.test.sh — add regression cases for both symptoms here.
close_reason: edit-after-failure-guard false-positive trifecta + hermetic tests shipped. CONFLICT whitelist (Auto-merging/Automatic merge failed → clear latch), last-Bash-only TTL (clean Bash clears prior failure), per-project .claude/no-edit-after-failure-guard marker bypass, test fixture unsets LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP at startup. Worktree commit a40aaf8, merged --no-ff into main. 45/45 fixture tests + full loom suite green + mkdocs --strict clean + e2e smoke confirmed. Successor of loom-7j5. See drawer drawer_loom_decisions_39d19d547859e7fc72586d4c.

title: UMBRELLA: docs-scaffold template polish post-real-publish-usage (mforth surfaced 3 gaps)
description:
2026-05-24 — mforth is the first real-world consumer-facing-publish stress test of /docs-scaffold. Three gaps surfaced same day, all sharing the root pattern: 'template ships content/config sized for a contributor audience or as a placeholder, which doesn't serve well as published end-user surface and provides no build-time signal that it should be customized.'

THREE MANIFESTATIONS:

1. **loom-ad1** (P2 bug) — templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template missing pymdownx.emoji config. Causes :material-* and :octicons-* icon shortcodes (used by the template's own docs/index.md grid-cards landing) to render as literal text. RENDERING REGRESSION. Downstream mforth fix: commit 1ab928c.

2. **loom-tww** (P2 bug) — templates/diataxis/docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md ship contributor-facing '## Discipline' h2 sections (~14 lines each, ~60 total) enumerating Diataxis discipline rules. Reads weirdly to end-users landing on published quadrant indexes. AESTHETIC/AUDIENCE REGRESSION. Downstream mforth fix: commit 419554b.

3. **(this umbrella) — templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/install.md** ships explicit placeholder text 'Replace this step with the project's actual installation command' with a Go/Python/Node/Bash menu of common shapes. Was never replaced for mforth → published site shipped the placeholder verbatim. The placeholder is honest (says 'replace this') but the scaffold provides no signal that the file is unreplaced — no build error, no warning, no FIXME marker. UNREPLACED-PLACEHOLDER REGRESSION. Downstream mforth fix: commit 3cef9df.

UNDERLYING ISSUE:
The scaffold treats the template as a starting point the consumer will hand-edit. That's the right intent. But:
- (a) Some content is wrong for ANY consumer (gaps 1+2) — no consumer-edit will fix mkdocs.yml missing pymdownx.emoji + ALL consumers should drop the Discipline meta. These should be FIXED in the template itself.
- (b) Some content IS rightfully a placeholder (gap 3 — install commands are genuinely project-specific) — but the scaffold provides NO MECHANISM that ensures the consumer notices + replaces. No build-time check, no scaffold-output reminder list, no FIXME-style sentinel that lints catch.

RECOMMENDED FIXES (multi-bead — this umbrella tracks the family):
- For (a): direct template fixes per loom-ad1 + loom-tww.
- For (b): introduce a MARKER convention in template placeholders. Either:
  * Literal sentinel like '!!UNREPLACED_PLACEHOLDER!!' that mkdocs build --strict treats as an error
  * Or a comment like '<!-- DOCS-SCAFFOLD-FIXME: replace this section before publish -->' that the scaffold appends + a /docs-doctor command (new) scans for and warns about
  * Or augment the scaffold's M6 'Next steps' summary to enumerate the placeholder files that need user-editing before publish (templates that contain 'Replace this step' or similar markers → list explicitly)

PAIRS WITH loom-ad1 + loom-tww (children of this umbrella). Future gaps in the same family should attach here.

Future-USR's downstream FYI: until this umbrella is addressed upstream, future loom-managed projects using /docs-scaffold will need to manually audit their docs/ post-scaffold for the same three issues. mforth's 3 commits (1ab928c, 419554b, 3cef9df) are the canonical reference for the downstream fixes.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: docs-scaffold quadrant indexes ship contributor-facing Discipline sections to end-user docs sites
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by mforth (downstream loom-managed project) — USR noticed the published Material site at https://fkberthold.github.io/mforth/tutorials/ has a 'Discipline' section between the intro paragraph and the 'Start here' content list. The Discipline section enumerates the Diataxis rules for what content belongs in that quadrant ('A tutorial earns its place when it names the defined starting point...'). Reads weirdly for users; reads correctly for docs contributors.

The pattern is consistent across ALL FOUR quadrant indexes:
* templates/diataxis/docs/tutorials/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~14 lines
* templates/diataxis/docs/how-to/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~16 lines
* templates/diataxis/docs/reference/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~14 lines
* templates/diataxis/docs/explanation/index.md — '## Discipline' section ~15 lines

Total: ~60 lines of contributor-facing meta-content shipped to every project that runs /docs-scaffold + publishes their site.

This is different from loom-ad1 (missing pymdownx.emoji): loom-ad1 is a rendering regression (icons leak as literal text), this is content-shape (the content renders fine but is wrong for the audience).

ROOT THINKING: the loom-template documents Diataxis to docs writers learning the discipline. That's correct for loom-internal/loom-template-consumer onboarding, WRONG to ship into the consumer project's published site verbatim. Each quadrant's intro paragraph + 'What X is not' navigation blockquote already communicate the discipline implicitly (the prose voice + the cross-links). The explicit '## Discipline' h2 section is redundant for users + odd-looking.

FIX OPTIONS:

(a) Delete the '## Discipline' sections from all 4 templates. Cleanest. The intro paragraphs already orient users; cross-links to the other quadrants handle the 'what is not' framing.

(b) Move the Discipline content into a single 'docs/about-these-docs.md' page in the template, linked from a footer or sidebar. Preserves the discipline-as-prose for projects that want to dogfood Diataxis discipline as part of their published site (loom itself probably wants this).

(c) Wrap the Discipline sections in HTML comments OR gate them behind a scaffold flag (--include-discipline) defaulting OFF. Lets sophisticated projects re-enable them by editing the index.md after scaffold lands.

Recommend (b): move into a single about-these-docs.md page that loom-managed projects can choose to link or not. Preserves the dogfooded discipline content (which IS valuable as a reference for the project's own docs contributors) without polluting end-user landing pages. The scaffold could nav-link it under a 'Meta' or 'About' section (off by default in nav, present as a file).

DOWNSTREAM mforth FIX commit (reference): https://github.com/fkberthold/mforth/commit/419554b (took option a — simple deletion across all 4 indexes, 67 lines removed).

ACCEPTANCE:
* All four templates/diataxis/docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md no longer ship the '## Discipline' h2 section verbatim on the user-facing landing page.
* Decision recorded in a MemPalace drawer about whether to delete (a) or relocate (b) — capture upstream rationale.
* Re-running /docs-scaffold against a fresh project produces user-facing quadrant indexes by default.

PAIRS WITH loom-ad1 — both are 'template ships content/config sized for a contributor audience that doesn't serve well as published end-user content/config' regressions surfaced by the same downstream mforth publish flow. Worth filing a tiny umbrella for 'docs-scaffold template polish post-real-publish-usage' if more emerge.
close_reason: Removed contributor-facing ## Discipline h2 sections from all 4 templates/diataxis/docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md (option a). 67 lines removed, matches mforth downstream commit 419554b exactly. Test extended in lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh with 4 absence-of-Discipline assertions. 17/17 green including mkdocs build --strict integration. Merged 44cc689, worker commit f2251fb.

title: docs-scaffold mkdocs.yml.template missing pymdownx.emoji breaks Material grid-cards icons on landing page
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by mforth (downstream loom-managed project) using /docs-scaffold then seeing literal ':material-school:{ .lg .middle }' on the published Diataxis front page instead of rendered SVG icons.

ROOT CAUSE: templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template's markdown_extensions block includes:
  - attr_list (good — needed for the icon-attribute syntax)
  - admonition, def_list, footnotes, md_in_html, tables, toc
  - pymdownx.details, pymdownx.highlight, pymdownx.inlinehilite,
    pymdownx.snippets, pymdownx.superfences, pymdownx.tabbed
But NOT pymdownx.emoji. The template's docs/index.md.template uses Material's grid-cards UI pattern (the canonical Diataxis landing-page idiom):

  <div class="grid cards" markdown>
  -   :material-school:{ .lg .middle } **New here — teach me**
      [:octicons-arrow-right-24: Tutorials](tutorials/index.md)
  -   :material-tools:{ .lg .middle } **I'm trying to do a specific thing**
  ...

Without pymdownx.emoji + Material's icon-emoji-index, those :material-*: and :octicons-*: shortcodes leak through as literal text in the rendered HTML. Visible regression on every project that scaffolds + publishes.

FIX (3 lines in templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template, after pymdownx.details):

  - pymdownx.emoji:
      emoji_index: !!python/name:material.extensions.emoji.twemoji
      emoji_generator: !!python/name:material.extensions.emoji.to_svg

Note: 'material.extensions.emoji' is the namespace for mkdocs-material 9.x. Older 'materialx.emoji' is for pre-9. mkdocs-material 9 has been stable since 2023; pinning to material.extensions.emoji is correct for current usage.

DOWNSTREAM mforth FIX commit (reference): https://github.com/fkberthold/mforth/commit/1ab928c

ACCEPTANCE:
* templates/diataxis/mkdocs.yml.template gains the pymdownx.emoji block in markdown_extensions
* templates/diataxis/docs/index.md.template's icon shortcodes render as SVGs (not raw text) in mkdocs build output
* Re-running /docs-scaffold against a fresh project produces a working grid-cards front page out of the box
* requirements.txt continues to pin mkdocs-material>=9.5,<10 (no version bump needed)

KG TRIPLE filed in mforth MemPalace: '/docs-scaffold mkdocs.yml.template → missing pymdownx.emoji extension config breaks → Material grid-cards icon shortcodes on the Diataxis landing page'.

This is a small template patch that should ship with no migration needed — adding the extension is purely additive.
close_reason: docs-scaffold mkdocs.yml.template gains pymdownx.emoji block (3 lines after pymdownx.details). New regression test lib/tests/diataxis-template-pymdownx-emoji.test.sh: 4/4 green. End-to-end verified: scaffolded tmp project, mkdocs build --strict rc=0, 22 inline SVGs rendered, 0 literal :material-school: text. Merged 95b3829, worker commit cda3730. Mirrors mforth downstream fix 1ab928c.

title: /audit-project: detect Python preflight + solo workspace, offer overrides
description:
On fresh non-Go non-team projects, `/audit-project` currently passes its checks
but leaves two defaults wrong for the project's shape:

1. bd's `preflight.template` ships with a Go checklist (`go test`, `go vet`, ...).
   Python, Rust, Node projects inherit it and see meaningless preflight output.
2. The CLAUDE.md "BEADS INTEGRATION" block generated by `bd init` hardcodes
   `bd dolt push` + `git push` lines that error/no-op on solo workspaces
   (no Dolt remote configured). loom-hsb shipped the guard pattern in loom's
   own CLAUDE.md, but new projects don't get it.

Surfaced by `/audit-project` against fresh ~/repos/mforth (Python, solo) on
2026-05-21 — both fell through the cracks since current checks only validate
loom-managed shape, not language or workspace topology.

Extends `/audit-project` to detect language (via canonical markers) and
workspace mode (via `bd dolt remote list`), surface a check row per the
existing audit-apply-flags pattern, and offer mechanical fixes for the
common cases. Greenfields-friendly: when no language markers exist yet,
prompts the user interactively (with a "skip" path that's remembered so
re-runs don't re-nag).
design:
## Detection logic (in agents/project-onboarder.md)

**detect_project_language() → python | go | rust | node | shell | unknown**
- python: pyproject.toml / setup.py / setup.cfg / requirements*.txt
- go: go.mod
- rust: Cargo.toml
- node: package.json (and NOT also pyproject.toml)
- shell: scripts/ + *.sh + bats fixtures, no other markers
- unknown: none of the above OR polyglot (tie-break: never guess)

**is_solo_workspace() → bool**
- TRUE if `bd dolt remote list --json` returns [] (no remotes)
- FALSE if any remote
- TRUE if command errors (degrade-safe; tolerate old bd / missing dolt)

## Check rules

`preflight-language-match`:
- PROMPT if language=unknown AND preflight.template is unset OR bd-default
  (user picks language; write override to .beads/config; PASS)
- WARN if language=python/rust/node AND preflight.template starts with "go"
- PASS otherwise (incl. polyglot — surface unknown, don't arbitrate)
- "skip" answer writes .claude/loom-audit-state.json memo → silent PASS on re-runs

`claude-md-solo-aware`:
- WARN if is_solo_workspace() AND CLAUDE.md BEADS INTEGRATION block has
  unguarded `bd dolt push` line (regex: not wrapped in loom-hsb-style
  `if bd dolt remote list ...; then ... fi` guard)
- PASS otherwise (incl. no BEADS INTEGRATION block at all)
- Fix mechanically rewrites the canonical block to use loom-hsb guard shape
- If block has been hand-edited beyond recognition, fix refuses with message;
  user gets pointer to loom CLAUDE.md as reference shape

## User-facing flow

Both checks render via existing PASS/WARN/PROMPT row pattern. WARNs offer
y/N/skip with diff preview before applying. Skip path writes state-file memo.

## State file

`.claude/loom-audit-state.json` (per-project, gitignored). Stores per-check
skip memos so re-runs respect "user said no". Not a config file — never
read by anything except `/audit-project`.

## Test mocking surface

New env var `LOOM_AUDIT_PROMPT_ANSWER` lets fixtures inject answers to the
interactive prompt. `BD_BIN` shim for canned `bd dolt remote list` JSON
already exists in lib/tests/.

## Scope guardrails

- Does NOT add new check categories — both fit under "workflow infrastructure"
- Does NOT change project-onboarder.md's read-only contract — prompts/writes
  happen in /audit-project (the interactive caller)
- Does NOT touch docs-substrate detection (different code path)
- Does NOT extend language enum beyond {python, go, rust, node, shell, unknown}
- Does NOT install hooks/watchers/session-startup integration
  (deferred-recheck option was explicitly rejected in favor of audit-time prompt)
acceptance_criteria:
- /audit-project detects project language via canonical markers (pyproject.toml,
  go.mod, Cargo.toml, package.json, shell-script fallback); polyglot returns
  "unknown" without guessing.
- When language=unknown AND preflight.template is unset/bd-default,
  /audit-project prompts the user interactively with {python, go, rust, node,
  shell, skip}; answer is applied to .beads/config; "skip" is memoized in
  .claude/loom-audit-state.json.
- When language is determinable AND preflight.template mismatches,
  /audit-project surfaces a WARN row with y/N/skip; "y" writes the override,
  "skip" memoizes silence.
- /audit-project detects solo-workspace via `bd dolt remote list --json` ==
  []; degrades safe on command error.
- When solo AND CLAUDE.md BEADS INTEGRATION block is unguarded,
  /audit-project surfaces a WARN with diff preview; "y" rewrites to
  loom-hsb-style guard; refuses gracefully on unrecognized hand-edits.
- Both checks are idempotent: re-running /audit-project after a successful
  fix returns PASS silently; re-running after "skip" returns PASS silently.
- lib/tests/audit-apply-flags.test.sh extended with at least 11 cases
  (6 for preflight-language-match, 5 for claude-md-solo-aware) covering
  PASS, WARN-without-fix, WARN-with-applied-fix, PROMPT-with-language-answer,
  PROMPT-with-skip-answer, polyglot tie-break, refuse-on-hand-edit, and
  state-file memo respect on re-run.
- All existing audit-apply-flags.test.sh cases continue to pass (no regressions).
notes:
Source: /audit-project run against fresh ~/repos/mforth (Python, solo) on 2026-05-21.
Brainstorm trajectory captured in diary entry diary_wing_claude-opus_20260521_085506766121_3f5dbae8c0d4.

Decisions during brainstorm:
- Triage: file only this loom bead (covers #2 + #3 from audit tail).
  Item #1 (`bd create --deps "blocked-by:X"` silent non-blocking edges in
  bd 1.0.2) is upstream-only — Frank will file as GitHub issue against beads
  project directly, no loom shadow bead.
- Splitting: one bead, not two. Both extensions share files
  (project-onboarder.md + audit-project skill + lib/tests/audit-apply-flags).
  Per loom CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic, shared files → one bead.
- Greenfields handling for #2: interactive prompt at audit time (vs deferred
  session-startup recheck). User is already interactive at audit time;
  cheapest UX.

Lineage:
- loom-c9z — /audit-project wing auto-detect (precedent for fresh-project shape detection)
- loom-hsb — solo-workspace bd dolt push guard (canonical shape this bead writes into CLAUDE.md)
- loom-7bn, loom-ki5 — sibling "upstream-PR" beads (NOT this pattern; this bead is a loom-side fix)
- lib/tests/audit-apply-flags.test.sh — file we're extending

Workflow mode: full (multi-file, new env var surface, new state-file).
Recipe: feature-a-bead.
close_reason: /audit-project gains items 13 (preflight-language-match) + 14 (claude-md-solo-aware). project-onboarder.md describes detection conceptually (read-only contract preserved); audit-project/SKILL.md owns prompt loop + y/N/skip gate + state-file r/w + verbatim loom-hsb guard. .claude/loom-audit-state.json gitignored. lib/tests/audit-apply-flags.test.sh 90→122 (+32). LOOM_AUDIT_PROMPT_ANSWER env var for fixtures. Merged via commit fae8a31.

title: [bug] edit-after-failure-guard: lax substring match trips on doc/drawer/source text containing 'fail'
description:
The edit-after-failure-guard PreToolUse hook (introduced by loom-z3m.6, shipped 2026-05-19) misfires on Edit/Write/MultiEdit when the recent transcript tail contains the substring 'fail'/'failed'/'failure'/'FAIL' inside ANY tool_result block — not just Bash output.

Surfaced 2026-05-19 by the e2e-api-tests Architecture.md rework session. 10 false-positive hits across one session, none of which were actual test/build failures:

- mempalace_get_drawer returning a brainstorm drawer that says 'Failure mode: arbiter down …'
- mempalace_search returning the prior session's diary entry that mentions the hook itself
- bd show on a bead whose description says 'Hard-failure detection (mark-failed, do not re-queue)'
- mempalace_search hits on Go source with log.Fatalf(... 'failed to connect ...')
- Read tool returning Architecture.md's own Mermaid state machine with 'TestGroupSetupFailed' / 'force-failed' identifiers
- A prior invocation of THIS hook reporting itself ('[edit-after-failure-guard] BLOCKED')
- The hook script's own source code read via cat to debug it

Root cause: the hook's docstring claims 'We only care about Bash tool results — test/build runners are invoked via Bash. Edit/Write tool results don't carry failure semantics relevant here.' The implementation, however, scans every tool_result regardless of which tool_use produced it. There is even a TODO inline acknowledging the gap: '# Conservative: any tool_result with failure markers counts. Tool-name disambiguation is best-effort below.' — the 'best-effort' is only on the Edit/Write tracker side, not on the failure-marker side.

Recoverable via LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP=1 — but the env var must be exported BEFORE claude is launched to propagate to the child process. In-session export does not take effect; the workaround in the e2e-api-tests session was exit-claude → export → relaunch (or python-via-Bash to bypass the Edit/Write surface entirely).
design:
Three fix candidates from the bug report (mutually compatible, can ship together):

1. **Source-discriminate tool_result blocks.** Pair each tool_result with its preceding tool_use via tool_use_id and only check Bash-produced results. This is the implementation that matches the existing docstring claim. Most surgical fix — drops false-positives from mempalace_*, bd_*, Read, etc.

2. **Tighten the failure-marker regex.** Replace the lax \bFAIL(?:ED|URE)?\b substring match with start-of-line / known-framing patterns: ^FAIL\b, ^panic:, '--- FAIL:', 'Go exit status [1-9]', 'pytest.*\d+ failed', 'npm ERR!'. Reduces false-positives even within Bash output (e.g., a curl response body containing 'failed' in prose).

3. **Whitelist hook self-references.** Skip transcript text that contains 'edit-after-failure-guard' — prevents the recursive-self-trigger sub-bug where the hook reports its own block, and the block report becomes a fresh failure marker for the next Edit/Write.

Suggested order: ship #1 first (biggest false-positive reduction with smallest blast radius), then #2 (defense in depth), then #3 (cheap insurance). Each ships with a fixture-based test under lib/tests/ covering at least one of the 10 documented false-positive surfaces.
notes:
Lineage:
- loom-z3m.6 (closed) — bead that introduced the hook
- Diary entry session-2026-05-19.e2e-api-tests.resource-locks-extraction-bead-admin-done-arch-rework-paused already lists this as candidate follow-up #3
- Diary entry session-2026-05-19.liza_base.u0pw-tit-centrality-voice-fix-prompt-patched also hit a sibling instance ('edit-after-failure-guard hook blocked Edit tool — recursive FAILURE word trigger same bug wdf8 worker hit')

The hook's docstring sets the right intent — Bash-only — the bug is that the implementation doesn't enforce it. The fix is to bring code into agreement with documented design, not to redesign.

Env-var bypass note for the bug report: in-session 'export LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP=1' does NOT propagate to a running claude — the parent process's env is frozen at fork time. The bypass requires exit-claude → export → relaunch, or use of a tool that doesn't go through the Edit/Write surface (Bash heredoc → python -c).

Where it lives: ~/.claude/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh → symlink into ~/repos/loom/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh
Closing drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_4a0a37a40e66ed2d4c219502 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-19). Findings drawer (pre-fix): drawer_loom_findings_da814ec42e13fc1c5db86902 (loom/findings, 2026-05-19; superseded by the closing drawer).
close_reason: Three-axis fix: tool_use_id source-discrimination + tighter framing regex + hook self-ref whitelist. 35/35 hook tests + 34/34 full suite. Commit 99e8ce0, merge c5c0e35. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<pending> to follow.

title: bd preflight: add mkdocs --strict check (loom-tx7 follow-up)
description:
Loom-59w drawer documented this as deferred option (b). Adds 'docs build --strict succeeds' to bd preflight so the check fires before close, not just at push time.

## What
Extend bd preflight (or wrap it with loom-side pre-close checks) to invoke lib/tests/docs-mkdocs-strict.test.sh when docs/ or mkdocs.yml or skills/ files are in the bead's diff. Should be skippable for non-docs beads (don't pay the mkdocs build cost when nothing relevant changed).

## Lineage
- loom-59w drawer (2026-05-15) — proposed (b) bd preflight extension, deferred
- loom-tx7 (2026-05-19) — 3rd recurrence triggered this bead
- lib/tests/docs-mkdocs-strict.test.sh — fixture to invoke

## Relationship to sibling follow-up
Composes with the pre-push hook follow-up (defense-in-depth: preflight catches at close, pre-push catches at push, CI catches on origin). Either alone narrows the gap; both together close it.
close_reason: Shipped hooks/bd-preflight-docs-strict.sh via commit 573be1b (merged da17527 on main). PreToolUse Bash hook gates bd close + bd preflight on mkdocs --strict when docs-relevant files in diff. Mode-aware (full=block, light=warn, off=silent), bypassable via LOOM_BD_PRECLOSE_STRICT_SKIP=1. 12/12 fixture tests pass, full lib/tests sweep 34/34 green, loom's own docs build strict-clean. Closes loom-tx7's deferred (b) option from loom-59w drawer. Composes with sibling loom-kbo (pre-push) for defense-in-depth: close → push → CI.

title: Pre-push hook: mkdocs --strict for staged docs/ changes (loom-tx7 follow-up)
description:
Third recurrence of the broken-doc-link bug class in 4 days (loom-59w 2026-05-15, loom-tx7 2026-05-19) justifies local pre-push enforcement. Loom-59w's close drawer documented this as deferred option (a).

## What
Add hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh that runs mkdocs build --strict when files under docs/, mkdocs.yml, or skills/ (any include-markdown source) are in the push range. Emit WARN (rc=0) not block (rc=2) by default — surfaces the failure without making every push interactive. Reuse lib/tests/docs-mkdocs-strict.test.sh as the test fixture pattern.

## Lineage
- loom-59w drawer (2026-05-15) — proposed (a) pre-push, deferred
- loom-tx7 (2026-05-19) — 3rd recurrence triggered this bead
- lib/tests/docs-mkdocs-strict.test.sh — regression test already exists, can be invoked from the hook
close_reason: hooks/pre-push-mkdocs-strict.sh shipped (WARN-only, rc=0). 9/9 fixture tests pass. install.sh wires symlink via git-common-dir so it works from worktrees. Env bypass LOOM_PRE_PUSH_MKDOCS_SKIP=1. Merged via commit 6f1fccc.

title: beadpowers:create-beads splitting-heuristic prompt at file time (upstream PR)
description: Companion to loom-z3m.5. The loom-z3m design point #4 prescribed adding a splitting-heuristic prompt to beadpowers:create-beads at bead-creation time. That skill lives in the beadpowers plugin cache, NOT in loom — loom does not own its source. The loom-side ritualization (seam-scan at claim) is shipped under loom-z3m.5; this bead tracks the upstream-PR piece. Two options: (a) file a PR against beadpowers to add the prompt at file time, or (b) document the gap as a known-limitation.
close_reason: Filed upstream as pprice/beadpowers#2 (issue #1); watch-bead loom-7nq spawned. upstream-a-bead --issue+pr lane.

title: Upstream PRs: superpowers verification-before-completion tool-inventory + systematic-debugging alt-hypothesis gate
description: Companions to loom-z3m.6. Cannot live in loom because superpowers is an external plugin. File upstream PRs against the superpowers repo for: (a) verification-before-completion tool-inventory pre-check step; (b) systematic-debugging '≥2 alternatives' diary-entry gate before fix proposal. Both surfaced by the loom-z3m retrospective dig.

title: docs-scaffold: fix GH Pages publish-source guidance to match gh-deploy workflow
description:
Root cause: skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md:269 tells the user to set GitHub Pages 'Source = GitHub Actions' — but the scaffolded .github/workflows/docs.yml uses 'mkdocs gh-deploy --force', which pushes to a gh-pages branch (requiring 'Source = Deploy from a branch: gh-pages').

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f5 (loom) — pre-cited as loom-no3 but never filed.
design:
Option A (recommended, smaller diff): fix line 269 of skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md to say 'Source = Deploy from a branch: gh-pages / (root)'. Add fixture in lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh asserting the SKILL.md line matches the workflow.yml shape.

Option B: switch the scaffolded workflow to actions/deploy-pages (which DOES use Source=GitHub Actions) and keep the existing SKILL.md text. Larger diff; would also require gh-pages-branch cleanup guidance for projects with existing branches.
notes:
[loom f5] sid=47c497a5 ts=2026-05-10T16:06:12Z user: 'As loom is designed right now, when the system makes docs for a repo, is it automatically making them in a way that gets published as a github page?'
Assistant after investigation: 'line 269 names wrong source for gh-deploy.'
user: 'Go ahead and file a bead for it.' -> loom-no3 (never filed)
close_reason: Regression assertions added to lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh (+4, 13/13 total) pinning skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md GH Pages source guidance to the gh-deploy workflow shape. SKILL.md text itself already correct from loom-no3 (dbb068b). Merged via commit fc5766d.

title: Cross-tracker dependency awareness: upstream:loom label + /check-loom-upstream sweep
description:
Root cause: project beads filed as workarounds for loom-side bugs have no auto-clearing signal when the loom fix lands. Stale P2 beads clutter bd ready in downstream projects; risk re-investigating already-solved problems.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f3 (HAW): bead 7iz filed for a loom-x4m fix scenario, stayed OPEN with no cross-link.
design: Three pieces: (1) label convention 'upstream:loom' (or 'loom-bug') for project beads that shadow loom-side fixes. (2) /check-loom-upstream slash command: scans the project's bd issues with label upstream:loom or description matching loom-hook/loom-script keywords, queries ~/repos/loom/.beads/issues.jsonl (or bd export) for recent closures referencing those keywords, surfaces 'these N project beads may be cleared by loom-XXX / loom-YYY' at cold-start. (3) audit-project auto-applies label when filing a new bead matching loom-hook/loom-script keywords.
notes:
[HAW f3] sid=9cbe9cc9 ts=2026-05-15T22:42:14Z user: 'I think 7iz wast a loom bug and is cleared. Let's start in with cy1.'
(Bead 7iz description: 'bd pre-commit hook drops spurious top-level issues.jsonl when run inside .worktrees/' — exact failure mode fixed by loom-x4m's bd-worktree-preseed hook, no cross-link.)
close_reason: P2 wave 3 — see drawer (forthcoming) for details. Merged via commits b0160f3 (vin) + 9bff3c4 (7p6.1) + 80180ae (z3m.11).

title: research-a-bead: explicit scope-decomposition step before first search wave
description:
Root cause: research-a-bead's M-step doesn't currently prompt for axis decomposition (static vs dynamic, snapshot vs trace, per-call vs per-session). Agent's first search wave skews to one axis; user re-steers to the missing axis mid-bead.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f6 (loom): loom-nsb research initially covered only static cost; user re-steered to dynamic/process cost.
design: Add a 'scope decomposition' step to skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md between 'define the question' and 'search prior art'. Step enumerates research axes explicitly: static-config vs dynamic-trace, observable-from-logs vs observable-from-settings, snapshot vs time-series, per-call vs per-session, scope-of-N vs scope-of-1. Each axis pair gets a one-line 'does this question apply to both sides?' check. Documented in skill body with a worked example (loom-nsb static+dynamic decomposition).
notes: [loom f6] sid=47c497a5 ts=2026-05-10T17:14:13Z user: 'So, theres the static cost, but Im thinking more in terms of the actual process, look at the logs of the subagents that are running, and what they are doing.'
close_reason: M1.5 scope-decomposition step added to skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md (120 words, between M1 and M2). Includes axis enumeration, one-line applicability prompts, loom-nsb worked example, stage-table row, Skip-M1.5 failure-mode entry. Merged via commit 34c8b60.

title: Bead-assumption-audit when systematic-debugging diagnosis diverges from bead description
description:
Root cause: bead descriptions baked at filing time become load-bearing-but-wrong when investigation surfaces a different root cause. Stale assumptions linger as unstated constraints, mislead a future session that lands on the bead via bd ready.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f4 (HAW): bead yho was filed as 'validator gap' but diagnosis pivoted to 'tighten observe-NPC prompt'; user agreed the bead's assumptions were off.
design: Add a 'bead-assumption-audit' step to bugfix-a-bead (and other activity recipes that invoke systematic-debugging) AFTER the root-cause hypothesis lands but BEFORE the RED test: if diagnosed cause materially differs from bead description, recipe mandates bd update <id> --description (or at minimum bd comment) capturing corrected framing. Creates paper trail; prevents stale assumptions misleading future sessions.
notes:
[HAW f4] sid=938eaf69 ts=2026-05-13T23:32:19Z user: 'I think that's a reasonable call, I agree that the bead's assumptions were off.'
(Bead yho — observe-Pooh phantom honey pot — filed as 'validator gap' but actual fix was 'tighten observe-NPC prompt'.)
close_reason: M2 bead-assumption-audit step added to bugfix-a-bead (sole systematic-debugging-invoking recipe). 79 words, between M1 (diagnosis) and RED test. bd update --description preferred + bd comment fallback + HAW yho citation + Skip-M2 failure-mode entry + stage table renumber (M3-M6). Doc-presence test lib/tests/bugfix-recipe-assumption-audit.test.sh (11/11). Merged via commit ca4ff86.

title: bd-close-capture hook: widen prefix regex to accept multi-hyphen prefixes
description:
Root cause: hooks/bd-close-capture.sh prefix regex is [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3,} — accepts underscore prefixes (liza_base) but rejects hyphen-separated prefixes (hundred-acre-woods). Blocked legitimate bd close calls in HAW, requiring --force 3x in one session.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f2 (loom) — pre-cited by worker as loom-2t7 but never filed.
design: One-line fix: widen prefix class to [a-z][a-z0-9_-]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}. Add bats fixture under lib/tests/bd-close-capture-prefix.test.sh covering: underscore-prefix (liza_base-xxx), hyphen-prefix (hundred-acre-woods-xxx), single-letter prefix (a-xxx), and legitimate-but-bad-shape cases the hook MUST still reject (no-suffix, suffix-too-short).
notes: [loom f2] sid=0f62de8a ts=2026-05-14T02:09:58Z user: 'A problem found: 1. Loom hook bug: bd-close-capture.sh regex [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3,} rejects HAW multi-hyphen prefix. Required --force bypass 3x this session. Fix: widen prefix class to [a-z][a-z0-9_-]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}.'
close_reason: Regression test fixture lib/tests/bd-close-capture-prefix.test.sh (5/5) pins the prefix-class widening already shipped under loom-2t7 (ddf99f9). Merged via commit cd04313.

title: Context-budget proactive wrap-up sensor (statusline + recipe nudge)
description:
Root cause: agent only initiates wrap-up after user notices context is heavy. No sensor reads usage telemetry and triggers checkpointing at threshold.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f5 (liza-base, 2 distinct sessions): user explicitly says 'we're too deep in context, let's wrap up' instead of agent proactively offering.
design: hooks/context-budget-sensor.sh as PostToolUse hook (or extend an existing one): reads usage.cache_read_input_tokens + message_count from latest transcript line, writes 'context_pressure: green|yellow|red' to workflow-state. Thresholds: >400k yellow, >700k red (tunable via env). Statusline shows 'CTX:Y' or 'CTX:R' when not green. Recipe-shells check the flag at each phase boundary and offer 'wrap-up checkpoint?' when red.
notes:
[liza f5] sid=a17aa8ae ts=2026-05-07T18:14:46Z user: 'We're too deep in on context. Let's get these logged as beads, get everything in mempalace etc, do a full wrap-up, then we'll start a fresh session.'
sid=6f066ea8 ts=2026-05-17T21:46:01Z user: 'Ok, we've used a lot of context, lets /wrap-up and then I'll clear, make sure we have all the context we'll need.'

title: Worktree + background-process orphan inventory + cleanup nudge
description:
Root cause: finishing-a-development-branch closes the bead and merges, but never enumerates live worktrees or background bash processes. Cleanup falls through unless user nags. Same gap surfaces after a session crash mid-dispatch.

Surfaced by:
- loom-z3m.1 f4 (loom): loom-path-leak comprehensive 5-mode writeup (Mode 1/2/4 mostly addressed by loom-ymc/loom-x4m/loom-rsk; orphan-inventory mode NOT yet covered)
- loom-z3m.1 f2 (liza-base): worktrees + bg scripts left running across sessions, user nagged 3x
design: hooks/worktree-bg-inventory.sh as Stop hook (or extend mempal-stop-hook.sh): runs git worktree list + jobs -l / ps --ppid + writes counts to workflow-state file. Statusline shows 'WT:N BG:M' so orphans are visible. New /cleanup-orphans slash command. finishing-a-development-branch nudges to /cleanup-orphans when N>0 or M>0. SessionStart check: worktrees for closed beads surface as cleanup targets in the startup banner.
notes:
[liza f2] sid=3fe1ed50 ts=2026-05-12T04:03:58Z user: 'Let's get those worktree's cleaned up now, don'l leave them sitting.'
sid=9d5afde1 ts=2026-05-16T03:57:01Z user: 'When things broke off, we had a number of subagetns running in worktrees, we're going to need to deal with all of them.'
sid=625092bf ts=2026-05-18T14:11:29Z user: 'You also have 2 scripts that seem to have been running for a while.'

title: T3: synthesize worker returns → bead pile + capture drawer + KG triples
description:
Goal: central agent (me) consumes 3 worker JSON returns, dedups across projects, surfaces ranked merged table to Frank for sanity-check, then files ~10–15 improvement beads under loom-z3m + capture drawer + KG triples.

Steps:
1. Merge top_5 ∪ runners_up across all 3 workers (~15–45 raw findings)
2. Group by root-cause semantic similarity (not lexical) — cross-project duplicates merge, recurrence count = weight
3. Rank merged groups by recurrence × impact × tractability
4. Dump merged ranked table to chat for Frank veto/redirect:
   | Rank | Root cause | Recurrence | Impact | Tractability | Proposed fix |
5. File surviving beads:
   - Each finding → one child of loom-z3m (NOT loom-z3m.1) so Phase 1 sub-epic stays closeable
   - Priority by rank: P1 for top 3, P2 for 4–10, P3 for 11–15
   - Body: title + root_cause + proposed_fix; --notes verbatim worker excerpt + session_id; --design the proposed loom intervention
   - bd dep add cross-links where fixes are prerequisites for each other
6. File loom/decisions capture drawer: worker meta-observations (verbatim, one per project) + merged ranked table + bead-ID map + cross-project patterns
7. File KG triples: <finding-bead> surfaced_by <worker>; <finding-bead> recurs_in <project>; pattern-level relationships
8. Cheap recursion: run audit-project skill against loom itself, check if any new bead-pile patterns overlap existing infrastructure gaps
9. Close loom-z3m.1; loom-z3m.2 (Phase 2 bead) becomes ready

Acceptance: bead pile filed, capture drawer in loom/decisions, KG triples filed, Frank's veto-pass applied.

Blocked by: loom-z3m.1 T2 (depends_on).
close_reason: 14 improvement beads filed (loom-z3m.4..17, P1×3 + P2×7 + P3×4) under loom-z3m. Capture drawer drawer_loom_decisions_82550a6ca95fc704672e6992. 5 KG triples. Frank's veto applied (proceed-all). Phase 1 complete.

title: T2: dispatch 3 parallel read-only retrospective workers
description:
Goal: dispatch worker-loom + worker-liza + worker-haw concurrently via 3 Agent({isolation: "worktree"}) calls in a single message per superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents + loom-7p6 discipline. Each worker reads its hit-list, classifies (SIGNAL/NOISE/UNCLEAR), ranks SIGNAL findings by impact × tractability, returns top-5 + runners-up + one-paragraph meta-observation as JSON.

Files: no new files. Brief template lives inline in the dispatch turn (per loom-7p6.1 template once landed).

Worker brief schema:
- finding_id, one-line title, signal_class (A|B), root_cause (1–3 sentences), evidence (verbatim 2–5 line excerpt), proposed_loom_fix (concrete file/skill/hook/recipe), impact (low/med/high), tractability (easy/med/hard)
- Returns JSON: { top_5: [...], runners_up: [...], meta: '...' }

Worker constraints:
- READ-ONLY: no edits, no bead/drawer/KG writes, no pushes
- Stay in hit-list (no skimming outside)
- Don't propose cross-project fixes (central agent's job)
- 45-min agent-work budget per worker; sample if hit-list huge
- Cite session_id + timestamp on every finding

Pre-flight: standard .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md smoke battery applies even for read-only work.

Steps:
1. Wait for T1 hit-lists to exist
2. Compose 3 dispatch messages with tailored brief per worker
3. Run all 3 Agent calls in a single response (parallel dispatch)
4. Collect JSON returns

Acceptance: 3 JSON returns received, schema-valid, evidence excerpts cite real session_ids.

Blocked by: loom-z3m.1 T1 (depends_on).
close_reason: 3 read-only retrospective workers dispatched in parallel, all returned schema-valid JSON. SIGNAL counts: loom 6/12, HAW 4/5, liza-base 20/25 sampled. Findings saved to ~/.loom-retro/<project>-findings.json. Cross-cutting themes visible: session-startup gaps, parallel-dispatch under-use, worktree-isolation rough edges, mid-flight verification reflexes. T3 unblocked for synthesis + bead filing.

title: T1: pre-scan tooling — grep transcripts + diaries, emit hit-list JSONL per project
description:
Goal: produce ~/.loom-retro/<project>-hits.jsonl for loom + liza-base + HAW. Each row: {session_id, ts, signal_class, evidence_offset_start, evidence_offset_end, raw_excerpt}.

Files: new script scripts/loom-retro-prescan (bash + jq), ~/.loom-retro/ output dir (gitignored).

Heuristics — Signal Class A (Frank corrections):
- Steering regex: \b(no|stop|wait|actually|don't|instead|but)\b in user turn ≤200 chars AND ≥3 preceding agent tool calls
- Diagnostic regex: 'you (should have|missed|didn't|forgot|misread)', 'why (did|didn't) you', 'that's (wrong|not right|backwards)', 'going down the wrong path', 'dead end' — no length filter
- Upgrade signal if next assistant turn contains 'you're right', 'apologies', 'let me redo', 'let me back up'

Heuristics — Signal Class B (dead ends):
- Long-run abandonment: ≥20 consecutive assistant turns followed by Frank turn with Jaccard distance >0.7 from last 5 agent turns
- Diary LESSON markers: grep ~/.mempalace/palace/...wing_claude-opus/.../diary for 'process-LESSON', 'process-WIN-LESSON', 'LESSON:'
- Explicit reversions: 'let me back up', 'restart this', 'wrong approach', 'let me try a different'

Steps:
1. Write fixture-based bash test that feeds 5 known struggle-moments + 5 known clean turns through prescan → expect correct classification
2. Implement script
3. Self-test on loom corpus (40MB, 19 sessions) — spot-check 5 random hits for false-positive rate
4. Tune until <40% FP rate
5. Run on HAW (92MB) + liza-base (1.2GB), confirm hit counts land in 50–200 range per project; adjust thresholds if outside

Acceptance: 3 hit-list files exist, hit counts in target range, FP rate <40% on loom spot-check.

Blocks: loom-z3m.1 children T2 + T3.
notes:
Design narrowing 2026-05-18 at claim time:
- T1 prescan is TRANSCRIPT-ONLY. Diary-LESSON marker harvesting deferred to T2 workers (each calls mempalace_diary_read directly via MCP).
- Rationale: ~/.mempalace/palace/ stores diaries inside chroma.sqlite3 (chromadb), not flat files. Original heuristic ("grep wing_claude-opus/.../diary") doesn't match on-disk layout. Cleanest separation is to let T2 workers query the diary via MCP.
- T1 contract unchanged otherwise: Signal A (steering + diagnostic regex on user turns) and Signal B-transcript (Jaccard long-run abandonment + explicit reversions) both stay.
close_reason: Phase 1 T1 shipped — prescan emits hit-lists for 3 corpora. Acceptance: 12/5/98 hits (loom/HAW/liza-base), 0% FP rate on loom 5-hit spot-check, all schemas valid, 26/26 fixtures green. liza-base hit 50-200 target; HAW under-target due to 8-session corpus density.

title: Phase 1: pre-scan + parallel dig + bead pile
description:
Phase 1 of loom-z3m. Run programmatic pre-scan against last 14d transcripts + diaries across 3 projects, dispatch 3 parallel read-only workers (one per project) via Agent+isolation=worktree per loom-7p6 discipline, central agent synthesizes worker outputs into ~10–15 improvement beads filed against loom under this epic.

Signal classes: (A) Frank-correction phrases in user turns; (B) dead-end signatures (long abandoned tool-runs + diary process-LESSON markers).

Deferred to Phase 3: wasted-token re-discovery, dispatched-worker failure dig.

Children: T1 pre-scan tooling → T2 parallel worker dispatch → T3 central synthesis + bead filing (sequential, each blocks the next).
close_reason: Phase 1 epic complete: all 3 children (.1.1 prescan, .1.2 worker dispatch, .1.3 synthesis) closed. 14 improvement beads filed for downstream work. Phase 2 (loom-z3m.2) becomes ready.

title: loom retrospective dig: 2-week usage across loom + liza-base + HAW
description:
Goal: find points where loom struggled, decisions went poorly, or tokens were wasted in ~2 weeks of usage across three projects (~/repos/loom, ~/repos/liza_base, ~/working/hundred-acre-woods).

Three phases, staged with stop-conditions at each boundary:
- Phase 1: programmatic pre-scan → 3 parallel read-only workers → central synthesis → ~15 improvement beads filed against loom
- Phase 2: long-form synthesis drawer (verbatim quotes, cross-cutting patterns)
- Phase 3: struggle taxonomy + structural fixes (hooks/skills/recipes)

Design: drawer_loom_decisions_20dc0710f0dec770fed63684 (mid-brainstorm checkpoint, 4 decisions locked) — close-out drawer to follow this filing.

Stop conditions: Phase 1 yields no surviving findings OR all P3-trivial → close epic. Phase 2 reveals no recurring patterns → file ad-hoc P3 fixes, skip taxonomy epic.

title: docs-a-bead: rewrite Phase B to dispatch worker (with voice handoff)
description:
## Goal

Rewrite skills/docs-a-bead/SKILL.md Phase B from 'central does sample sibling-doc voice → draft → review against code' to 'central samples voice with user; briefs worker on {voice excerpts, doc target, accuracy checks}; reviews return'.

## Files

- skills/docs-a-bead/SKILL.md

## Steps

1. Read current Phase B section.
2. Keep voice-sampling CENTRAL (user in the loop on whether the sibling-doc tone is right for the new doc).
3. Replace drafting + accuracy-review execution with worker dispatch per design Section 2 table row 6.
4. Reference shell's worker-brief template (T1) — docs brief includes a Voice block with confirmed excerpts verbatim.
5. Add voice-handoff guidance per design Section 3: 2-3 short quotes from sampled docs go in Voice block; tone notes go alongside.
6. Note re-dispatch trigger specific to docs: central reviews returned draft for voice drift; if drifted, re-dispatch with sharper voice notes.

## Acceptance

Phase B's voice-sampling-with-user stays central; drafting + accuracy-check delegates; voice handoff is explicit.

## Depends on

loom-7p6.1.

## Provenance

drawer_loom_decisions_9b92cbb794d50a1ef1061006.
close_reason: docs-a-bead variable-middle dispatch-shape rewrite with voice handoff merged; M4 non-negotiability + voice-anchors-in-brief preserved; mkdocs strict clean. Epic loom-7p6 7/7 children complete.

title: research-a-bead: rewrite Phase B to dispatch worker
description:
## Goal

Rewrite skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md Phase B from 'central does search palace + KG + bd memories + external → synthesize → file' to 'central frames question; briefs worker on {question, search surfaces, synthesis shape}; reviews return + files drawer central'.

## Files

- skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md

## Steps

1. Read current Phase B section.
2. Keep question-framing CENTRAL (user-facing dialogue on what we're trying to know).
3. Replace search + synthesis with worker dispatch per design Section 2 table row 5.
4. Reference shell's worker-brief template (T1) — research has no GREEN target so brief uses 'synthesis shape' instead.
5. Drawer filing stays central (the act of mempalace_add_drawer; the drawer body comes from the worker).
6. Note pattern parallel: this generalizes the existing bug-family-researcher subagent pattern to all research beads.

## Acceptance

Phase B's question-framing stays central; search + synthesis delegates; drawer-filing stays central.

## Depends on

loom-7p6.1.

## Provenance

drawer_loom_decisions_9b92cbb794d50a1ef1061006.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 964ad4e, merged via f1f63e8 to main. research-a-bead variable middle rewritten to dispatch worker; question → prior-art → external-docs → synthesize → drawer discipline preserved. State reconciliation only — no new work.

title: cleanup-a-bead: rewrite Phase B to dispatch worker
description:
## Goal

Rewrite skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md Phase B from 'central does identify → hunt orphans → remove → verify' to 'central identifies scope; briefs worker on {removal target, orphan-search scope, verification}; reviews return'.

## Files

- skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md

## Steps

1. Read current Phase B section.
2. Keep scope-identification CENTRAL (user often needs to confirm what's safe to delete).
3. Replace orphan-hunt + removal execution with worker dispatch per design Section 2 table row 4.
4. Reference shell's worker-brief template (T1).

## Acceptance

Phase B's what-to-delete stays central; the deletion + orphan-hunt delegates.

## Depends on

title: refactor-a-bead: rewrite Phase B to dispatch worker
description:

Rewrite skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md Phase B from 'central does characterize → restructure → verify' to 'central identifies scope; briefs worker on {characterization plan, restructure target, invariants}; reviews return'.
