title: bead-lifecycle-shell: Phase B dispatch boilerplate + worker-brief template
description:
## Goal

Add the dispatch-discipline boilerplate to skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md Phase B section, and define the canonical worker-brief template that the six activity recipes will cite.

## Files

- skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md (primary)

## Steps

1. Locate the Phase B section (variable middle) in bead-lifecycle-shell.
2. Add boilerplate at the top of Phase B: 'Dispatch discipline (uniform across all recipes): Phase B is worker territory. Brief a single worker via Agent + isolation: worktree covering the full variable middle in one dispatch. Do NOT use Edit/Write/MultiEdit yourself between bead-claim and bead-close. While the worker runs (background): answer user questions, pre-stage the next bead, or revise the contract — but do NOT start parallel code-work in the central session. The worker returns; you review; you re-dispatch only on surprises.'
3. Add explicit 'while-worker-runs' allowed/forbidden lists per design Section 4.
4. Add worker-brief template (Subject / Context / Scope / Anti-scope / Voice / Dispatch hygiene / Stop-and-report triggers per design Section 3).
5. Add re-dispatch decision rule per design Section 4 (clean / minor polish ≤3 lines central does / substantive rework fresh worker).
6. Note that central re-runs verification after worker returns (trust-but-verify, matches superpowers:verification-before-completion).
7. Clarify which phases central owns: A2/A3 (claim + worktree), C (commit + finish-branch), D (file outputs); which delegate: A1 (bug-family-researcher), B (worker), D drafting (drawer-author + kg-relationship-extractor).

## Acceptance

- Phase B section reads as 'dispatch a worker' not 'do the work yourself'.
- Brief template is concrete (copy-pasteable) not abstract.
- bd-close gate still passes (no test regressions).

## Blocks

T2-T7 (the six recipe rewrites cite this shell's brief template). Once T1 lands, T2-T7 dispatch in parallel.

## Provenance

drawer_loom_decisions_9b92cbb794d50a1ef1061006 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-18).
close_reason: P2 wave 3 — see drawer (forthcoming) for details. Merged via commits b0160f3 (vin) + 9bff3c4 (7p6.1) + 80180ae (z3m.11).

title: Central-agent delegation discipline: dispatch workers from Phase B
description:
## Why this exists

The central conversational agent routinely 'hops into' code-work itself when a single bead is in flight (no parallelism opportunity to motivate dispatching-parallel-agents). The central context fills with code-work tokens that should have lived in an isolated worker context; central's role (user-facing interlocutor + reviewer) gets diluted.

## What this delivers

Recipe-level rewrite of bead-lifecycle-shell + all six activity recipes so that Phase B (the variable middle) mandates worker dispatch via Agent + isolation: worktree. Uniform across all six recipes. Prompt-only enforcement (no PreToolUse hook for first cut).

## Locked design decisions (2026-05-18 brainstorm)

- **Delegation line**: anything recipe-driven (bead claim onward). Out-of-recipe quick fixes stay central.
- **Enforcement**: recipe-level mandate, prompt-only, no hook. (Hook deferred unless drift recurs.)
- **Phase B split**: central designs + dispatches; ONE worker owns RED→GREEN→refactor in a single dispatch; central re-engages on surprises, not on phases.
- **Uniformity**: applies to all six recipes (bugfix, feature, refactor, cleanup, research, docs). docs-a-bead worker gets voice-calibration excerpts in brief to mitigate drift.
- **Sync/background**: background by default. While worker runs, central may answer user questions / pre-stage next bead / revise contract, but must NOT start parallel code-work in central session.

## Children

- T1 (foundation): bead-lifecycle-shell boilerplate + worker-brief template
- T2-T7 (parallel after T1): one per activity recipe, rewriting Phase B

## Provenance

Design captured in drawer_loom_decisions_9b92cbb794d50a1ef1061006 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-18).
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Constitution: PreToolUse enforcement hook (hooks/constitution-enforce.sh)
description:
## Goal
Ship the narrow hard-enforcement hook: PreToolUse on Bash only, parses front-matter, matches command against forbidden/pkg_mgr/run_prefix rules, blocks with helpful suggestion. Fails open on missing yq, malformed YAML, or absent constitution file.

## Files
- hooks/constitution-enforce.sh (new)
- lib/tests/constitution-enforce/*.bats (new test directory, fixture-driven)
- lib/tests/constitution-enforce/fixtures/* (fixture project trees + constitution files)
- install.sh (wire PreToolUse:Bash → constitution-enforce.sh)
- docs/reference/constitution-enforce-hook.md (new — how the hook decides, bypass mechanisms)

## Steps (TDD)
1. Write bats fixtures FIRST: a set of (constitution.md, command, expected_exit, expected_stderr_match) cases covering:
   - bare `python` blocked with `devbox run python` suggestion when shell=devbox
   - `devbox run python` allowed
   - `python --version` allowed via bypass_patterns
   - `npm install` blocked when pkg_mgr=pnpm; suggestion mentions `pnpm install`
   - `pnpm install` allowed
   - command with LOOM_CONSTITUTION_SKIP=1 in env — exit 0
   - No constitution file present — exit 0 silent
   - Malformed YAML — exit 0 with stderr warning
   - yq missing in PATH — exit 0 with stderr warning
   - Substring false-positive (e.g., `tipping.py`) NOT blocked — anchored regex discipline
2. Implement hook to pass fixtures: walk up for .claude/project-constitution.md, parse front-matter via yq, match command, emit suggestion, exit 1 on block
3. Add mtime-keyed cache (XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/loom-constitution-<sha256>.json) — re-parse only on file change
4. Wire into install.sh PreToolUse:Bash chain (after edit-write-pwd-guard which is on Edit/Write)
5. Write reference doc

## Acceptance
- All bats fixtures pass
- Hook is silent in a project without a constitution file
- Cache survives multiple Bash calls (test: confirm yq not re-invoked when file unchanged)
- LOOM_CONSTITUTION_SKIP=1 always bypasses
- install.sh wires hook correctly; uninstall removes it

## Provenance
Parent epic loom-6f8. Depends on schema bead. Design: drawer_loom_decisions_76ec9140c47ff768735358c0.

title: Constitution: surfacing at session-start, subagent dispatch, debugging recipes
description:
## Goal
Wire the constitution into three load points so it never gets forgotten: session-startup, dispatched-agent preflight, and the verification phase of bug/feature/refactor recipes.

## Files
- skills/session-startup/SKILL.md (add step 1b: read constitution into context, surface fingerprint one-liner)
- .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md (add step 0 to smoke battery: cat .claude/project-constitution.md)
- skills/bugfix-a-bead/SKILL.md (add phase-B line: re-read constitution; canonical_commands.test is authoritative)
- skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md (same phase-B line)
- skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md (same phase-B line)
- skills/dispatching-parallel-agents (one-line: worker briefs MUST quote fingerprint front-matter when a constitution exists)

## Steps
1. Edit session-startup SKILL.md — new step 1b between CI health and in-progress check. Reads file if present; surfaces one-line summary; nudges /audit-project if missing in a loom-managed project; never blocks
2. Edit dispatched-agents.md preflight smoke battery — insert step 0 that cats the constitution before pwd verification (information, not action)
3. Edit bugfix/feature/refactor-a-bead phase-B with one-line re-read prompt
4. Note in superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents (if in scope) that worker briefs must include fingerprint
5. research-a-bead, cleanup-a-bead, docs-a-bead deliberately skipped — they don't run project commands

## Acceptance
- Cold-start session in a project WITH constitution surfaces the one-line fingerprint
- Cold-start in loom-managed project WITHOUT constitution shows the soft /audit-project nudge
- Dispatched subagent's first Bash call shows the constitution body in stdout
- Phase-B step appears verbatim in the three recipes
- No regression in session-startup tokens > +2000 in the common case

## Provenance
Parent epic loom-6f8. Depends on schema bead (needs known file location). Design: drawer_loom_decisions_76ec9140c47ff768735358c0.

title: Constitution: capture flow (extend /audit-project --check=constitution)
description:
## Goal
Extend /audit-project with a new --check=constitution mode that detects project tooling via heuristics, drafts the front-matter, interactively confirms per field, writes the file unstaged, and mirrors to MemPalace.

## Files
- skills/audit-project/SKILL.md (extend with new mode)
- agents/project-onboarder.md (extend with detection logic + per-field confirmation)
- .beads/ (KG triple emission, via mempalace_kg_add: <project> uses_shell <shell>, uses_package_manager <pkg>)

## Steps
1. Implement detection heuristics (devbox.json → shell=devbox; pnpm-lock.yaml → pkg_mgr=pnpm; flake.nix → shell=nix-shell; Cargo.toml → runtime=rust; go.mod → runtime=go; Makefile + targets → canonical_commands; ./scripts/test etc. → canonical_commands)
2. Render draft front-matter from detected signals; empty fields stay empty
3. Per-field interactive confirmation prompt (one field at a time, never lump-sum)
4. Write .claude/project-constitution.md UNSTAGED; user reviews and commits
5. Mirror to MemPalace drawer in <project>/decisions room
6. Emit KG triples for project tooling
7. Prose body: emit as MISS [HUMAN AUTHOR] with TODO stub — never agent-authored (loom-d50 lesson)
8. On re-run: diff detection against captured file, surface drift per field with confirm/skip

## Acceptance
- bats tests against fixture project trees (devbox+pnpm, nix+cargo, go-only, python+venv, bare-bash) all detect expected fingerprints
- Manual e2e: run /audit-project --check=constitution in a fresh project, verify file written, drawer mirror created, KG triples present
- Re-run on a project with mutated devbox.json surfaces the diff without overwriting prose body

## Provenance
Parent epic loom-6f8. Depends on schema bead (sibling). Design: drawer_loom_decisions_76ec9140c47ff768735358c0.

title: Constitution: schema + dogfooded sample + reference doc
description:
## Goal
Lock the YAML schema for .claude/project-constitution.md front-matter. Ship a fillable template, a reference doc explaining every field, and a worked example as loom's own .claude/project-constitution.md (loom dogfoods itself).

## Files
- templates/project-constitution.md (new — fillable template with TODO markers in prose body)
- .claude/project-constitution.md (new — loom's own, dogfooding: shell=bash/none, no devbox in loom, scripts/* canonical commands)
- docs/reference/project-constitution.md (new — every front-matter field, allowed values, example)
- references/project-constitution.schema.json (optional — JSON Schema for tooling)

## Steps
1. Define the front-matter schema: shell{enter, run_prefix}, package_manager, language{runtime, version}, forbidden[], canonical_commands{build, test, lint, gen, dev}, bypass_patterns[]
2. Write templates/project-constitution.md with all fields commented + prose-body stub with TODO markers
3. Write loom's own .claude/project-constitution.md (loom uses bash, no pkg mgr, scripts/* and bats commands)
4. Write docs/reference/project-constitution.md (Diataxis reference quadrant)
5. Optional: emit JSON Schema for editor tooling

## Acceptance
- Template fills cleanly for a hypothetical devbox+pnpm Python project
- Loom's own constitution file present and accurate (validates against schema)
- Reference doc covers every field with allowed values and one example each
- No prose-body content auto-generated for the dogfooded sample — Frank authors

## Provenance
Parent epic loom-6f8. Design: drawer_loom_decisions_76ec9140c47ff768735358c0.
close_reason: P2 wave 3 — see drawer (forthcoming) for details. Merged via commits b0160f3 (vin) + 9bff3c4 (7p6.1) + 80180ae (z3m.11).

title: Project constitution: capture, surface, enforce per-project tooling consistency
description:
## Why this exists

Loom's existing primitives — bd memories, CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/, /audit-project — never capture a project's tooling fingerprint (shell-entry, package manager, canonical commands, forbidden patterns). Result: agents drift mid-session and especially in dispatched subagents, reaching for raw `python` in a devbox project, `npm` in a pnpm project, etc. Metaphor: walking into your own house and forgetting which room is the bathroom.

## What this delivers

A new `.claude/project-constitution.md` file per project, holding a YAML front-matter machine-readable tooling fingerprint + Markdown prose conventions. Captured interactively by an extended `/audit-project --check=constitution`. Surfaced at session-start, in subagent briefs, and at debugging-recipe entry. Narrowly enforced via PreToolUse hook (blocks bare `python` in devbox projects, `npm` in pnpm projects, etc.) with `LOOM_CONSTITUTION_SKIP=1` bypass.

## Locked design decisions (from 2026-05-18 brainstorm)

- **Scope**: tooling + conventions; NOT full README-for-agents.
- **Storage**: file primary (committed), palace mirror (searchable).
- **Enforcement**: soft surfacing everywhere + narrow hard hook on Bash. Detect what project uses; enforce consistency. Never prescribe.
- **File**: `.claude/project-constitution.md`, YAML front-matter + Markdown body.
- **Prose body**: never agent-authored (loom-d50 lesson).

## Children

1. Schema + dogfooded sample
2. Capture flow (audit-project extension)
3. Surfacing (3 load points)
4. Enforcement hook
5. Staleness + docs

## Provenance

Design captured in drawer_loom_decisions_76ec9140c47ff768735358c0 (2026-05-18).

title: docs: broken link to how-to/loom-guest.md (should be guest-mode.md) breaks Deploy docs CI
description:
`docs/reference/bd-remember-guest-guard.md` line 3 links to `../how-to/loom-guest.md`, but the actual file in docs/how-to/ is `guest-mode.md`. The doc file was renamed at some point and this one reference was missed.

mkdocs builds with --strict in .github/workflows/, so this single warning fails the workflow. Every push since at least 2026-05-13 has failed Deploy docs as a result.

## Fix

## Acceptance

## Follow-up (separate bead, not blocking)

title: git auto-merge of .beads/issues.jsonl can silently revert bead state
description:
Split off from loom-b20 sub-issue 3 (not in scope for that bead).

## Symptom

When a feature branch is based on stale main and merges back into post-newer-work main, git's line-based auto-merge of \`.beads/issues.jsonl\` can silently revert bead state. The \`--ours\` conflict-resolution pattern only fires on ACTUAL conflicts; auto-merges look successful but reconcile lines across semantic boundaries.

Hit during liza_base 2026-05-06: integrating b33 (worktree based on pre-Wave-C main) into post-Wave-C main reverted closed-state of 1a7, 37b, h1x to their pre-Wave-C open-state. Manual re-close needed.

## Options (from b20 brainstorm)

Three approaches in order of effort:

1. **Document pattern (minimal):** after every merge involving \`.beads/issues.jsonl\`, run \`bd export\` and verify state via \`git diff\`. Cheap, but only as good as the discipline.

2. **Post-merge check in \`bd preflight\` / a git hook:** detect that \`.beads/issues.jsonl\` was touched by a merge and prompt for \`bd export\` reconciliation.

3. **Custom git merge driver (strongest):** ship \`scripts/bd-merge-driver.sh\` that runs \`bd export\` instead of line-merging. Wire via \`.gitattributes\` (\`.beads/issues.jsonl merge=bd-export\`) and install.sh writes the driver into \`.git/config\`.

## Recommendation

Start with (1) as a CLAUDE.md convention reminder; spec out (3) as the durable fix. (2) is the bridge if (3) is too heavy.

## Prior art

- Wave-1 close-out 2026-05-04: canonical reconciliation = \`bd export > .beads/issues.jsonl\`
- loom-22h: \`/issues.jsonl\` gitignored as a defense
- loom-x4m: 3-layer worktree preseed (\`drawer_loom_decisions_d09f9f243008f5a6731542e3\`)

## Out of b20 because

b20 closed two of three sub-issues. This one needs its own design (merge driver script + .gitattributes + install.sh wiring + tests) and is structurally separate.
close_reason: Closed

title: worker-brief pre-flight smoke tests for dispatched agents (path + import + bd state)
description:
Fix (f) from Frank's 2026-05-13 PM writeup. Three smoke tests at the START of every dispatched worker session, catching the most common loom-path-leak modes at their cheapest detection point.

## Tests

1. **Path**: pwd == git rev-parse --show-toplevel (catches Mode 1 if dispatcher's brief had absolute paths)
2. **Import**: python -c 'import <project>; print(<project>.__file__)' resolves to a worktree path (catches Mode 5 Python shadow)
3. **bd state**: bd list -n 1 returns at least one issue, not empty (catches Mode 3 bd-state-empty fresh worktree)

The worker aborts (and asks for guidance) if any test fails.

## Scope

1. Update .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md to:
   - Drop the contradictory 'use relative paths' line (Mode 4 inverts it)
   - Add the three smoke tests as a 'verify environment before starting' section
2. Cross-reference loom-ymc (hook that catches paths at write-time) and the loom-x4m hook (auto-recovery for bd state)
3. Update worker-brief templates (if any in skills/) to include the smoke tests

## Acceptance

- .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md updated and self-consistent
- Smoke test text in a copy-pasteable form workers can include in their first bash call
- Cluster cross-references in place
notes: From Frank's 2026-05-13 PM writeup section 'Why rule-discipline alone keeps failing'. Cheap defense-in-depth on top of loom-ymc's mechanical guard.
close_reason: Closed

title: bd-close-capture regex prefix class allows hyphens (HAW-style multi-hyphen prefixes)
description: Current regex [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3,} allows underscore prefixes (liza_base-dab) but NOT hyphen-separated prefixes like HAW's 'hundred-acre-woods-bng'. Frank reports 3 --force bypasses required this session for cross-repo bd close invocations against HAW IDs. Widen prefix character class to include hyphen: [a-z][a-z0-9_-]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}. Extends loom-gcb.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-13. Forward-only widening.
close_reason: Shipped via commit ddf99f9 on main. One-char regex prefix widening in hooks/bd-close-capture.sh: [a-z][a-z0-9_]* -> [a-z][a-z0-9_-]* so multi-hyphen prefixes like 'hundred-acre-woods-bng' capture in cross-repo bd close invocations. Frank required --force 3x this session for HAW IDs before the fix. Extends loom-gcb (commit 951c6c7, 2026-05-12) which widened the hash {3} -> {3,}; parent design drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628 (loom-8vb, 2026-05-06) still governs — this was another unstated gap in its 'shapes intentionally locked in' list. Regression test added (case hundred-acre-woods-bng). Suite: 32/32 in bd-close-capture (was 31), 250/250 full (was 249). KG triples: loom-2t7 -> closed_by_commit -> ddf99f9; loom-2t7 -> extends_pattern -> bd-close-capture-regex-scoping.

title: bd-close-capture regex prefix class allows hyphens (HAW-style multi-hyphen prefixes)
description:
Current regex [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}(\.[0-9a-z]+)* allows underscore-prefix workspaces (liza_base-dab) but NOT hyphen-separated prefixes like HAW's 'hundred-acre-woods-bng'. Frank reports 3× --force bypass required this session for cross-repo bd close invocations against HAW IDs.

## Fix

Widen the prefix character class to include hyphen:
  [a-z][a-z0-9_-]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}(\.[0-9a-z]+)*

Greedy match still terminates correctly: the last hyphen before the 3+char hash anchors the suffix. 'hundred-acre-woods-bng' parses as [h][undred-acre-woods][-][bng].

## Acceptance

- Existing tests still pass (3-char hash, underscore prefix, sub-suffix, 4-char hash from loom-gcb).
- New test case: 'bd close hundred-acre-woods-bng' extracts the full ID.
- Full loom shell-fixture suite stays green.

Extends loom-gcb (closed 2026-05-12, commit 951c6c7) + loom-8vb (locked design drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628). Forward-only widening.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-13 by Frank after 3 --force bypasses during this session for HAW-style cross-repo bd close.

title: worktree rebase WIP snapshot/restore wrapper script
description:
Per loom-35b split (drawer pending): when an agent crashes mid-flight in a worktree (e.g. API 529) leaving WIP files on disk, a resume agent that runs 'git stash + rebase + pop' choreography wipes untracked WIP. This was observed in liza_base 2026-05-06 across 9uo's resume agent (328-LOC background.py + 365-LOC test_background.py from previous crash didn't survive the stash/rebase recovery).

The 9uo case was rescued by the thorough decision drawer enabling code reconstruction — but that's tribal knowledge dependent. A mechanical wrapper would prevent the loss.

## Fix shape

Ship a wrapper script 'scripts/loom-rebase-worktree' that:

1. Lists untracked files in the worktree (excluding .beads/embeddeddolt/* which the bd-worktree-preseed hook self-heals, and standard ignores).
2. Copies them to a temp dir alongside their relative path metadata.
3. Runs the requested rebase command.
4. Restores the untracked files to their original paths (only if they don't conflict with files brought in by rebase).
5. Reports what was preserved + what conflicts arose.

Optional: also snapshot .beads/embeddeddolt/ for completeness (though 35b-A makes that redundant).

## Acceptance

- Wrapper script handles vanilla 'rebase' and 'rebase -i'.
- Test fixture: worktree with WIP files + rebase → WIP preserved post-rebase.
- Documented in docs/reference/ (new file or extend bd-worktree-preseed.md).
- Worker brief template in loom CLAUDE.md (or new doc) recommends using the wrapper for any rebase choreography.
notes: Split from loom-35b (3 symptoms). This addresses symptom 2 (WIP loss on stash+rebase). Larger effort than 35b-A; takes a wrapper-script approach.
close_reason: Shipped via merge commit 1fd0338 on main (worker commit 2fff674 on frank/loom-azt). New scripts/loom-rebase-worktree wrapper: refuses in main tree; snapshots untracked WIP (excluding .beads/embeddeddolt); pre-detects collisions and renames colliding WIP to <path>.wip BEFORE rebase (to avoid git's untracked-would-be-overwritten abort); runs git rebase; restores snapshot post-rebase. Retains snapshot tmpdir on rebase failure or collisions for manual recovery. 9 fixture tests cover preserved-WIP / no-WIP-passthrough / collision-becomes-.wip / dolt-excluded / main-tree-refusal. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 17/17 files, 245 tests (was 236, +9 new), 0 failures. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<filed-this-call> captures full design + lessons-during-implementation (initial design assumed git would let rebase proceed with colliding untracked → wrong; pre-detection is required). bd-state corruption paths in worktree-isolation cluster are now fully covered (x4m + 8vc + azt). Remaining cluster: loom-ecf (P3 stale-base docs/convention), loom-0hi (P2 cwd drift, separate concern). KG triples: loom-azt -> closed_by_commit -> 1fd0338; loom-azt -> complements -> loom-x4m.

title: Investigate actual SessionStart-prefix injection point for subagents (CC 2.1.112+)
description:
loom-w58 worker (2026-05-12) discovered empirically that Claude Code 2.1.112 may not fire SessionStart hooks for sidechains at all. Worker checked ~/.claude/projects/-home-frank-repos-loom/*/subagents/*.jsonl transcripts and found no SessionStart-hook records inside subagent sessions.

If that's correct, the ~21KB subagent preamble tax cited in loom-nsb research is being delivered through a different injection point — likely the system-reminder mechanism, not SessionStart hooks. loom-w58's defensive guard still works (no-op when not invoked, skip when invoked) but the savings don't materialize today.

## Scope

1. Sample 3-5 recent subagent transcripts and identify exactly where the preamble bytes are coming from (system-reminder vs SessionStart vs some other injection point).
2. If system-reminder: investigate whether loom can intercept (likely not — system-reminders are user-message injections from the harness).
3. Document findings in loom/decisions with concrete byte attribution.
4. Decide whether loom-w58's guard should stay, expand to cover the actual injection point, or be reverted as no-op.

## Acceptance

- Findings drawer in loom/decisions naming the actual injection mechanism + byte counts per subagent invocation.
- Either: (a) follow-up fix bead filed against the actual injection point, or (b) confirmation that the loom-w58 guard already covers it, or (c) ack that the injection is unreachable from loom hooks.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-12 by loom-w58 worker empirical check. See drawer_loom_decisions_a4a6babeb0557cfd6a949692 for context.
close_reason: Research complete. Empirical finding via 53 subagent transcripts in ~/.claude/projects/-home-frank-repos-loom: subagents are billed ~9-10K cached input tokens of preamble per dispatch (visible via cache_read_input_tokens), but the bytes arrive via the API system prompt slot — NOT via SessionStart hooks, which confirmed do NOT fire for sidechains in CC 2.1.112 (zero SessionStart-hook records in any sidechain JSONL). Three contributors: (1) agent definition file content (loom-controllable for loom agents in ~/.claude/agents/), (2) tools schema descriptions (harness-set), (3) Anthropic harness baseline (not loom-touchable). Findings drawer drawer_loom_decisions_97575168914bc64b58007112 documents full empirical data + re-evaluation of related beads. Implications: loom-w58 is defensive-no-op today (correct shape if CC behavior changes, but doesn't save bytes now); loom-b1l still useful for app-spawned Claude Code subprocesses where SessionStart DOES fire; loom-98x's subagent-skip half similarly suspect (should split); loom-nc2 only helps main agent (real savings nonetheless). Filed follow-up loom-a82 (P3) for the actually-actionable savings path: trim loom agent definitions (556 lines across 4 files, project-onboarder is heaviest at 224). 4 KG triples filed for cross-referenceable facts (delivered_via, does_not_fire, is defensive-no-op-today, spawned_followup).

title: Stop-hook auto-save: gate on substance heuristic + skip in subagents
description:
**From loom-nsb research (drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec).**

The MemPalace stop-hook auto-save fires unconditionally on every Stop event. Each forced save costs an extra Claude turn (mempalace_diary_write + mempalace_add_drawer round-trip), which bills the *full current context* through one more turn. Sampled transcript showed 10 stop-hook auto-save fires in one ~9-hour session — many of those saves likely repeat near-identical diary content.

Two sub-fixes:

## Part A: gate on session-substance heuristic

Don't force auto-save if the session was substantively idle since the last save. Heuristic candidates (need design call):
- ≥N tool uses since last save (e.g. 5)
- ≥N user messages since last save (e.g. 2)
- ≥N bd commands since last save (e.g. 1)
- Time-since-last-save floor (e.g. 10 min)
- Some combination

The user can still manually save via /wrap-up or explicit MCP call.

## Part B: skip in subagents

Application-helper subagents (e.g. liza_base voice translators) and parallel-dispatch workers should NOT trigger auto-save — they don't carry session state worth preserving, and the stop-hook turn-amplification compounds across N subagents in a wave.

Detect subagent context via the same mechanism as the slim-SessionStart bead (isSidechain / parentUuid). When detected, the stop-hook short-circuits with a no-op exit 0 (no blocking error, no forced save).

## Estimated savings

5-15% on chatty / multi-stop-event sessions. Compounds with the slim-SessionStart fix on parallel-dispatch workloads.

## Design call

Substance heuristic threshold + which signals to use — file as a design question on this bead before implementing. Could land Part B (subagent skip) immediately and defer Part A (substance heuristic) for a separate design pass.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. Part B (subagent skip) compounds with the slim-SessionStart bead — both should detect subagent context the same way.
close_reason: Closed as upstream-pending. Per loom-7zy + 98x scope investigation (drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<filed-this-call>): the offending hook is the MemPalace plugin's mempal-stop-hook.sh (~/.claude/plugins/cache/mempalace/3.3.0/), a thin shell wrapper around python3 -m mempalace hook run --hook stop. The substance-gate fix must live in mempalace's own Python entry point — loom hooks can't suppress sibling-plugin stop-hook chains (Claude Code's hook spec has no 'block subsequent hooks' mechanism; each hook independently decides). Subagent-skip half is moot in CC 2.1.112: confirmed 0 stop-hook records across 3 sample sidechain JSONLs. Proposed upstream issue draft captured in the drawer; Frank to file at github.com/MemPalace/mempalace when timing is right. KG triples: loom-98x -> closed_as_upstream_pending -> mempalace-plugin-stop-hook; cc-2.1.112-sidechain -> does_not_fire -> Stop-hooks. If upstream gate lands, file a new loom bead to verify behavior + update docs if user-facing config needed.

title: liza_base/.claude/settings.json registers duplicate bd prime SessionStart + PreCompact hooks
description:
**Static-phase finding from loom-nsb research (drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec).**

`bd prime` is registered as a SessionStart + PreCompact hook in TWO places, causing it to fire 6× per session in liza_base (~900 KB of duplicate content in a single 5.4 MB transcript):

1. `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/beads-marketplace/claude-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` — plugin-level registration (the canonical one).
2. `/home/frank/repos/liza_base/.claude/settings.json` — project-level registration that DUPLICATES the plugin's. Both have empty matcher, so both fire on every SessionStart subtype + PreCompact.

Verified via direct grep of the settings.json hooks block.

## Fix

One-file edit in liza_base: remove the SessionStart + PreCompact entries from `liza_base/.claude/settings.json`'s hooks block. The beads plugin already registers them.

## Estimated savings

~50% of SessionStart bytes in liza_base — ~8-9% of session bytes overall, larger in context-window terms because these blobs sit in the prefix and re-bill every turn.

## Why filed in loom

The actual fix is a single-line config edit in liza_base, not loom code. But the *finding* came from loom-nsb research, and the cross-project visibility belongs here. Filing the actual fix execution in loom for tracking — when closing, note the liza_base commit SHA.

Also unblocks the audit-detection feature: the canonical pattern for what "duplicate plugin-registered hook command" looks like is documented here.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. The actual fix is a one-file edit in liza_base/.claude/settings.json (remove the duplicate SessionStart + PreCompact bd prime registrations).
close_reason: Shipped via liza_base commit 2da2ab1. Removed duplicate bd prime SessionStart + PreCompact hook registrations from liza_base/.claude/settings.json (now {}). Plugin-level registration via beads-plugin's plugin.json remains canonical. Estimated ~50% of liza_base SessionStart bytes reclaimed. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_05bc14d4a8c5759d0f1c7a6c. Preventive sibling loom-ann (audit-project Check 5) flagged for future work.

title: research: token burn analysis for loom running in liza_base
description:
FRA flagged that loom running in ~/repos/liza_base is burning a lot of tokens. Question: is this inherent to liza_base's task difficulty, or is loom contributing avoidable overhead that can be tightened?

Scope:
1. Dig through recent transcripts in ~/.claude/projects/-home-frank-repos-liza-base/ — what are the dominant token-cost contributors? (Large tool outputs, frequent SessionStart hook re-injection, stop-hook auto-saves, MemPalace status returns, agent dispatches, etc.)
2. Audit loom's injection surface — SessionStart hook content, statusline, skill list, MemPalace protocol prompts, bd prime output.
3. Compare against what a non-loom session would inject (baseline).
4. Identify avoidable vs inherent overhead.
5. Propose tightening opportunities with rough token-savings estimates.

Output: findings drawer in loom/decisions room + follow-up beads if avoidable overhead found.
notes: FRA prompt: loom in ~/repos/liza_base is burning lots of tokens. Research whether problems are inherently difficult or loom can be tightened. Filed 2026-05-09.
close_reason: Research complete. Findings drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec. Six follow-up beads filed: loom-w58 (P1, slim SessionStart for subagents — top-leverage), loom-b1l (P3, LOOM_SUBAGENT_LEAN env opt-out), loom-sd5 (P2, duplicate bd prime in liza_base settings.json), loom-ann (P3, audit-project duplicate-hook detection), loom-98x (P2, stop-hook substance gate + subagent skip), loom-nc2 (P3, trim bd prime memories injection). Honest split: ~50-55% user-task / ~25-30% loom-injected / ~10-15% mixed. Static-side biggest win = removing duplicate bd prime (~8-9% session). Dynamic-side biggest win = slim SessionStart for subagents (~20% per subagent invocation, compounds across high-fanout workloads like liza_base's voice translator).

title: bd CLI / hook gotchas: --notes brittle on close, close hook needs literal bead ID, auto-merge regresses state
description:
Three bd-related gotchas surfaced during heavy parallel-agent dispatch.

## 1. `bd close --notes "..."` flag silently fails

Multiple agents tried `bd close <id> --notes "..."` and the command spilled bd's full help text to stdout without surfacing an error — the bead was NOT closed. Bare `bd close <id>` worked. Notes can be added separately via `bd update <id> --notes`.

Hit by t5j integration step in liza_base 2026-05-06: 'bd close with --notes flag failed silently bd help text spilled no error surfaced bare bd close worked.'

Suspected cause: `--notes` is a valid `bd update` flag but not `bd close`. The CLI parser may be falling back to help-text rendering instead of failing loudly with 'unknown flag'.

**Proposed fix:** Either accept `--notes` on `bd close` (forward to an implicit update + close), or fail loudly with 'unknown flag for close subcommand'.

## 2. bd-close-capture hook requires literal bead ID match in evidence

The PreToolUse hook `bd-close-capture.sh` blocks `bd close` unless drawer/KG-triple/diary/memory mentions the bead ID literally. Drawer titled 'B33 ARCHITECTURE LOCKED' (short form) failed the check; full form `liza_base-b33` would have passed.

The agent had to use `BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1 bd close liza_base-b33` as documented bypass.

**Proposed fix:** hook could accept short-form bead IDs (e.g., `b33` matching `liza_base-b33`) when scanning drawer bodies. OR: every agent brief that involves filing a decision drawer should be told 'reference the bead ID literally (`<prefix>-<short>`) in the drawer body, not just the title.'

## 3. bd-state regression on git auto-merge of issues.jsonl

When a feature branch is based on stale main and merges back into post-newer-work main, git's line-based auto-merge of `.beads/issues.jsonl` can silently revert bead state. The `--ours` conflict-resolution pattern only fires on ACTUAL conflicts; auto-merges look successful but reconcile lines across semantic boundaries.

Hit when integrating b33 (worktree based on pre-Wave-C main) into post-Wave-C main: the auto-merge reverted closed-state of 1a7, 37b, h1x to their pre-Wave-C open-state. Manual re-close needed.

**Proposed fix:**
- Document pattern: after every merge involving `.beads/issues.jsonl`, run `bd export` + `git diff` and verify bd state didn't regress.
- Or: loom's integration recipe could include a post-merge bd-state diff check.
- Or: switch `.beads/issues.jsonl` merge driver to `-X ours` always, with reconciliation done explicitly via `bd export` afterward.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-06 across liza_base FRH dispatch sessions.
close_reason: Closed

title: worktree-isolation: stale base + WIP loss on rebase + bd db wipe on rebase
description:
Three closely-related rebase-flow gotchas that bit multiple parallel-dispatch agents in a single session.

## 1. Worktree branch base may be stale

`Agent({isolation: "worktree"})` doesn't always branch the worktree from latest main. In a session with multiple sequential waves of dispatch, later worktrees may inherit a base from earlier in the session — possibly missing intervening merges that the new bead depends on.

Hit by:
- bqo's agent (Wave B 2026-05-06): 'Worktree branched off pre-e5n. 5d4c6fd predates the e5n merge cf91178 on local main. Rebased onto local main to pick up the VoiceSection.anchor_path field; tests then turned GREEN.'
- 9uo's retry agent: had to rebase onto main 1959aad to pick up pdx + h1x.
- 982's retry agent: was 5 commits behind main, rebased before working.

## 2. Untracked WIP files don't survive rebase

Stash + rebase choreography wipes untracked files. An agent that crashes mid-flight (e.g., API 529) leaves WIP on disk; a resume agent that rebases its worktree onto current main loses that WIP.

Hit by:
- 9uo's resume agent: '328-LOC background.py + 365-LOC test_background.py from previous crash DID NOT survive the stash/rebase recovery dance. Reconstructed from scratch using the canonical 9uo design drawer.' (The reconstruction worked because the decision drawer was thorough — turning decision drawers into a load-bearing crash-resume surface.)

## 3. bd db gets wiped during rebase

Multiple agents reported the worktree's bd db getting wiped during rebase choreography, requiring re-import.

Hit by:
- wx7's agent: 'bd db reset after rebase. The worktree's bd db was wiped during the rebase choreography; first commit landed with an empty jsonl. Caught + recovered.'
- 982's retry agent: 'bd db got wiped at one point during the session (as the brief warned can happen) — re-imported from parent's working-copy jsonl after the wipe.'
- 9uo's retry agent: 'The bd database got transiently wiped during the rebase-recovery flow (twice — handled by re-importing from main and re-claiming each time).'

## Proposed fix

**(a) Bundle them — loom hardens worktree-rebase choreography:**
- Pre-rebase: snapshot WIP files to a safe location.
- Pre-rebase: snapshot bd db state.
- After rebase: restore both.
- Document that 'after rebase, verify bd state' as a concrete agent step until the choreography is reliable.

**(b) Promote 'file decision drawer FIRST, before substantial code' from convention to recipe-mandatory step.** The 9uo case proved a thorough decision drawer can rebuild substantial code. Recipes for parallel-dispatched agents should require the drawer in phase A, before any non-test code edits.

**(c) loom should branch worktrees from current local main HEAD always, not from some prior reference point.** If `Agent({isolation: "worktree"})` lets loom specify the base, use `main` (latest local). If it doesn't, loom may need to inject a 'rebase onto main' step at worktree creation.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-06 across the liza_base FRH dispatch sessions.
close_reason: Split into 3 sibling beads per loom-7zy-style scope analysis (drawer to follow). The original bead bundled 3 distinct symptoms with different mechanisms; splitting per loom CLAUDE.md heuristic. Successors: loom-8vc P3 (harden bd-worktree-preseed for dolt-empty re-preseed — symptom 3, the bd db wipe), loom-azt P2 (WIP snapshot/restore wrapper script — symptom 2, WIP loss on stash+rebase), loom-ecf P3 (stale base convention + worker-brief template — symptom 1, worktree branched off pre-merge state). x4m's hook (merge 467c9e2) already partially covers symptom 3; 8vc completes the coverage by removing the sentinel-survives-but-dolt-doesn't gap. Starting work on 8vc next as the smallest immediate win.

title: worktree-isolation: cwd drift between Bash calls causes edits to land in parent repo
description:
Subagents dispatched via `Agent({isolation: "worktree"})` start in the worktree's cwd, but the Bash tool's cwd appears to reset to the parent repo between calls in some configurations. Agents calling Edit/Write with absolute paths or relative paths that depend on cwd assumption end up modifying files in the PARENT repo, not the worktree.

## Symptoms

- Agent runs `Edit` on `tests/foo.py` (relative): edit lands in parent repo.
- Agent runs `Bash` with `cd .claude/worktrees/agent-X`, then a subsequent `Bash` call has cwd back at parent repo.
- Most clearly: agent commits succeed (because git commit operates on cwd), but those commits land on whichever branch parent's HEAD is on — sometimes `main` directly.

## Concrete cases (liza_base 2026-05-06 FRH session)

- e5n's agent: 'Bash tool's cwd reset between calls hid this from me until the final git status. Recovered cleanly: snapshotted the diff via git diff in main, applied to worktree, reverted main with git checkout --.'
- bqo's agent: 'Worktree CWD reset gotcha — initial bash cd switched out of the worktree; first round of edits hit the parent repo. Reverted those, copied the changes into the worktree.'
- cue, 4mv, w8v: committed directly to main instead of their worktree branch (subsequent merge attempts said 'Already up to date' because main had the commits). Their work landed but in the wrong place.
- Bundle A's m6c+1a7 agent: 'The Edit tool repeatedly silently failed to persist changes on tests/palace/test_client.py early in the session — Read returned phantom content while the file on disk stayed at HEAD.'

## Proposed fix

- (a) Pin cwd absolute path in agent briefs: 'Run `cd /home/frank/repos/<project>/.claude/worktrees/agent-<hash>` at the start of EVERY Bash call.' Heavy but defensive.
- (b) loom's parallel-agent dispatching could pass an explicit `workingDirectory` parameter that pins the agent's cwd — if Claude Code's Agent tool supports such a thing, loom should leverage it.
- (c) Fix the Bash tool's cwd persistence within an Agent session if the issue is upstream.

## Likely upstream

If Claude Code's Bash tool documents that cwd persists in the harness but doesn't actually persist for sub-agents in worktree-isolation mode, that's an upstream bug worth filing against Claude Code itself.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-06 across the liza_base FRH dispatch sessions. ~3 of 12 worktree agents committed to main directly; 2 more reverted edits that landed in main.
close_reason: Superseded by worktree-isolation cluster: edit-write-pwd-guard (loom-ymc) catches Edit/Write leaks from cwd-drift, bd-worktree-preseed (loom-x4m) catches bd-state regressions, pre-flight smoke battery (loom-g5k) catches pwd-mismatch at the first bash call. Phantom-Edit case (bundle A) is structurally distinct — re-file as new bead if observed. See drawer_loom_decisions_ff3824488f235d5837c8cfc7.

title: worktree-isolation: bd embedded-dolt db starts empty + bd writes from worktree silently land in parent repo
description:
When a subagent is dispatched via `Agent({isolation: "worktree"})`, the worktree gets created with `.beads/issues.jsonl` checked in (from HEAD) but the embedded dolt db itself is empty. Agents that run `bd update --claim` or `bd close` from the worktree's cwd get a permissions warning (`.beads has permissions 0775`) and the writes silently land against the PARENT repo's bd db, not the worktree's.

## Symptoms

- `bd update <id> --claim` from worktree: warning fires, parent's bd db is updated, worktree's stays empty.
- `bd close` from worktree: same. Bead state in worktree's db remains stale.
- After commit + push of the worktree's branch, parent main may have closed the bead but the worktree's `.beads/issues.jsonl` (committed via the auto-export hook) reflects an inconsistent state.

## Frequency

Hit by 6+ agents in a single liza_base FRH session (2026-05-06): Wave A's e5n, t5j; Wave B's 8k7; release-gate Bundle A; Wave C's wx7, 982, 9uo retry. Multiple agents had to recover by:

```
cp /home/frank/repos/<project>/.beads/issues.jsonl .beads/issues.jsonl
bd import .beads/issues.jsonl
```

**Critically:** import from the parent's WORKING-COPY jsonl, not HEAD's — pending bd writes from the dispatching session may not be in HEAD yet. (5h2's agent surfaced this nuance.)

## Proposed fix

Two paths:

**(a) loom seeds the worktree's bd db at dispatch time.** When loom (or the dispatching-parallel-agents skill) creates the worktree, it should also `bd import` the parent's working-copy jsonl into the worktree's bd db. Cleanest — agents never see the gotcha.

**(b) loom adds 'bd import' as the first step in every parallel-agent activity recipe brief.** Cheaper but assumes the agent reads carefully. Could be a literal preamble that agents inherit.

## Sibling concerns to consider

- `.beads/` directory permissions (0775) are looser than bd recommends (0700). The warning is benign but suggests loom's worktree-creation isn't running the same setup that `bd init` does. Probably fix-in-passing when (a) is implemented.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-06 across the liza_base FRH dispatch sessions. Captured as bd memory `worktree-bd-db-empty-needs-import-2026-05-06` in liza_base + MemPalace findings drawer `drawer_liza_base_findings_e9f8af27fa9e3a63c913ea28`.

title: loom-guest: BEADS_DIR resolution for personal-bd mode
description:
Goal: When guest mode is active with bd_mode == "personal", bd commands should resolve to ~/.loom/guests/<repo-key>/.beads/ instead of the cwd's .beads/.

Files:
- ~/.claude/scripts/session-startup-bd-resolve.sh (new) OR augment session-startup skill
- Possibly a tiny bd wrapper at ~/.local/bin/bd-loom that exec's bd with --workspace
- lib/tests/bd-resolve.bats (new)

Approach (pick whichever bd actually supports — verify by reading bd --help):
- Option A: bd respects BEADS_DIR env var → export it from session startup.
- Option B: bd has a --workspace flag → wrap bd in a shim that injects it.
- Option C: bd walks up from cwd looking for .beads/ → create a symlink at ~/.loom/guests/<repo-key>/.beads/ inside the repo (defeats guest mode; reject).

Repo-key: <basename(toplevel)>-<sha8(toplevel-path)>. Example: ~/repos/atlas → atlas-a3f8b2c1.

Steps:
1. Investigate bd's workspace resolution; pick option A or B.
2. Write bats covering: in non-guest mode, bd resolves cwd .beads/; in guest+host mode, bd resolves cwd .beads/ (host's); in guest+personal mode, bd resolves external workspace.
3. Implement.
4. Add `loom guest init` initialization step that creates the external .beads/ directory and runs `bd init` against it (when bd_mode=personal is chosen).

Acceptance: bats pass; bd ready in a guest+personal repo only shows beads from the external workspace.

Depends on: task 2 (workflow-state guest.bd_mode + repo_key). Coordinates with task 1 (which initializes bd_mode and triggers external bd init).
close_reason: Closed

title: loom-guest: statusline [GUEST] indicator
description:
Goal: Statusline shows [GUEST] prominently when guest mode is active; [GUEST/personal-bd] when bd_mode == "personal".

Files:
- ~/.claude/scripts/statusline.sh (and the loom repo's source)
- lib/tests/statusline-guest.bats (new)

Format:
- Inactive: existing statusline unchanged.
- Active, bd_mode=host: prefix with "[GUEST] " (with trailing space).
- Active, bd_mode=personal: "[GUEST/personal-bd] ".
- Active, bd_mode=none: "[GUEST/no-bd] ".

Steps:
1. Write bats fixture covering all states.
2. Add a guest-prefix function near the top of statusline.sh that reads workflow-state guest.active + guest.bd_mode and emits the prefix.
3. Insert prefix at the start of the rendered statusline string.
4. Verify visually + bats.

Acceptance: bats pass; live statusline shows expected prefix in each state.

Depends on: task 2 (workflow-state guest fields). Independent of tasks 1, 3-5, 7-9; can parallelize with them once 2 lands.
close_reason: Closed

title: loom-guest: bd remember interceptor (case A only)
description:
Goal: PreToolUse hook that intercepts bd remember when guest mode is active AND bd_mode == "host", refuses with MemPalace-drawer hint.

Files:
- hooks/bd-remember-guest-guard.sh (new)
- settings.json snippet to wire it (do NOT commit to ~/.claude/settings.json — document the snippet)
- lib/tests/bd-remember-guard.bats (new)

Behavior:
- Inactive guest mode → pass through (no-op).
- Active + bd_mode=host → print to stderr: "Guest mode + host bd. Refusing bd remember (would commit to host's issues.jsonl). Use a MemPalace drawer instead: mempalace_add_drawer wing=<project> room=notes ..." Exit 2 (block tool).
- Active + bd_mode=personal → pass through (your own external bd is fine to remember to).
- Active + bd_mode=none → pass through (there's no bd to remember to anyway; let bd's own error message handle it).

Steps:
1. Write bats covering all four states above.
2. Implement hook reading tool input JSON (look for "bd remember" in tool_input.command).
3. Wire via PreToolUse hook config in settings.json snippet.
4. Verify with bats.

Acceptance: All four states behave per spec. False positives (e.g., bd remember in a non-bd context, or a script that contains the literal string) — prefer false-allow over false-deny; the goal is steering, not iron-clad enforcement.

Depends on: task 2 (workflow-state guest.bd_mode).
close_reason: Closed

title: loom-guest: shared refuse-on-guest hook helper
description:
Goal: Shared bash helper that hooks/skills source to check guest mode and refuse with a consistent message format.

Files:
- lib/refuse-on-guest.sh (new)
- lib/tests/refuse-on-guest.bats (new)

API:
- refuse_if_guest <action-name> [<override-hint>]
  - Reads workflow-state get guest.active.
  - If active: prints "Guest mode active — refusing <action>. Run /loom-guest off to override." to stderr; exit 1.
  - If inactive: returns 0, no output.

Wired into:
- /docs-scaffold skill (refuse before scaffolding)
- /audit-project AUTOFIX phase (refuse per in-tree-write item; checks themselves still run)
- Any hook that writes to .claude/rules/, CLAUDE.md, README.md in host tree

Steps:
1. Write bats covering: refuses when active, exits 1, message format correct; passes through when inactive.
2. Implement helper.
3. Wire into /docs-scaffold (separate small edits).
4. Wire into /audit-project AUTOFIX — finer-grained: each AUTOFIX item that writes in-tree must call refuse_if_guest "<item-name>".
5. Verify with bats + manual smoke (run /docs-scaffold inside guest-mode test repo).

Acceptance: bats pass; /docs-scaffold refuses cleanly in guest mode; AUTOFIX skips in-tree items but still runs read-only checks.

Depends on: task 2 (workflow-state guest fields).

title: loom-guest: .git/info/exclude idempotent helper
description:
Goal: Small bash helper that idempotently appends loom's exclude lines to .git/info/exclude on guest-on, and removes them on guest-off. Per-clone, never committed.

- lib/info-exclude.sh (new)

- info_exclude_add <pattern>... — append if not already present, wrap in BEGIN-LOOM/END-LOOM block markers so removal is precise.
- info_exclude_remove — strip the BEGIN-LOOM..END-LOOM block.
- info_exclude_status — return 0 if block present, 1 otherwise.

  .claude/settings.json
  .claude/rules/

Steps:

2. Implement helper using awk/sed with explicit BEGIN-LOOM/END-LOOM block markers.
3. Verify against bats.

title: Loom guest mode — workflow without polluting host repos
description:
Goal: A per-repo flag that lets loom run its full workflow (recipes, hooks, MemPalace capture, statusline) inside repos where the user is a guest, without writing artifacts to the host tree.

Design: drawer_loom_decisions_12d7f8163e8855be037a007c (loom/decisions, 2026-05-06).

Activation: /loom-guest on (persistent), /loom-guest off (reversible), /loom-guest status. No auto-detect.

Storage: in-tree files (workflow.json, settings.json) hidden via .git/info/exclude (per-clone, not committed). docs/ never scaffolded. .beads/ uses host's if present, otherwise optional external personal workspace at ~/.loom/guests/<repo-key>/.beads/. MemPalace wing uses same project-named convention (concern is not polluting host repos, not siloing palace).

bd integration — three cases:
- A: host has bd → use it; bd remember disabled (would commit); bd dolt push disabled unless host has remote.
- B: no host bd → prompt once for personal-external or skip.
- C: --personal-bd flag forces case B even when host bd exists.

Hooks/recipes: /docs-scaffold and /audit-project AUTOFIX hard-refuse with /loom-guest off hint. Activity recipes, MemPalace capture, code-review skills run unchanged. Statusline shows [GUEST] (or [GUEST/personal-bd]) prominently. Drawer-author prefixes guest-project drawers with [guest project: <repo>].

9 child tasks. Parallel-safe: 1, 6, 9. Sequential: 2 unblocks 3-5, 7, 8.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: bd-close-capture: real artifact verification + regex fix + honest error message
description:
The bd-close-capture PreToolUse hook is broken in two ways and degrades workflow integrity. Locked design lives in MemPalace drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-06).

CURRENT STATE (verified by reading hooks/bd-close-capture.sh end-to-end):

Bug A — cosmetic regex captures words from --reason text. Line 42 greps the entire command line, so `bd close liza_base-dab --reason "...re-dispatch...rule-based..."` parses out tokens like 're-dispatch' and 'rule-based' as if they were bead IDs. Confusing error messages.

Bug B — the hook performs no verification at all. In full mode with no bypass flag, it unconditionally exits 2. There is no MemPalace query, no drawer lookup, no KG check, no diary check. The error message ("until the bead's decision drawer + KG triples + diary entry have been captured") implies a verification the code does not perform.

Consequence: /wrap-up itself can't close cleanly. Its step 4 runs bare `bd close`, which hits the unconditional block. Every close path requires BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1, including the canonical ritual. Two consecutive liza_base trials (2026-05-04, 2026-05-05) had to bypass.

NEW DESIGN (locked):

Pure (b) — real artifact verification with broader matchers. The hook scans for evidence of capture for <bead-id> in any of these forms:

1. Drawer in any room of the project's wing (not just `decisions`) mentioning the bead ID
2. KG triple where subject or object references the bead ID or its work
3. Diary entry mentioning the bead ID since claim time
4. bd memory containing or tagged with the bead ID
5. Substantive close --reason — ≥200 chars AND references a commit SHA or drawer ID

Pass condition: ANY ONE of the five. Block only when ZERO evidence exists.

Why broader matchers: surveyed liza_base (135 drawers across 5 rooms) and tla_puzzles (213 drawers across 11 rooms — audit_results, curriculum, pedagogy_rules, expertise, etc.). Drawers don't only live in `decisions`. Wave-batch closes, repairs, and epic roll-ups have lineage in substantive close_reasons or sibling drawers, not always in their own canonical triple.

Why pure (b) over (c) sentinel-from-/wrap-up: /wrap-up can fail mid-ritual (mempalace -32000 errors), subagent-to-file gap (drawer drafted but never filed), explicit user skip (no drawer flag). Sentinel would silently grant lineage-loss. Verification catches /wrap-up's own bugs — defense in depth. Cost is cheap: filesystem grep against MemPalace JSON files is millisecond-fast and race-free.

ERROR MESSAGE FORMAT (mandatory: explicit "what's missing"):

```
[bd-close-capture hook] No capture evidence found for beads-cve.

Looked for (need ANY ONE):
  ✗ Drawer in liza_base/* mentioning beads-cve
  ✗ KG triple referencing beads-cve
  ✗ Diary entry since claim mentioning beads-cve
  ✗ bd memory tagged with beads-cve
  ✗ Substantive close --reason (≥200 chars + commit/drawer ID)

Recommended:
  /wrap-up                            — full ritual (drawer + KG + diary)

Or, for wave-batch closes, add lineage to --reason:
  bd close beads-cve --reason "Wave 1 voice pass: filter haiku→sonnet,
  drives saturation 1.0→0.8. Commit abc1234. Sibling drawer
  drawer_liza_base_decisions_<id>."

Bypass (use sparingly):
  BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1 bd close beads-cve   # auditable; recorded in workflow state
```

When SOME evidence is found, mark each matcher with ✓/✗ and allow with a one-line warning if at least one matcher passed.

SCOPE (single hook file, single bead, single branch):

1. Replace unconditional block with 5-matcher artifact verification
2. Fix bead-ID regex to parse only the `bd close <ids...>` arg list, ignore --reason "..." contents
3. Map bead-prefix → MemPalace wing (reuse loom-c9z's wing auto-detect from lib)
4. Filesystem-direct queries for drawer/KG/diary (no MCP cache lag, race-free per loom-2xh)
5. Rewrite error message to enumerate what's missing + actionable fixes
6. Shell-fixture tests under lib/tests/ covering: full-ritual pass, wave-batch substantive-reason pass, missing-everything block, regex-parses-only-bead-args, multi-room drawer match
7. Once shipped: supersede loom-2xh — its race diagnosis is wrong; this fix resolves the same trial symptom

Bypass paths preserved unchanged: --force, BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1, mode != full. Goal is to make the canonical path actually work so bypass becomes the genuine exception again.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Bare `bd close <id>` succeeds when any of the 5 matchers fires; blocks otherwise with the explicit message
- /wrap-up's step 4 close succeeds without BD_CLOSE_FORCE in full mode
- Cosmetic regex bug fixed: --reason text no longer leaks into BEAD_IDS
- Test suite under lib/tests/ covers all 5 matchers + the regex scope
- loom-2xh closed or superseded once this lands

REFERENCES:
- Drawer (verbatim design + reasoning): drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628
- Locked decision #2 (2026-05-02): hundred_acre_woods/decisions WORKFLOW INFRASTRUCTURE PLAN
- Source trials: liza_base 2026-05-04 + 2026-05-05 BD_CLOSE_FORCE fallbacks
- Related (misdiagnosed): loom-2xh
close_reason: Real artifact verification + regex fix shipped (commits ab1a9de, f71b631; merge 8295102). 5-matcher kernel via python3+sqlite3 reads against palace/chroma.sqlite3 (FTS5) + palace/knowledge_graph.sqlite3 (triples). Bug A regex scoped to bd close arg list via shlex (--reason content cannot leak). Tests 30/30 GREEN, full suite 108/108. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_791d132361dd2875f5555e71. Self-test reported 3/5 matchers (✓✓✓✗✗) on bare bd close, the first canonical close path that actually verifies.

title: bead-lifecycle-shell: within-bead M-step parallelism nudge
description:
Extend the existing 'Decision: parallel vs sequential' section of skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md to cover within-bead parallelism in addition to across-bead. Today the section only addresses bead-level parallelism (when bd ready shows multiple unblocked beads); the new sub-section covers M-step parallelism within a single bead's variable middle.

The nudge: when an activity recipe's variable middle has 2+ independent M-steps (different files, no shared state, no sequential dependency), dispatch them via superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents with isolation: worktree. Cost-payoff threshold: parallelism wins when each task is non-trivial (more than a one-line edit) — for tiny edits, sequential is faster than dispatch overhead. Cross-reference the loom-tag worker-leak finding (drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a): parallel workers must use relative paths in prompts to stay sandboxed inside the worktree.

Cross-reference the creation-time CLAUDE.md heuristic (loom-XXXa) — the heuristics are paired.

~10 lines added. Umbrella: loom-csu.
close_reason: parallelism prompts shipped: CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic + bead-lifecycle-shell within-bead nudge

title: CLAUDE.md: bead-creation splitting heuristic (creation-time fan-out prompt)
description:
Add a 'Splitting heuristic at bead creation' rule to CLAUDE.md (loom's project-instructions Conventions section): when filing 2+ candidate items, ask whether they are independent (no shared files, no sequential dependency); if yes, file as sibling beads under an umbrella feature/epic; if no, file as one bead.

The heuristic is project-instructions level (CLAUDE.md), not a plugin skill, because the bead-creation skills (beadpowers:create-beads, beadpowers:brainstorming) are plugin-owned and not loom-editable. CLAUDE.md is loom-owned, loaded at session start, and applies whenever bd create is being authored.

Cross-reference the recipe-middle within-bead parallel nudge (loom-XXXb in bead-lifecycle-shell) — the heuristics are paired: this one prevents the slip at the source; the recipe-middle one catches what slips through.

~5 lines added. Umbrella: loom-csu.
close_reason: parallelism prompts shipped: CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic + bead-lifecycle-shell within-bead nudge

title: Parallelism prompts at bead-creation + recipe-middle (umbrella)
description:
Umbrella feature for two small adds that systematize the 'can this fan out?' question. Two miss-classes today: (a) at bead-creation, candidate items get filed as one bead with bullet-list subtasks instead of sibling beads under an epic — bd ready never surfaces them as parallel; (b) at recipe-middle, independent M-steps within a bead get done sequentially because no nudge points at superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents.

Children: loom-XXXa (CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic), loom-XXXb (bead-lifecycle-shell within-bead parallel nudge).

Loom-tag worker-leak finding (drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a) is per-dispatch, not per-prompt, so adding two prompts does not multiply that hazard. Each child cross-references the loom-tag finding so workers use relative paths.

Lineage: 2026-05-05 conversation following loom-c9z close — user observation that parallelism is under-prompted both at design-time and execution-time, with both worth catching.
close_reason: parallelism prompts shipped: CLAUDE.md splitting heuristic + bead-lifecycle-shell within-bead nudge

title: lib/docs-generated.sh: Signal 2a false-positives on documentation comments + echo strings
description:
The docs-generated detector's Signal 2a regex (looks for 'docs/' tokens in scripts/*.sh, Makefile, package.json, pyproject.toml) fires on documentation cross-references inside scripts that aren't actually building docs/. Two false-positive sources observed in loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05:

- bootstrap_liza_home.sh L110 — comment: '# Subdirectories per the v2 design (docs/v2-design.md...)'
- bootstrap_liza_home.sh L520 — echo string: '(See ${LIZA_BASE}/docs/setup.md...)'

Neither is a build target. The detector treats them as evidence that docs/ is generated, which would cause /audit-project AND /docs-scaffold to refuse on this project. Both customer trials (loom-b6o, loom-wxo) involved generated-OR-not-generated decisions; loom-b6o was true-positive (docs/ was actually built by scripts/build-docs.sh) but loom-wxo was false-positive (docs/ is hand-written; the bootstrap script just CITES doc paths in messages).

The detector spec already says it 'accepts some false-positives in exchange for simplicity' — but that simplicity-tradeoff was set before customer trials proved how often it fires. With 1-of-2 trials hitting an FP, the heuristic is too loose.

Required fix: tighten Signal 2a to require a write context. Reject matches where:
- Match is inside a shell comment (line starts with optional whitespace + #)
- Match is inside an echo / printf string literal that doesn't contain 'cp', 'mkdir', or '>'
- Match is in a docstring or comment in package.json/pyproject.toml

Accept matches where:
- 'docs/' appears as a target of cp/mkdir/install (left-of- the path) in a shell line
- 'docs/' appears as a Make target (right-side of ':' in Makefile rule)
- 'docs/' appears as a build script target in package.json scripts

Severity: P2 — the detector is the foundation of loom-qp0 + loom-3hb (both P2 fixes). An FP-prone detector pushes the false-positive rate of those skills up. With only 2 trials we already see one FP; future trials will hit more.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions, friction Signal 2a section). Trial: loom-wxo. Tests: lib/tests/docs-generated.test.sh — should add FP cases for shell comments and echo strings.

Related:
- loom-qp0 (audit-project generated-docs detection — uses this detector)
- loom-3hb (docs-scaffold generated-docs refusal — uses this detector)
- A symmetric P4 follow-up could narrow package.json scope-2c (already noted in Wave 1 close-out drawer)
close_reason: Signal 2a tightened: skip comment-only lines and echo/printf without write operators. 4 new RED→GREEN tests + 1 echo-redirect positive guard. Full lib/tests/ suite (78/78) clean. Merged --no-ff to main as 28338cd.

title: audit-project: drop _→- normalization in wing auto-detect; use filesystem basename verbatim
description:
The audit-project skill's auto-detect normalizes filesystem project names from snake_case to kebab-case (e.g., liza_base → liza-base, tla_puzzles → tla-puzzles) when resolving the MemPalace wing slug. But the MemPalace's de-facto convention is snake_case (loom, hundred_acre_woods, tla_puzzles, liza_base, liza). The normalization invents wing slugs that don't match the actual convention, producing a [WING WARN] every time:

  [WING WARN] auto-detected wing may not be canonical
    resolved:   liza-base (0 drawers, basename auto-detect from liza_base → _→-)
    variants:   liza_base (105 drawers), wing_liza_base_session_assistant (9 drawers)
    suggested:  re-run with --wing liza_base

This WARN fires on EVERY audit of liza_base. Same pattern hit tla-puzzles in loom-b6o (tla-puzzles filesystem, tla_puzzles canonical wing with 197 drawers). The normalization is the bug, not the naming.

Fix: drop the _→- normalization in auto-detect. Use filesystem basename verbatim. The wing-variant detection logic (loom-p1l) still fires correctly when there ARE genuine variants — it just won't fire when filesystem-name == canonical-wing-slug, which is the common case.

Likely location: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md Step 1 (wing resolution) + agents/project-onboarder.md (the actual auto-detect impl).

Follow-up to verify: re-running /audit-project against tla-puzzles after this fix should produce no [WING WARN] (since tla_puzzles wing matches tla_puzzles filesystem). Re-running against liza_base should likewise produce no WARN.

Known edge cases that should still WARN (to preserve loom-p1l behavior):
- filesystem name with REAL non-slug chars (spaces, capitalization that doesn't match) → still WARN with the normalization-applied alternatives
- multiple actually-similar wings (e.g., 'liza_base' filesystem with both 'liza_base' and 'wing_liza_base_session_assistant' wings) → still WARN about the multiplicity, just not invent a third nonexistent slug

Severity: P2 — the WARN appears on every audit; users have to either pass --wing explicitly every time or accept the noise. Trivial fix; high recurring annoyance.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_cb8647655ba0d62a257c96ef (loom/decisions, wing-naming friction surfaced 2026-05-05). Trial: loom-wxo (and historically loom-b6o).
close_reason: dropped _→- normalization in audit-project wing auto-detect; use filesystem basename verbatim

title: audit-project: AUTOFIX bypassed per-item approval gating in --dangerously-skip-permissions session
description:
First end-to-end exercise of /audit-project --apply-onboarding (Wave 2 / loom-a29) shows the per-item approval contract is broken silently.

Observed in loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04. The audit transcript shows:

  **Item 1:** Create `.claude/workflow.json` with `{"v":1,"mode":"full"}`. Apply suggested fix? (yes / skip / edit)
  Done — `.claude/workflow.json` written.

The 'Done — written' line appears with no user turn between the prompt and the application. Same pattern for Item 2 (.gitignore append) and Item 3 (.claude/rules/tests.md draft + write).

Possible causes (need investigation):
(a) The session was running with --dangerously-skip-permissions, which bypasses Claude Code's tool-call permission prompts. The audit-project skill may be (incorrectly) treating skipped permission prompts as user-approval, then auto-answering its own 'yes/skip/edit' question.
(b) The skill's per-item-approval flow has a bug where the prompt-and-apply happen in the same model turn, with no genuine pause for user input.
(c) The new --apply-onboarding flag was applied without --apply-onboarding being explicitly set, OR the skill defaults to apply-all when the flag is absent.

Required investigation:
1. Reproduce the bypass in a NON-skip-permissions session — does the per-item gate work there?
2. Check the skill's text for any 'when --dangerously-skip-permissions, treat as auto-yes' logic.
3. Determine whether the user explicitly passed --apply-onboarding (they didn't say so in the loom-side conversation; the parallel session may have inferred it).

Required fix (after diagnosis):
- The per-item contract MUST be respected. AUTOFIX items should still pause for explicit user 'yes' even with --dangerously-skip-permissions (which is about TOOL permissions, not USER approval). The two are different gates.
- If --apply-onboarding was implicitly enabled, that's a separate bug — the flag should be explicit.

Severity: P2 because the new flags are now untrusted until this is fixed. Future trials may apply changes the user didn't sanction.

Source: loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04. Trial drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_1101f772a4ac7660a0f45aa1 (Wave 2 close-out, where the per-item contract is documented). Adoption drawer in liza_base: drawer_liza_base_decisions_aaad51050ba624209965e671.
close_reason: Closed

title: docs-scaffold + customer-trial protocol: handle generated docs/ (skip-or-warn) and update step 5 conditionality
description:
Two-part fix needed. Discovered during loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04 when the trial's step 5 ('Run /docs-scaffold') was implicitly skipped because docs/ in tla-puzzles is generated by scripts/build-docs.sh (gitignored, regenerated from README.md on every build). Running scaffold against generated docs/ would write template files that the next build cycle erases — silent breakage.

Part 1 — /docs-scaffold should detect generated docs/:
- Detect via .gitignore entry for docs/, OR docs/* referenced as a build target in any scripts/*.sh, Makefile, package.json scripts, etc.
- On detection, refuse to run with [SCAFFOLD SKIP][GENERATED] message pointing at the build script and explaining why.
- This shares the detection logic from loom-qp0 (audit-project's same problem); should probably be extracted into a shared helper.

Part 2 — customer-trial activity recipe (the loom-b6o pattern documented in loom/decisions km8.6 closing drawer) needs step 5 to be conditional:
- Currently step 5 says 'Run /docs-scaffold — capture friction at M1-M6, with attention to the partial-Diataxis docs/ shape.'
- Should be: 'Run /docs-scaffold UNLESS docs/ is generated (in which case scaffold itself should refuse — capture that as the only friction signal for this step).'
- Update the trial protocol drawer + the activity recipe (when it's written for trial-style customer onboarding).

Severity: P2 — correctness. Trial protocol's universality assumption was wrong; without the conditional, future trials of generated-docs projects either silently break the project or get manually skipped (as happened here).

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_dfb5ece53785610c83ca6619 (loom/decisions, friction signal G). Trial: loom-b6o.

Related:
- loom-qp0 (audit-project generated-docs detection) — same root detection logic; consider implementing the helper once and using it from both skills.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: audit-project: bd-hooks check uses wrong path (core.hooksPath, not .git/hooks/)
description:
The /audit-project skill checks for '.git/hooks/pre-commit' to determine whether bd hooks are installed. False positive: 'bd hooks install' actually writes to '.beads/hooks/' and sets 'git config core.hooksPath=.beads/hooks'. So the audit reports MISS even when hooks ARE installed correctly via the canonical bd path.

Discovered during loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04. The parallel session ran 'bd hooks install' as one of the audit's recommended onboarding fixes, then verified — at which point it noticed bd's own install layout, and that the audit's check would have continued to report MISS even after install.

Required fix: replace the '.git/hooks/pre-commit existence' check with one of:
(a) Read 'git config core.hooksPath' and check '<that-dir>/pre-commit' exists.
(b) Check both locations: '.git/hooks/pre-commit' OR '.beads/hooks/pre-commit' (and any future bd hook dirs).
(c) Run 'bd doctor' (or whatever bd's self-check is) and parse its hook-install status.

Option (a) is the most general (works for any project setting core.hooksPath, not just bd). Option (c) is the most accurate (delegates to bd's own knowledge). Option (a) is recommended unless bd's self-check is stable + scripted-friendly.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions). Trial: loom-b6o.

Severity: P2 — false positive on a check that drives onboarding behavior. Users who already have hooks correctly installed via canonical bd path will be told to install them again, possibly leading to redundant or conflicting setups.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: audit-project: detect generated docs/ (gitignored or built by script) and redirect drift checks to source
description:
The /audit-project skill assumed docs/ was hand-written in tla-puzzles, but docs/ is gitignored (.gitignore:28) and fully generated by scripts/build-docs.sh (line 19: 'cp README.md "$DOCS/index.md"'). Result: skill reported [DOC FIX] cardinality drift in docs/index.md and suggested edits there; the parallel Claude session applied those edits, then discovered they will be overwritten on next build. README.md was the actual source-of-truth.

This is a correctness bug, not ergonomics — in any project that generates docs, the skill points the user at the artifact instead of the source. The fix-suggestion is wrong by design until detection lands.

Required behavior:
1. Detect generated docs/ via either signal:
   (a) docs/ matches a .gitignore entry
   (b) docs/index.md (or other docs/ files) referenced as cp/build target in any scripts/*.sh, Makefile, package.json scripts, etc.
2. On detection, either:
   (a) Skip the docs/ drift check entirely with [DOC SKIP][GENERATED] tag and a note pointing at the build script
   (b) Walk the build chain back to source files and report drift there (advanced — needs build-script parsing)
   (c) Hybrid: skip docs/ drift, but report drift in known root-level docs (README.md, etc.) — this is the related case for loom-ojn (MIRROR scope decision)
3. Minimum-viable fix: option (a) — just skip and warn. Don't report drift in artifact files.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions). Discovered during loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04, when the parallel session caught the generation chain only after applying fixes.

Severity: P2 not P3 because skill is *actively wrong* in this case, not just sub-optimal.

Related:
- loom-ojn (MIRROR tag scope) — overlaps because this project is exactly the case where 'README is source, docs/ mirrors README' makes README-drift checks the right thing to do.
- loom-8hg (--apply-trivial) — this bug would have caused --apply-trivial to silently corrupt the wrong file.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: docs-scaffold: drop templates/diataxis/README.md from apply set (would overwrite project README)
description:
The bash recipe documented in templates/diataxis/README.md is:

```bash
cp -r templates/diataxis/. "$TARGET/"
```

This copies EVERY file under templates/diataxis/ — including the templates' own README.md, which lands at `<target>/README.md` and would **silently overwrite the project's own README.md** (data loss).

The skill SKILL.md M5/M6 spec doesn't explicitly drop this file from the apply set, so a strict implementation following the documented recipe will produce data loss for any target project that has a README.md at root.

HAW trial caught this only because HAW happens to lack a root README.md. A target project WITH a README would have lost it.

Fix:
- skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md M5: explicitly exclude templates/diataxis/README.md from the apply set ("the templates' own README is for loom maintainers, not the target project").
- templates/diataxis/README.md substitution-mechanism section: change the recipe to either (a) explicitly omit README.md, or (b) move README.md out of templates/diataxis/ entirely (e.g. templates/diataxis-README.md as a sibling) so cp -r doesn't pick it up.

Either fix works; (b) is more bulletproof since it makes the recipe correct as documented.

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M5 phase. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_9bebc79fba4116c3a8b2489a (will supplement on close).
acceptance_criteria: docs-scaffold cannot land templates' own README.md into a target project; verified by running scaffold against a project that has its own README.md (it survives unchanged); templates/diataxis/README.md content/location updated so the documented bash recipe is correct as-written
close_reason: Moved templates/diataxis/README.md → templates/diataxis-README.md (out of the templates dir entirely). The documented cp -r recipe is now correct as-written and cannot overwrite a target's own README.md. Updated 4 inbound references (SKILL.md ×3, how-to ×2 — replace_all) and the README's own self-description. lib/tests/diataxis-template.test.sh still passes (9/9, mkdocs build --strict).

title: audit-project: WARN when basename-variant MemPalace wings exist (-/_ collision)
description:
