Wing auto-detect picks the basename-derived slug, but a project's canonical wing may use a different separator (e.g. underscore vs hyphen) due to historical naming.

HAW trial: root `/home/frank/Working/hundred-acre-woods` → auto-detected wing `hundred-acre-woods` (3 drawers) → silently scoped drift checks to the smaller wing. The canonical wing has 13 drawers under `hundred_acre_woods`. The project-onboarder check 5 noticed 'two parallel wings present' but framed it PASS (informational), not WARN.

Silent-wrong is worse than loud-missing. The user has no signal that the docs-drift check (Check 5 in particular) is going to miss most of the prior decision history.

Fix: when audit-project resolves the wing AND mempalace_list_wings shows >1 wing whose slug is a basename-variant of the resolved one (`-` ↔ `_`, plural ↔ singular, snake_case ↔ kebab-case), emit a WARN with: 'did you mean --wing <other>? (N drawers) vs <auto> (M drawers)'. Surface this BEFORE running Step 3 docs-drift, so the user can rerun with the right --wing.

Source: loom-km8.6 HAW first-customer trial M1 audit phase. Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_f2e3a5c273214cbf335a8918 §F2 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-04).
acceptance_criteria: audit-project Step 1 emits a WARN-level line when basename-variant wings exist with non-trivial drawer counts; the WARN names both wings and their drawer counts; the WARN appears before the project-onboarder dispatch and before Step 3
close_reason: Added Step 1b 'wing-variant warning' to audit-project SKILL.md: when --wing is auto-detected (not explicit), enumerate basename-variant siblings (-/_, plural/singular, snake/kebab) and emit a [WING WARN] BEFORE Step 2 if any sibling has more drawers. Shape: 'resolved <W> (M drawers) | variants <S>(N drawers) | suggested --wing <largest>'. Non-blocking; user owns the call. Combined report now has Pre-flight warnings section.

title: Agent tool with isolation:worktree may write edits to main repo paths instead of worktree
description:
## Symptom

The km8.4 worker subagent (dispatched via `Agent` tool with `isolation: "worktree"`) reported in its return summary that it had edited files at absolute paths under the main repo:

- `/home/frank/repos/loom/skills/audit-project/SKILL.md`
- `/home/frank/repos/loom/commands/audit-project.md`

(no worktree prefix in the agent's reported paths).

After the agent returned, the worktree at `/home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0e34a73/` showed only a staged `.beads/issues.jsonl` change — no actual edits to the audit-project files. Meanwhile, the **main repo working tree** showed both `commands/audit-project.md` and `skills/audit-project/SKILL.md` modified with the agent's content.

The agent's content was correct and well-formed; only the location was wrong.

## Evidence

- 2026-05-04 session shipping loom-km8.{2,3,4} in parallel
- Decision drawer: `drawer_loom_decisions_9bd804d6326ad49467f4e7fe` (loom/decisions, "LOOM-KM8 PHASE 2 — PARALLEL DISPATCH OF T2/T3/T4")
- KG: `Agent-tool-with-isolation-worktree → may_write_to → main-repo-paths-instead-of-worktree` (2026-05-04)
- km8.2 and km8.3 worker agents (dispatched in the same message, same `isolation: "worktree"` parameter, similar prompts) wrote to their **worktree** paths correctly. The bug was specific to km8.4's run, which suggests it's intermittent rather than systematic.

## Hypotheses to investigate

1. **Agent used cwd-relative paths.** The Edit tool resolves relative paths against the agent's pwd. If the agent's pwd was the main repo (not the worktree) and it called Edit with relative paths like `commands/audit-project.md`, the writes would land in main.
2. **Edit tool path resolution.** Even with absolute paths, something about how worktree paths resolve under `.claude/worktrees/` (which is inside the main repo's directory tree) could route writes to the main repo's working tree instead of the worktree's.
3. **Agent wrote absolute paths but stripped/rewrote them.** The agent's summary contains absolute paths without the worktree prefix — possibly the agent itself never used the worktree paths because its tools didn't surface that it was in a worktree.
4. **Per-tool isolation gap.** `isolation: "worktree"` may set up the git worktree but not change the agent's tool cwd or path-resolution context.

## Difference from working agents (km8.2 + km8.3)

- km8.2 worker created NEW files (no pre-existing main copy to clobber). Its summary explicitly named worktree paths (`/home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/worktrees/agent-ad662d4f/commands/docs-scaffold.md`).
- km8.3 worker EDITED an existing tracked file (`agents/project-onboarder.md`) — same shape as km8.4. Its summary named the worktree path. No leak to main.

So the difference isn't "new file vs. edited file" or "single file vs. multiple files." km8.3 and km8.4 are structurally similar; only one leaked to main.

## Repro plan

1. Dispatch a single agent with `isolation: "worktree"` and a prompt that says "edit `<absolute-path-to-tracked-file-in-main-repo>`" (using the main path explicitly).
2. Compare against: prompt that says "edit `<file>` in your worktree" (worktree-relative or worktree-absolute).
3. Compare against: prompt that names no path at all and lets the agent discover the file via Read.
4. In each case, observe where the Edit lands.

## Acceptance

- Document the actual path-resolution behavior for `Agent` + `isolation: "worktree"` + Edit tool.
- If the bug is in user-facing behavior, file a follow-up to either (a) make worker prompts always use worktree-prefixed absolute paths, or (b) have the harness sandbox the worker's filesystem to its worktree.
- Update the loom dispatching-parallel-agents how-to (if any) with the verified safe pattern.

## Out of scope

- Fixing the underlying tool/harness behavior (this is a Claude Code / Agent SDK issue, not loom-internal). Loom can document a workaround but can't patch the tool.
close_reason: research finding filed: drawer_loom_decisions_df73c725b47dd67832935e3a; CLAUDE.md updated with safe-pattern (relative paths in dispatch prompts, post-dispatch git diff --stat in both trees)

title: bd pre-commit hook re-export defeats loom-22h workaround when subagents work in worktrees
description:
loom-22h closed 2026-05-04 with a defensive workaround: `/issues.jsonl` in .gitignore (root-anchored) + CLAUDE.md 'Don't' note recommending main-path commits over worktree-mode commits. Today's session (loom-9z1.{8,9} parallel dispatch) demonstrated the workaround is insufficient when subagents work in worktrees.

**Evidence (2026-05-04):**

1. **loom-9z1.8 worker** — bd pre-commit hook staged stray `issues.jsonl` at worktree root on the first two commit attempts. Worker bypassed via `git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null commit`. Final commit 9c80a9c clean. Worker's report explicitly noted loom-22h still active.

2. **loom-9z1.9 worker** — bd pre-commit hook staged stray `issues.jsonl`. Worker tried `git rm --cached issues.jsonl` + `git commit --amend`; the hook re-added the file on amend. Worker did NOT skip hooks (CLAUDE.md forbids `--no-verify` without explicit user approval). Commit c5fa4a2 shipped to branch with the stray file. Dispatcher had to clean at merge: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff` then `git rm --cached issues.jsonl && rm issues.jsonl` before the merge commit (6ee0e62 / linearized as 6f3e0cb).

**Why the existing workaround fails:**

- `.gitignore` doesn't help once the file is tracked. The hook re-stages on every commit, so even after `git rm --cached`, the next commit re-adds it.
- CLAUDE.md 'commit from main' advice is human-targeted prose. Subagents dispatched into worktrees don't reliably internalize it — they see the natural workflow as 'commit where I'm working.'
- The `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` bypass works but is undocumented. One worker discovered it independently; the other didn't and had to ship the broken commit for the dispatcher to fix.

**Root cause (unchanged from loom-22h):** bd's pre-commit hook (`bd hooks run pre-commit` invoked from `.beads/hooks/pre-commit`) exports `issues.jsonl` to the worktree root when commit happens inside a git worktree, instead of (or in addition to) the canonical `.beads/issues.jsonl`. The export message lies about the path it's writing to.

**Proposed fix shapes (to triage during work):**

- **A. Upstream fix in bd** — patch `bd hooks run pre-commit` to resolve the canonical export path via `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` (returns the main repo's .git directory even from a worktree) and write only to `<common-dir>/../.beads/issues.jsonl`. This is the right fix but requires a bd CLI change.
- **B. Loom-side wrapper hook** — replace bd's pre-commit installer with a wrapper that detects worktree mode (`git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree` + path check) and skips the export there entirely, deferring it to the main-repo commit. Cost: divergence from upstream bd hook contract.
- **C. Per-worktree git config disable** — install.sh / worktree-creation helper sets `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` (or a no-op directory) on the worktree's local git config so the hook never fires from worktrees. Cost: hides the hook entirely from worktrees, which may surprise users who do want hook behavior there.
- **D. Recipe-side defense** — bake the `core.hooksPath=/dev/null` bypass into bead-lifecycle-shell phase C (commit step) when `stage=worktree`, with a clear comment explaining the workaround. Cost: every recipe inherits a workaround for a bug that should be fixed at the source. Has the virtue of being immediate and not requiring upstream coordination.

**Default recommendation pending brainstorm:** A is the right long-term fix; D is the right immediate fix because it propagates via the shell to every recipe and unblocks today's dispatch pattern. B and C are intermediate options that may not be worth the complexity if D ships.

**Acceptance:**
- A subagent dispatched into a worktree can commit normally without the bd hook staging stray `issues.jsonl`. Verify by running the loom-9z1.{8,9}-style dispatch pattern after the fix.
- The dispatcher does not need to merge-clean stray `issues.jsonl` from worktree branches.
- Existing CLAUDE.md 'commit from main' note can either stay (belt-and-suspenders) or be removed (if the fix is robust enough to make the advice unnecessary).

**Out of scope:** changing the canonical export path; only the worktree-context re-export bug.

**Recipe shape:** bugfix-a-bead. Depends on: nothing structural — loom-22h is closed and superseded-in-spirit by this bead but not formally.
close_reason: Closed

title: Set up MkDocs Material + GitHub Pages deploy + Diataxis skeleton
description:
Goal: Foundation for the Diataxis docs site. Get the publishing pipeline working end-to-end against placeholder content; subsequent migration beads (A2-A5) fill the quadrants.

Files: mkdocs.yml, requirements.txt (build env), .github/workflows/docs.yml, docs/index.md (4-quadrant landing), docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md (placeholder quadrant landings).

Steps:
1. Author mkdocs.yml with Material theme + mkdocs-include-markdown plugin (glob auto-discovery enabled). Enable search, dark mode, navigation tabs.
2. requirements.txt: mkdocs-material + mkdocs-include-markdown-plugin pinned to current versions.
3. GitHub Actions workflow: build on push to main, deploy to gh-pages branch.
4. Skeleton landing pages: root index.md orients readers by intent (new? → Tutorials / solving a problem? → How-to / looking up a fact? → Reference / want to understand why? → Explanation), each quadrant index.md is a stub TOC.
5. Configure repo Pages settings: source = gh-pages branch, custom domain optional.

Acceptance:
- mkdocs build succeeds locally.
- GH Actions deploys on push to main.
- Published page resolves; the 4-quadrant nav is navigable.
- Stub landing pages render without broken links.

Diataxis-discipline note (from R1 drawer at claim time): ensure index.md orientation copy follows the 4-quadrant intent pattern Daniele Procida recommends (NOT a hierarchical product-feature nav).

Recipe shape: feature-a-bead.
close_reason: MkDocs Material + GH Pages deploy + Diataxis 4-quadrant skeleton landed (commit 4f3cd06; merged 30a5b5c). All four quadrants land with at-least-one real page per R1 C3 (no empty stubs): Tutorials = 2 walkthroughs git-mv'd; How-to = install.md from README; Reference = session-startup auto-included via mkdocs-include-markdown demonstrating C1; Explanation = §1 mental-model lifted from manual.md. mkdocs build --strict exits 0. Unblocks loom-9z1.3-.6 (A2-A5 quadrant migrations), loom-9z1.8 (B1 doc-surface check), loom-9z1.9 (/audit-project --check=docs). manual.md retire deferred to A6 (loom-9z1.7).

title: Diataxis: principles, fail points, loom-specific accommodations (research)
description:
Goal: Establish a locked understanding of Diataxis (the documentation framework) BEFORE any restructure work begins. Every implementation/migration bead in this epic depends on this drawer.

Define the question:
- What does each Diataxis quadrant (Tutorials, How-to, Reference, Explanation) actually require — voice, audience, depth, what to keep OUT?
- Where do real-world Diataxis adoptions fail? (Common patterns to validate: tutorial creep into how-to; reference padding into explanation; four-quadrant nav becoming a navigation trap; primitive-as-reference duplication; getting-started tutorial bloat.)
- What loom-specific accommodations do we need? (Markdown-heavy infra repo where SKILLs are already reference-shaped; canonical-skills with mkdocs-include-markdown glob; a single-author meta-project with no external users yet but designed to be picked up.)

Steps:
1. Search palace + bd memories for prior Diataxis art (probably none).
2. Fetch authoritative external docs: diataxis.fr (canonical site by Daniele Procida); published critiques + case studies; any blog posts about Diataxis fail modes.
3. Synthesize.

Output: decision drawer in loom/decisions naming
(a) discipline rules per quadrant (voice / audience / what to include / what to keep OUT),
(b) documented fail patterns + how to avoid them,
(c) loom-specific accommodations (canonical-skills, glob auto-discovery, single-author dynamics).

Recipe shape: research-a-bead. Drawer is the deliverable.

Blocks: A1, D1 (which transitively blocks the rest of the epic).
close_reason: R1 research output filed in loom/decisions (drawer DIATAXIS DISCIPLINE — R1 RESEARCH OUTPUT). Synthesized 4-quadrant verbatim-Procida discipline rules + 10 fail-pattern catalog (F1-F10) + 6 loom accommodations (C1-C6). Unblocks A1 (loom-9z1.2), A2-A5, B1, C1, D1.

title: Diataxis docs restructure: MkDocs Material + canonical-skills + drift defenses
description:
Rebuild loom's docs as a Diataxis-shaped MkDocs Material site published via GitHub Pages. Skills/commands/agents/hooks remain canonical (single source of truth); docs site curates and links via mkdocs-include-markdown glob auto-discovery. Process integration (recipe phase B1 doc-surface check) + drift detection (/audit-project --check=docs) defend the alignment.

Phase 1 (this epic): loom's own docs.
Phase 2 (D1's plan + future child beads): extract pattern as template loom-managed projects can adopt via /audit-project.

Locked decisions captured in conversation 2026-05-03:
- Scope: Option 3 (loom first, then template-ize)
- Tooling: MkDocs Material + GitHub Pages
- Source-of-truth: option 1 (skills canonical, docs link via include-markdown)
- Process: B1 doc-surface check in bead-lifecycle-shell
- Drift detection: extend /audit-project with --check=docs (system + beads + MemPalace > docs precedence)
- Glob auto-discovery for primitive includes (no nav.yml maintenance)
- 10 beads under one epic

The full design will be filed as a decision drawer at R1's close + supplemented at D1's close. The child beads' descriptions will be enriched with detailed per-page content specs at claim time, sourced from R1's drawer.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Drop disable-model-invocation flag from activity recipe SKILL.mds + shell
description: The six activity recipe SKILL.mds (bugfix/feature/refactor/research/cleanup/docs-a-bead) and bead-lifecycle-shell carry 'disable-model-invocation: true' in frontmatter. This makes the Skill tool refuse to load them ('cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation'), which blocks the loom-1ab router from dispatching to recipes via Skill(<recipe>) — the canonical handoff path. The existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands also say 'Invoke the X skill' and would hit the same wall (untested). FIX: remove 'disable-model-invocation: true' from frontmatter of all 7 SKILL.mds. Recipes are explicitly invoked via slash command (or via the router); the flag was belt-and-suspenders against accidental auto-fire. Descriptions are already narrow and workflow-mode off/light gating provides additional defense. ACCEPTANCE: 7 SKILL.mds edited; Skill(<any-recipe>) loads cleanly; existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands work end-to-end. See loom/decisions drawer 0e40c06 (2026-05-03).
close_reason: Removed disable-model-invocation:true from 6 recipe SKILL.mds + bead-lifecycle-shell. Updated body text + manual.md. Unblocks loom-1ab router. commit c44d7cb.

title: loom: /wrap-up final step — deploy via install.sh when in loom checkout
description: When working on loom, the /wrap-up command should auto-detect the loom checkout (presence of install.sh + settings.snippet.json + skills/ + commands/ at pwd root) and run a deploy step as its final action. The deploy step needs to (a) mkdir any missing ~/.claude/skills/<new>/ parent dirs, (b) rm dangling symlinks owned by loom (target points into $LOOM_ROOT but file no longer exists), then (c) ./install.sh. This compensates for two install.sh gaps documented today in MemPalace drawer 'install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND' (loom/decisions, 2026-05-03). Until install.sh itself absorbs the fix (separate bead, P3), this addendum keeps loom self-deploying after every wrap-up. Edit only commands/wrap-up.md; the loom-detection check guards the step from firing in non-loom projects.
close_reason: Added section 6 to commands/wrap-up.md: detects loom checkout, mkdir's missing skill subdirs, prunes dangling loom-owned symlinks, runs ./install.sh. Section is no-op in non-loom projects. Cross-refs the install.sh gap drawer for lineage. Will collapse to plain ./install.sh once install.sh fix lands (separate bead).

title: v2: router — /working-a-bead becomes selector that dispatches by bead.type
description: commands/working-a-bead.md becomes a router. Logic: takes bead-id (optional --recipe=<name> override), runs bd show, scores against the 6 recipes by bead.type field + description heuristics. Routes to the matching recipe. On ambiguity (bead matches 2+ recipes plausibly): list ambiguous candidates with one-line 'because' for each and prompt user to pick or use --recipe=<name> override (Frank's locked decision 6, 2026-05-02). Default routing: type=bug → bugfix; type=feature → feature; type=epic → planning (TBD); type=task → check description keywords (refactor/cleanup/docs/research) with fallback to bugfix.
close_reason: Router shipped at commands/working-a-bead.md. Dispatches by bead.type with keyword scoring for tasks, ambiguity prompt on ties, --recipe override, epic guard. Skill(<recipe>) dispatch enabled by loom-7z1. End-to-end smoke test deferred to next session. commit da939e6.

title: v2: research-a-bead recipe
description: skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: define the question → search prior art (mempalace_search + kg_query + bd memories + diary_read) → fetch authoritative docs (context7 if library; web if needed) → synthesize → file findings as drawer + KG triples + optional follow-up beads. No code, no worktree (usually). Captures the discipline that was implicit in the deploy-day MemPalace search habit.
close_reason: research-a-bead recipe authored; sibling to bugfix-a-bead under epic loom-0y6

title: v2: feature-a-bead recipe
description: skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: brainstorm (superpowers/beadpowers) → writing-plans (multi-task) → TDD on NEW behavior (the contract) → integration test → bug-class equivalent for negative cases. Differs from bugfix: no symptom to reproduce; the RED test pins desired behavior, not failure. References the shared bead-lifecycle-shell for claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: refactor-a-bead recipe
description: skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify scope → write characterization tests if missing (pin current behavior) → refactor → verify behavior preserved (all original tests pass) → close. Differs from bugfix: TDD inverted — tests come BEFORE the refactor and must KEEP passing. No new behavior; structural change only.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: rename working-a-bead → bugfix-a-bead + retitle for activity shape
description: Today working-a-bead is bug-shaped (forged during HAW deploy-day cleanup). Once the activity recipes land it should be one of N recipes the router selects among. Rename the skill directory + filename + frontmatter name, update internal references in router + session-startup hand-off + manual + walkthrough. Shell out the bug-specific middle (reproduce → RED → minimal GREEN → bug-class coverage → enshrined-test sweep) — the shared bead-lifecycle-shell skill already owns claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.
close_reason: Renamed skills/working-a-bead/ → skills/bugfix-a-bead/ + commands/working-a-bead.md → commands/bugfix-a-bead.md. SKILL.md rewritten as slim variable middle (M1-M5) with phases A/B/C/D delegated to bead-lifecycle-shell. All refs swept across install/uninstall/README/CLAUDE/manual/walkthrough/session-startup/shell/agents. workflow.json set to light. Commit bc841d7 merged to main as 47-line diff. Unblocks loom-1ab router and the 5 sibling recipes.

title: v2: activity-shaped recipes + router (continued from HAW bng)
description: Continuation of the v2 work originally tracked in HAW epic hundred-acre-woods-bng. The shared bead-lifecycle-shell skill (was 7o9) shipped before migration. Remaining: 5 activity recipes (feature, refactor, research, cleanup, docs) + the /working-a-bead router that selects among them by bead.type/heuristics + final pass on docs/manual.md and docs/walkthrough.md to reflect the multi-recipe shape. Design + locked decisions captured in MemPalace drawer 'RECIPE SHAPES — ACTIVITY MATRIX' (hundred_acre_woods/decisions, 2026-05-02) — see also LOOM PROVENANCE drawer in loom/decisions wing for the full inheritance chain. Frank's locked decisions: 6 recipes (bug already exists as bugfix-shaped working-a-bead; rename pending), shared shell as separate skill (shipped), router goes straight to selector pattern not phased intro, full schema for state file with stage as first-class.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: mempalace_update_drawer has no append/patch — precipitate-per-round forces full-drawer rewrites
description:
Surfaced by the /explore dogfood (loom-ld1q.5, drawer 9f467b06 + exploration 0bc491f8). The /explore and /design-a-cycle skills prescribe 'precipitate firm findings into the drawer after each round / each locked decision' — but mempalace_update_drawer REPLACES full content (no append/patch API). One exploration forced ~4 full-drawer rewrites (resending ~3KB each) just to add a finding or flip a STATE HEADER field. This is real ergonomic friction that discourages the per-touch cadence the skills depend on, and is itself an instance of the capture-tax the exploration flagged (substrate-consolidation design cycle drawer_loom_decisions_14f08e6d6b4c40f0a63fb9cf, Move-3 budgeting / C4 capture-fan-out).

Options: (a) loom-side helper that reads-mod-writes a drawer section (append to ## Findings / patch a STATE HEADER field) over the existing get+update; (b) upstream mempalace feature request for an append/patch tool; (c) accept + document the full-rewrite cadence. 
Files: (investigate — likely a scripts/ helper or an upstream request; confirm mempalace MCP surface first)
Relates-to: substrate-consolidation Move-3 / [CLARIFICATION C4]; capture-tax.

title: deep-research: peer-reviewed literature included by default when loom invokes it
description:
Goal (Frank): 'deep research should in most cases include peer-reviewed literature.' This is the GENERAL preference beyond /explore (which already owns tier-4 inclusion in its own brief, T2). NOTE: deep-research appears to be a BUILT-IN skill (no loom-editable file found under ~/.claude), so the lever is loom-side.
Files: (investigate — likely a loom invocation convention/wrapper in docs or CLAUDE.md, or an upstream request; confirm deep-research editability first)
Steps: 1. Locate deep-research skill source + determine loom-editability. 2. If loom-editable: make peer-reviewed inclusion the default. Else: document the loom-side invocation convention (pass peer-review instruction) and/or file upstream via upstream-a-bead.
Acceptance: peer-reviewed inclusion is the default behavior when loom invokes deep-research, by whatever lever fits.
Separable; soft-dep for /explore tier-4 (which degrades gracefully without it).

title: Verify/correct the '29 MCP tools' count in docs/reference/mempalace-mcp.md against the live server
description: The Diataxis docs audit (2026-06-08) flagged docs/reference/mempalace-mcp.md's '29 MCP tools' claim as UNVERIFIED against the live MemPalace MCP server (the audit scope did not include re-counting). The server appears to expose more than 29 tools (status/kg_*/diary_*/search/add_drawer/update_drawer/tag/tunnel/traverse/...). Count the live mempalace_* tools and update the page's number + catalogue if stale. Low priority docs hygiene. Files: docs/reference/mempalace-mcp.md

title: Configure bd preflight for loom (shell suite + mkdocs --strict) instead of bd's default Go-project template
description: bd preflight in loom prints a generic Go-project PR checklist (go test -tags... / golangci-lint / gofmt / vendorHash / version.go-vs-default.nix) — bd's default template, wrong for loom (a bash+markdown repo with no Go). Loom's actual PR-readiness gates are: the shell test suite (bash lib/tests/*.test.sh, 62 files) + mkdocs build --strict + bd lint/stale/orphans. Investigate where bd reads its preflight checklist (bd config / .beads/) and set a loom-appropriate checklist, OR document that loom's gates live elsewhere. Surfaced 2026-06-08 during /wrap-up. Files: .beads/ (bd preflight config; exact path TBD during investigation)

title: loom-mine-history: gated_count uses grep -c || echo 0 (0\n0 cost-preview bug on zero survivors)
description:
Surfaced by the loom-bn7.2 idiom sweep. lib/loom-mine-history.sh lines ~398 + ~406 compute gated_count via `grep -c . "$survivors" 2>/dev/null || echo 0`. Same bug class fixed for n_clusters/n_arcs/n_drafts in bn7.2: when survivors is empty (gate dropped everything), grep -c prints 0 AND exits 1, so || echo 0 APPENDS a second 0 -> gated_count="0\n0". The cost-preview line 'N harvested -> ${gated_count} gated' then splits across two lines.

Fix: drop the || echo 0; rely on grep -c always printing 0 on no-match; default via ${gated_count:-0} for the file-missing case. Mirrors the bn7.2 fix.
RED test: a fixture where ALL commits are junk (0 survivors) -> assert the cost-preview line 'harvested -> 0 gated' is ONE line (grep -E 'harvested.*-> 0 gated' matches).
Files: lib/loom-mine-history.sh, lib/tests/loom-mine-history.test.sh. Bug-shaped -> bugfix-a-bead.

title: install.sh: invocability assertion for shipped skills/commands (loom-k2g.7 canary friction)
description: Surfaced by loom-k2g.7: upstream-a-bead shipped (k2g.1-.6 merged) but was never installed - no ~/.claude/skills/upstream-a-bead symlink until ./install.sh was re-run. 'Shipped but not invocable' is a real gap the implementation waves missed. Add an install.sh --check (or a bead-lifecycle-shell phase C step) that verifies each skills/*/SKILL.md and commands/*.md has a live ~/.claude symlink, and/or runs ./install.sh automatically when a bead adds new skills/commands. Independent of the recipe-refinement bead (touches install.sh, not the SKILL). Source: loom-k2g.7 notes + drawer_loom_decisions_ffd73e9e7bb47f51daee9a72.

title: upstream-a-bead: prose-only sub-lane + trivial-PR inline escape (loom-7bn canary friction)
description:
Surfaced by the loom-k2g.7 canary driving loom-7bn through upstream-a-bead. Two coupled refinements to skills/upstream-a-bead/SKILL.md (shared file -> one bead):

1. PROSE-ONLY SUB-LANE: M3/M4 (RED/GREEN) assume the upstream has a native test command. beadpowers (and superpowers, loom-ki5's target) are pure-prose skill plugins with no test harness. Recipe Q4 'AGNOSTIC - run upstream's native test command' presumes one exists. Add explicit handling: when no test harness, M3/M4 collapse to a documented before/after (doc-presence grep) on a single doc commit; say so in the PR body's Verification section.

2. TRIVIAL-PR INLINE ESCAPE: recipe defaults M2-M5 to worker territory. For a small prose PR with no loom-side code, inline-central is correct (loom-csu within-bead threshold: sequential wins for <=~5-line edits). Add an escape mirroring the within-bead nudge.

3. (sub-note) The dispatched-agents pre-flight smoke battery (.claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md) assumes a loom-side .claude/worktrees worktree, not an external ~/.loom/upstream clone; if M3/M4 are ever dispatched for upstream work, there's no equivalent pre-flight for the clone. Note in the SKILL or cross-ref.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_ffd73e9e7bb47f51daee9a72 (canary checkpoint).

title: watch upstream pprice/beadpowers#2: create-beads independence heuristic
description: Watching for merge or rejection of https://github.com/pprice/beadpowers/pull/2 (issue https://github.com/pprice/beadpowers/issues/1). Work-bead: loom-7bn (filed via upstream-a-bead canary loom-k2g.7). Closes when /check-upstream-prs detects the PR merged (auto) or rejected (surface to user).

title: Canary trial: drive loom-7bn or loom-ki5 through upstream-a-bead recipe
description:
Goal: First real-customer trial of upstream-a-bead recipe after T1-T6 ship. Drive one of the in-flight upstream-PR beads (loom-7bn: beadpowers create-beads splitting-heuristic; OR loom-ki5: superpowers verification + debugging) through the new recipe end-to-end. Reveals friction the test-fixtures missed.

Files (will vary based on canary chosen):
- ~/.loom/upstream/<owner>/<repo>/ (auto-created by recipe at M2)
- /tmp/issue-<bead>.md + /tmp/pr-<bead>.md (drafts)
- Loom-side: closing drawer in loom/decisions + 1-2 KG triples + diary

Steps:
1. Pick canary (loom-7bn vs loom-ki5) based on which feels more representative — loom-7bn is small + isolated; loom-ki5 is two-PR
2. Invoke /upstream-a-bead <bead-id>
3. Walk through M1-M7
4. File real upstream issue + PR
5. Spawn watch-bead
6. Capture all friction encountered in canary close-out drawer; file follow-up beads for each friction point

Acceptance: canary upstream PR filed; watch-bead exists in bd list --label=upstream:watch; close-out drawer enumerates friction; follow-up beads filed.

Design source: drawer_loom_decisions_a6e64f9cfb21a9d16fc47604
notes:
CANARY PRE-FLIGHT FRICTION (discovered 2026-05-29 session-startup): The upstream-a-bead recipe shipped to the repo (k2g.1-.6 all closed + merged) but was NEVER installed — no ~/.claude/skills/upstream-a-bead symlink, /upstream-a-bead returned 'Unknown skill'. Root cause: install.sh globs skills/*/SKILL.md + commands/*.md (so no code edit needed), but install.sh was simply never re-run after the k2g implementation waves. FIXED this session by running ./install.sh (symlinks now present; harness re-scanned and the skill became invocable mid-session). 

FRICTION TO CAPTURE IN CANARY CLOSEOUT: 'recipe shipped but not installed/verified-invocable' is a real gap the implementation waves missed. Candidate follow-up: add an install-invocability assertion (e.g. install.sh --check verifies each skills/*/SKILL.md has a live ~/.claude symlink, or a closeout step in bead-lifecycle-shell phase C that runs install.sh when new skills/commands are added). 

START-HERE FOR FRESH SESSION: run /upstream-a-bead loom-k2g.7. Recipe is now installed + invocable. Pick canary target loom-7bn (beadpowers splitting-heuristic; small/isolated/single-PR — recommended first dogfood) vs loom-ki5 (superpowers; two PRs). Lane likely --issue+pr. WIP-rescue bead loom-6ah (audit-project portability) is unrelated, filed same session.
close_reason: Canary complete: drove loom-7bn through upstream-a-bead end-to-end; PR#2 filed; 5 friction points captured in drawer_loom_decisions_aa11fe3d98a3afe735853668; follow-ups loom-bqz + loom-7f3 filed.

title: Audit loom-installed primitives for hardcoded "loom-" references in logic (not examples)
description:
## Why this exists

Loom-installed primitives (skills/, commands/, hooks/, agents/, scripts/) symlink into every project's `~/.claude/` on install. References to the literal string `loom-` in LOGIC (regexes, file path lookups, command parsing) silently break for non-loom projects. References in EXAMPLES (sample workflow.json showing `./install.sh`, sample bead IDs in prose) are fine — they're illustrative.

Surfaced 2026-05-27 while filing loom-6p6 (/wrap-up discovery enhancement). Frank flagged that the new discovery grep had to use the generic bead-ID regex `[a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}(\.[0-9a-z]+)*` rather than `loom-[a-z0-9]+` because /wrap-up symlinks into every project. The reflexive question: are there OTHER loom-installed primitives that made the same mistake?

## What this delivers

A grep sweep + categorization + fix-or-defer report:

1. `grep -rEn 'loom-' commands/ skills/ hooks/ agents/ scripts/ rules/` (and any other dirs that get installed).
2. Categorize each hit:
   - **LOGIC HIT** (regex, file lookup, command parsing) — bug, fix in this bead or split out.
   - **EXAMPLE HIT** (illustrative bead ID in prose, sample workflow.json) — fine, leave.
   - **LINEAGE HIT** (reference to a loom-XXX bead/drawer as provenance) — fine, leave.
3. For each LOGIC HIT, propose generic-ification: substitute `<prefix>-XXX` placeholder + discover prefix from `.beads/config.yaml` or similar.
4. Either fix in this bead (if hits are <5) or file follow-up beads per hit (if many).

## Acceptance

- Categorized report of all `loom-` occurrences across installed primitives.
- Zero LOGIC HITs remain, OR each LOGIC HIT has a follow-up bead.
- EXAMPLE/LINEAGE HITs explicitly enumerated as 'fine, leave' (not just ignored).

## Provenance

Conversation between Frank and Claude 2026-05-27 during loom-6p6 design dialogue. Frank quote: 'this shouldn't be loom-specific, this should be for any repo that it's in. (And this might mean that we have to do a trawl through loom to find other loom specific searches if this is a real problem)'.

title: Upstream-PR flow — clone, RED/GREEN diagnose, file bug + PR in one shot
description:
## Why

loom keeps hitting upstream cases (mempalace stop-hook, superpowers skills, beadpowers, Claude Code itself) where the right fix lives outside loom. Today these become 'closed as upstream-pending' beads with a hand-rolled bug report. We have no consistent mechanism for actually driving an upstream contribution from inside the loom workflow.

Examples this round:
- loom-98x (closed upstream-pending) — mempalace stop-hook substance gate
- loom-7bn — beadpowers:create-beads splitting-heuristic prompt
- loom-ki5 — superpowers verification-before-completion + systematic-debugging

## What

A mechanism for handling upstream PRs end-to-end inside loom:
- Pull the target repo into an untracked, gitignored subdir (e.g. .upstream/<repo>/)
- Run loom's discipline against it: RED test that pins the bug → minimal GREEN fix → coverage
- Produce two artifacts in one shot: a polished bug report (markdown) and a PR-ready branch with commits
- Capture the upstream lineage in a decision drawer

## Open design questions (for brainstorming)

- Subdir vs sibling repo vs git worktree of a remote? Probably plain clone into .upstream/ + gitignore.
- Does loom-managed bd track the upstream work, or is it scoped only to loom's beads?
- How does the upstream repo's test framework integrate with loom's recipe shells?
- What's the handoff format — auto-filed gh issue + PR draft, or markdown drawer the user files manually?
- Activity recipe? Probably a new 'upstream-a-bead' recipe that defers to bead-lifecycle-shell.

## Acceptance (rough, to be refined)

- A documented flow / skill / command that takes a target repo + symptom, produces a RED test in the upstream repo, a GREEN fix, a bug report, and a PR branch
- At least one in-flight upstream case driven through it (loom-7bn or loom-ki5 as canary)
- Decision drawer captures the chosen design and any conventions

## Lineage

Surfaces this session 2026-05-27 morning while discussing loom-d2o cleanup. Recurring pattern across loom-98x, loom-7bn, loom-ki5.
close_reason: Epic complete: upstream-a-bead recipe + infra (k2g.1-.6) shipped & merged; canary k2g.7 validated the full --issue+pr lane end-to-end (pprice/beadpowers#2). Friction → loom-bqz + loom-7f3.

title: Loom-managed projects: auto-disable Claude Code harness defaults that conflict with loom discipline (task tools + auto-memory)
description:
## Why this exists

Loom-managed projects use `bd` for task tracking and `bd remember`+MemPalace for memory (per project constitution). Claude Code's harness defaults inject competing primitives:

1. **TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskList/TodoWrite** — every 2-3 tool calls the harness injects `<system-reminder>The task tools haven't been used recently...` regardless of whether the project uses task tools. CLAUDE.md tells the agent to ignore these, but they still consume context tokens AND each one creates a fresh competing-instruction surface the agent has to reason about. Observed during the 2026-05-27 loom-7p6 parallel-dispatch session: 6+ TaskCreate reminders fired across the 7-worker session despite explicit project-level prohibition. Upstream issues [#26038](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/26038) + [#45986](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/45986) request granular disable; only built-in mechanism today is to disable the tools entirely.

2. **Auto-memory (`~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/MEMORY.md`)** — Claude Code's per-project file-based memory system. Loom's CLAUDE.md says 'Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with bd memories'. Currently agent-compliance-only; a structural fix is the env var.

3. **EnterPlanMode** — user's global CLAUDE.md already disables ('Planning is handled by external tooling'). Listed for completeness; no per-project env var exists.

## What this delivers

A loom-canonical `.claude/settings.json` `env` block, wired into:
- `install.sh` — adds the env block to the LOOM repo's own `.claude/settings.json` on install
- `/audit-project` skill — detects missing env block in any loom-managed project + offers to wire it (same pattern as loom-1tq for workflow.json .deploy)
- `docs/reference/loom-env-vars.md` — Diataxis reference page listing each var loom disables, with the upstream-feature lineage and rationale

## Locked decisions to make at brainstorm

- **Scope:** loom-managed projects only (per workflow.json presence, OR explicit opt-in). Non-loom projects keep whatever the user wants.
- **Granularity:** single `env` block with both vars, or per-var opt-out flags? Default to bundled.
- **Migration of existing loom-managed projects:** does `/audit-project` only WARN, or AUTOFIX? Lean WARN by default; AUTOFIX behind `--apply-onboarding` flag (consistent with loom-8hg/loom-a29 pattern).
- **Discovery of new conflicts:** `docs/reference/loom-env-vars.md` doubles as a discovery surface — when Claude Code ships a new conflicting default, file a sibling bead that adds the var to the loom-canonical block.

## Env vars confirmed today (web research 2026-05-27)

```
{
  "env": {
    "CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS": "false",
    "CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY": "1"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance

- `install.sh` writes the env block into loom's own `.claude/settings.json` (idempotent — preserves user additions, only inserts loom-owned keys; mirrors the existing settings.snippet.json merge pattern)
- A test under `lib/tests/` exercises install.sh's env-block-merge: fresh project, project with existing env block, project with conflicting key
- `/audit-project` adds a check (`--check=env-conflicts` or similar) that warns when a loom-managed project is missing the canonical env block; `--apply-onboarding` auto-applies
- `docs/reference/loom-env-vars.md` exists, lists both vars with upstream-feature links + rationale
- Smoke test: open a fresh Claude Code session in loom after the fix, run 10 tool calls, confirm zero TaskCreate system-reminders fire

## Anti-scope

- Do NOT remove the project's CLAUDE.md prohibitions on TodoWrite/TaskCreate — they're still useful for sessions where Claude Code's env var is overridden somehow (CI, alternate harnesses)
- Do NOT touch user's global `~/.claude/settings.json` from install.sh — the env block goes into the loom repo's per-project settings only; user can opt-in to global separately
- Do NOT try to disable EnterPlanMode via this bead — no env var exists upstream yet

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-27 by Frank after the 6-TaskCreate-reminder-session ('The TaskCreate and other intrusions from claude are a persistent nuisance'). Sibling discipline to loom-1tq (audit-project + workflow.json .deploy) and loom-0k0 (settings.json deploy hint). Upstream issue links: [#26038](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/26038), [#45986](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/45986), [#23544](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/23544), [#23750](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/23750).
close_reason: Canonical env block ({CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS=false, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1}) wired to project-level .claude/settings.json via install.sh + /audit-project Item 16 + [AUTOFIX:loom-env-block]. Merged 8242dd4. Tests 46→47, mkdocs --strict clean, dogfooded in loom-the-repo. Takes effect on Claude Code session restart; existing sessions retain harness defaults until restarted.

title: Persistent-bash cwd drifts into worker worktree under parallel-dispatch session
description:
## Symptom

During the 2026-05-27 loom-7p6 + loom-cuk parallel-dispatch session (7 background workers dispatched in one batch), central agent's persistent-bash cwd silently drifted from `/home/frank/repos/loom` (main checkout) into `/home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/worktrees/agent-a36b96c117ccefeda/` (the 7p6.7 worker's worktree). The drift was invisible — no explicit `cd` was issued; pwd just resolved to the worktree path on the next Bash invocation.

## Consequences observed

1. `bd close loom-7p6.7` ran from the worktree's bd context — surfaced as a permissions warning (`/home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/worktrees/agent-a36b96c117ccefeda/.beads has permissions 0775`), but the bd close itself propagated through bd's dolt/jsonl sync.
2. `git merge --no-ff frank/loom-7p6.7` returned 'Already up to date' because the merge target was the worktree's HEAD (which IS the branch tip), not main.

## Recovery (one-shot)

- cd back to `/home/frank/repos/loom` (main checkout)
- `git stash` the bd state drift from worktree-context close
- Re-run `git merge --no-ff frank/loom-7p6.7` from main (merged cleanly)
- `bd export` to regenerate canonical issues.jsonl from main's dolt
- Commit bd state as standalone bd-close commit

## Root-cause hypothesis

Claude Code's persistent-bash cwd state may be shared across worker dispatch context, OR the Agent harness's worktree-isolation propagates cwd back to central when the worker returns. The drift happened around the 7p6.7 completion notification, suggesting the worker's worktree path leaked through the notification path or the harness's cleanup.

## Fix plan (LOCKED 2026-05-27 by central)

**Defense in depth — hook + convention together.**

### Hook layer: `hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh`

PreToolUse Bash hook that refuses (exit 2) when BOTH:
- cwd realpath resolves inside any `.claude/worktrees/agent-*/`
- command matches central-op allowlist: `git merge`, `git push`, `bd close`, `bd update`, `bd dolt push`

Shape mirrors `hooks/edit-write-pwd-guard.sh` (loom-ymc) — same realpath canonicalization, same exit-code convention, same bypass-env shape.

Bypass: `LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP=1` (literal-1 match, per loom-b1l precedent — rejects =yes/=true/=0/empty).

Stderr message names recovery: `<retry>` or `LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP=1 <command>`.

### Convention layer: 5th section in `.claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md`

Titled 'Central-side cwd verification (after worker dispatch returns)'. Documents the failure mode, the new hook as the mechanical fix, and the convention fallback (verify pwd before merge/push/bd-close after any parallel dispatch returns).

## Acceptance

- `hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh` created + registered in `settings.snippet.json` PreToolUse Bash matcher
- 10+ fixture cases in `lib/tests/cwd-drift-guard.test.sh` covering all 5 allowlist subcommands × refuse-in-worktree + negative-from-main cases + bypass-env + tool-name-guard
- Docs page `docs/reference/cwd-drift-guard.md` + hooks index row updated
- `.claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md` gains the 5th section
- `mkdocs build --strict` clean

## Lineage

- loom-ymc — sibling worker-side hook for Edit/Write path leak
- loom-x4m — bd-worktree-preseed hook (subcommand-matching precedent)
- loom-0hi / loom-b1l — env-var literal-1 bypass convention
- loom-g5k — dispatched-agent smoke battery (worker-side companion)
close_reason: Shipped in merge commit. hooks/cwd-drift-guard.sh blocks 5 central-context Bash commands (git merge/push, bd close/update, bd dolt push) when cwd resolves under .claude/worktrees/agent-*/. Mirrors loom-ymc shape. Plus .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md 5th section + docs/reference/cwd-drift-guard.md. 30 new fixture cases + 721/0 full lib/tests sweep. Bypass: LOOM_CWD_DRIFT_GUARD_SKIP=1 (literal-1 per loom-b1l). Defense in depth: hook (mechanical) + convention (post-dispatch verify pwd).

title: docs/reference: workflow.json schema page + loom-print-deploy-hint reference
description:
## Context

loom-0k0 (shipped 2026-05-26) added `.deploy` to the `<project>/.claude/workflow.json` schema and shipped `scripts/loom-print-deploy-hint`. Neither has a dedicated docs/reference page yet. The full schema (`.v`, `.mode`, `.guest`, `.deploy`) is documented only in `lib/workflow-config.sh`'s header comment and scattered references in skill bodies.

## Scope

Two new docs/reference pages, sibling to existing `docs/reference/loom-rebase-worktree.md`, `loom-worktree-python.md`, `bd-prime-wrapper.md`, etc.:

### 1. `docs/reference/workflow-json.md`

Cover the full schema:

- **`.v`** — version field (currently `1`).
- **`.mode`** — `"full"` / `"light"` / `"off"`. Resolution priority. Env override via `CLAUDE_WORKFLOW_OFF=1`.
- **`.guest`** — guest-mode block: `{active, bd_mode, repo_key}`. Cross-link to `docs/how-to/guest-mode.md` and to `loom-4re` lineage.
- **`.deploy`** — surface-only project deploy hint (string command). Cross-link to `loom-print-deploy-hint.md` and to `commands/wrap-up.md` section 6.

Include: file location (`<project>/.claude/workflow.json`), how to set fields manually vs. via slash commands, full example JSON, what consumers read each field.

### 2. `docs/reference/loom-print-deploy-hint.md`

Cover the script:

- **What it does** — prints `Next step (project deploy): <command>` if `.deploy` is set; silent skip otherwise. Always exits 0.
- **Why a wrapper script and not a sourced lib snippet** — the `lib/workflow-config.sh` uses `BASH_SOURCE[0]` for path detection (bash-only); the wrapper isolates that constraint from the invoking shell (zsh under Claude Code's Bash tool).
- **Where invoked** — `commands/wrap-up.md` section 6. Sample output for both `.deploy`-set and `.deploy`-absent cases.
- **Installed at** — `~/.claude/scripts/loom-print-deploy-hint`. Self-relative lib resolution.

## Out of scope

- mkdocs.yml navigation tree updates beyond what these two pages need (add them to the existing `Reference` section).
- Updating `docs/reference/installed-files.md` tree — that page is intentionally a partial listing; convention is not to enumerate every script there (see loom-0k0 doc audit).

## Acceptance

- Both pages exist and pass `mkdocs build --strict`.
- Each page cross-links to the other.
- Both pages appear in mkdocs.yml nav under Reference.
- `pre-push-mkdocs-strict` hook passes when pushing.

## Family lineage

- Parent: `drawer_loom_decisions_9fb2868e288751d22c6dd7ec` (loom-0k0 closing drawer).
- Sibling follow-up: `/audit-project` integration for `.deploy` (TBD).
- Pattern: sibling docs/reference pages for loom-installed scripts (loom-azt, loom-rsk, loom-x4m precedents).

title: /audit-project: detect missing .deploy field; prompt to set or explicitly skip
description:
## Context

loom-0k0 (shipped 2026-05-26) added `.deploy` (string command, optional) to the `<project>/.claude/workflow.json` schema. wrap-up section 6 reads it via `~/.claude/scripts/loom-print-deploy-hint` and surfaces `Next step (project deploy): <cmd>` if set; silent skip otherwise.

Loom's own workflow.json now sets `.deploy: "./install.sh"`. Other loom-managed projects ship without `.deploy` set — silent skip is the safe default, but it also means the field is undiscoverable until a user reads the wrap-up.md source or stumbles on this drawer.

## Scope

Extend `/audit-project` (and the `project-onboarder` subagent it drives) to:

1. Add a check item to the structured PASS/WARN/MISS checklist for the `.deploy` field. Status:
   - **PASS**: `.deploy` set to a non-empty string in `<project>/.claude/workflow.json`.
   - **MISS**: `workflow.json` exists but `.deploy` is absent. Prompt the user:
     - "What command should `/wrap-up` surface as the project deploy hint? (Common shapes: `./install.sh`, `make deploy`, `./scripts/build`, `kubectl apply -k ...`. Leave blank to explicitly opt out — sets `.deploy: ""`.)"
   - **N/A**: `workflow.json` doesn't exist yet (other audit items already cover this).

2. When the user provides a command, write it into `workflow.json` (via `jq` or hand-merge — same pattern as `.guest` block writes in `lib/workflow-config.sh`).

3. When the user opts out, write `.deploy: ""` so future audits don't re-prompt (treat empty string as "explicitly chose nothing" vs. absent which is "never decided").

## Out of scope

- Auto-detecting the right command from `Makefile`/`scripts/`/`package.json` — keep it user-driven for now; auto-detect can land as a separate follow-up if friction shows.
- Validating that the command exists or is executable — surface only by design (loom-0k0 decision).

## Acceptance

- `/audit-project` in a project without `.deploy` set reports it in the checklist with status MISS.
- The interactive fix flow prompts for a command, writes it to `workflow.json`, and the next `/audit-project` reports PASS.
- The opt-out path (empty input) writes `.deploy: ""` and reports PASS on re-run.
- A test under `lib/tests/` exercises the schema-write path (parallel to existing `workflow-config-guest.test.sh`).

## Family lineage

- Parent finding: `drawer_loom_decisions_9fb2868e288751d22c6dd7ec` (loom-0k0 closing drawer).
- Sibling follow-up: docs/reference page for the workflow.json schema + loom-print-deploy-hint (TBD).
- Pattern: same shape as the `.guest` block discovery + onboarding pattern (loom-4re).

title: git-push-bd-sync hook fires pre-emptively after push completes (N=4 observations)
description:
Recurring observation across 4 recent sessions (loom-vca Wave 1 close 2026-05-26 +3x followups + loom-egl close 2026-05-26 this): the git-push-bd-sync hook emits its PreToolUse warning ("About to push, but .beads/ has uncommitted modifications...") AFTER the git push has already completed AND bd state is clean. Warning is stale/unactionable and adds noise to the session-close protocol.

REPRODUCTION:
- Run the canonical session-close sequence: bd close <id> → git add .beads/issues.jsonl → git commit → bd dolt push → git push
- When all commands are batched in one Bash call (";" separated), the bd state is fully clean by the time git push fires
- Hook still triggers because it scans .beads/ state in PreToolUse handler but doesn't reconcile against "work was already done in this same Bash call"

HYPOTHESIS:
- Hook reads .beads/issues.jsonl mtime/diff BEFORE the Bash command's individual sub-commands run, not at the moment of the actual git push subprocess invocation
- Or: hook fires on the Bash tool call entry (sees dirty state from start of the call), not per-subcommand

CANDIDATE FIXES:
- (a) Make hook re-check .beads/ cleanliness right before the git push subprocess, not at the start of the Bash tool call
- (b) Suppress the warning when the immediate prior commands in the same Bash invocation already committed + pushed bd state
- (c) Document as known false-positive and demote warning level

PRIOR ART:
- loom-vca Wave 1 close diary entry 2026-05-26 noted: "GIT-PUSH-BD-SYNC-HOOK-FIRED-LATE: hook fired PreToolUse on git push but push had already completed AND drift already committed via c8ebc6b"
- Wave 1 followups diary 2026-05-26 observation #4: "git-push bd-sync hook fired pre-emptively 3x this session"
- loom-egl close 2026-05-26 evening: same hook fired late after clean push

LINEAGE:
- Hook lives in hooks/bd-sync-on-push.sh or similar (verify path during fix)
- Related to bd auto-export discipline (loom-4um, loom-x4m, loom-22h family)

ACCEPTANCE:
1. Hook only fires when bd state is ACTUALLY dirty at the moment of the git push subprocess (not at start of Bash tool call)
2. Regression test: scripted session-close sequence in a fresh worktree, hook emits zero false-positive warnings
3. Document the false-positive pattern in docs/reference/hooks/ if (c) is chosen instead

EXECUTION: bugfix-a-bead recipe. Worktree on frank/<this-bead-id>. RED test scripts the bd close + push sequence and asserts hook emits no warning.

title: audit-project: detect missing tree-sitter.json in grammar directories (ABI-15 compat warning)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 by mforth — running 'tree-sitter generate' against tree-sitter-mforth/ printed:

  Warning: No tree-sitter.json file found in your grammar, this file is required to generate with ABI 15. Using ABI version 14 instead.

tree-sitter 0.25+ (current default ABI 15) wants a tree-sitter.json sibling to grammar.js. Older grammar repos (pre-0.25) work fine without it but quietly fall back to ABI 14. Same shape as the docs-scaffold gaps (loom-ad1, loom-tww, umbrella loom-vca) — an upstream tool's template/init defaults don't pass through to a real publish — but a DIFFERENT upstream (tree-sitter, not mkdocs-material).

mforth downstream fix: commit 216f482 (https://github.com/fkberthold/mforth/commit/216f482). Wrote tree-sitter.json directly mirroring the existing package.json [tree-sitter] block.

WHY THIS IS A LOOM AUDIT-PROJECT CHECK:
- Catches a real silent gap that drift-creates over time (tree-sitter upgrade in nixpkgs / homebrew silently degrades old grammar repos).
- The fix is tiny + well-defined (the schema is the existing package.json [tree-sitter] block, restructured).
- audit-project already has the surface for adding checks (the loom-km8.3 project-onboarder pattern + the existing --check=docs option).

PROPOSED CHECK: --check=tree-sitter (or fold into a broader --check=grammars).
Behavior:
1. Find any directory under the project root containing grammar.js (typical: tree-sitter-* subdirs, but a few projects use other naming).
2. For each, check if tree-sitter.json is a sibling.
3. If absent: WARN with the message + a one-line fix recipe ('tree-sitter init -p <dir>' to scaffold interactively, OR see <example tree-sitter.json schema link>).
4. If present: OK.

OUT OF SCOPE: validating tree-sitter.json schema beyond presence (tree-sitter generate itself does that).

ACCEPTANCE:
- audit-project gains the check (folded into the appropriate granularity).
- Running it against mforth (post-216f482) returns OK.
- Running it against a synthetic test fixture with grammar.js + no tree-sitter.json returns WARN with the message + fix recipe.
- KG triple linking the new check to this bead.

tree-sitter init can't be invoked non-interactively (requires TTY) so the audit-project fix should NOT try to auto-run it — recipe-only.

Priority P3 per USR — small infrastructure-polish, not blocking anything, but worth catching before more downstream grammar repos hit the warning.

title: evaluate ast-grep as lightweight AST query/rewrite substrate for refactor-a-bead + cleanup-a-bead
description:
DEFERRED P3 from loom-gn5 verdict (drawer_loom_decisions_c9de5e283972378e084a9602). The Serena evaluation surfaced ast-grep as a separate question worth evaluating: would lightweight AST-based queries (find references, AST-precise rewrites) materially help recipe-fit refactor/cleanup steps beyond what grep + Edit/MultiEdit already provide?

Scope: empirical test against a concrete refactor or cleanup bead. Pick one from a Python project (liza_base most active candidate) where the touched-files set is >3 and includes a symbol-precise concern (rename across files OR orphan-reference hunt). Run BOTH paths:
1. Native: grep + Edit/MultiEdit (current loom default)
2. ast-grep: AST pattern matching + rewrite

Compare on: precision (false positive / false negative count), token cost, wall-clock cost, error-recovery cost when something goes wrong.

Deliverable: findings drawer in loom/decisions with explicit YES/NO recommendation on whether to mention ast-grep in refactor-a-bead + cleanup-a-bead recipe text.

Why P3: not blocking any active queue work. Filed to keep the question tracked so it doesn't get lost. If a refactor bead with symbol-surgery shape arises and ast-grep would have clearly helped, bump to P2.

Lineage: spun off from loom-gn5 (Serena evaluation, NO verdict). Drawer drawer_loom_decisions_c9de5e283972378e084a9602 carries the analytical context that led here.

title: [docs] edit-after-failure-guard: add 'fixing the guard hook itself' bootstrap recipe
description:
The edit-after-failure-guard PreToolUse hook can block edits to its OWN source file when bug-fixing the hook in a live session — the live hook is still running the old buggy code while you're trying to fix it. Surfaced empirically during loom-7j5 (2026-05-19): the first Edit attempt on hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh was blocked because recent transcript tail contained 'fail' substrings (from earlier test runs, from Read of the hook's own source containing the FAIL_RE regex literal, etc.) and no test file had been touched since.

The same friction was independently rediscovered by the sister e2e-api-tests session 2026-05-19 (the one that filed loom-7j5).

Two workarounds proved reliable; neither is documented in docs/reference/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.md:

1. **Edit a test file first.** Any Edit/Write/MultiEdit on a path matching the test-file conventions (tests/, *.test.sh, etc.) clears the test_edit_after_failure tracker. Re-attempt the source edit immediately after; it'll be allowed. Caveat: any subsequent non-Bash tool_result containing FAIL substrings (a Read of the source you're fixing, a mempalace_search returning the bead description, etc.) will re-arm the guard. Repeat as needed.

2. **Bash + python heredoc.** Apply the source change via 'python3 <<EOF ... pathlib.Path(...).write_text(...) ... EOF' invoked through Bash. The hook gates only Edit/Write/MultiEdit; Bash file operations bypass it entirely. Works in any session.

In-session 'export LOOM_EDIT_AFTER_FAILURE_GUARD_SKIP=1' does NOT work — parent env is frozen at fork time. The env var must be set before claude launches, or claude must be exited and relaunched with the var exported.

Scope: one section addition to docs/reference/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.md (placed next to the existing 'Bypass' section), titled something like 'Bootstrap path when fixing the hook itself' or 'Self-modifying-hook recipe'. ~15-25 lines of prose explaining the two workarounds + the env-var constraint.
notes:
Lineage:
- loom-z3m.6 (closed) — introduced the hook
- loom-7j5 (closed) — three-axis fix; surfaced this bootstrap-path gap in its OPEN section
- loom-7j5 closing drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_4a0a37a40e66ed2d4c219502 (loom/decisions, 2026-05-19) — see PROCESS NOTES section for the full empirical evidence
- Sister session: e2e-api-tests Architecture.md rework 2026-05-19 independently discovered the Bash+python workaround

Where:
- ~/.claude/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.sh (symlink → loom repo)
- docs/reference/hooks/edit-after-failure-guard.md (the doc to extend)

Not blocking; cleanup-shaped follow-up to make the next person's debugging cheaper.
