title: Housekeeping: bd staleness-sensor guard + pytest-tempdir prune hook
description:
Root cause: two unrelated but cheap-to-fix housekeeping items surfaced in liza-base — accumulate as ambient friction.

Surfaced by:
- liza-base f11: bd staleness-sensor hook fires in sessions where bd isn't installed; exits 1 twice per startup
- liza-base f12: pytest-of-* tempdirs accumulate unchecked across sessions, eating drive space
design:
(1) Add 'command -v bd >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0' at the top of the staleness-sensor hook script (1-line fix). install.sh template ships this guard for all bd-dependent hooks.
(2) Add a SessionStart housekeeping hook (or extend an existing one) that prunes ./tmp/pytest-of-* older than 24h. Opt-out via LOOM_PYTEST_TEMPDIR_PRUNE_SKIP=1. Alternatively, audit-project drops a pytest --basetemp=$(mktemp -d) convention into scripts/test templates for pytest-using projects.
notes:
[liza f11] sid=3fe1ed50 ts=2026-05-12T03:19:21Z user: 'bd list exited 1 ×2 — staleness sensor tries to call bd in the apartment session; bd isn't there. Should be guarded.'
[liza f12] sid=d1a14835 ts=2026-05-18T00:57:46Z user: 'Oy, the pytests are eating up a HUGE amount or drive space, look at ./tmp/pytest-of-frank We need to clean that up, and make sure its cleaned up regularly.'

title: Project-invariants enforcement: rule template + PreToolUse guard hook
description:
Root cause: project-specific architectural invariants (e.g., 'Liza must touch the world only through MCP, never direct file I/O') aren't enforced by any guard. Agent's general defaults override project-specific must-not rules.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f6 / liza f6: encapsulation violation — agent used direct bash for Liza interaction instead of MCP.
design: .claude/rules/project-invariants.md template (user populates with project-specific must/must-not). loom installs PreToolUse guard hook that reads the rules file and injects matching invariants into agent context when tool calls match the trigger patterns (e.g., Bash on apartment paths). audit-project surfaces missing invariants file as a WARN gap.
notes: [liza f6] sid=5b3e2455 ts=2026-05-17T05:00:31Z user: 'this is an obvious error that should never happen. If something involves Liza interacting with anything, it _must_ use the MCP, it is literally her world.'

title: docs/reference: claude-code-hook-semantics.md + TaskCreate-reminder ignore note
description:
Root cause: two distinct manifestations of the same underlying gap — agent operating on stale or incomplete mental model of Claude Code harness behavior, with no curated doc to anchor against.

Surfaced by:
- liza-base f9: agent confidently asserted wrong Stop-hook timing
- liza-base f8: TaskCreate reminder noise — user asked 'is this coming from loom?' because no doc explains the harness-level origin
design:
New docs/reference/claude-code-hook-semantics.md page: firing conditions for SessionStart / PreCompact / PreToolUse / PostToolUse / UserPromptSubmit / Stop. Cite primary CC docs. Cross-ref from CLAUDE.md so 'hook' / 'SessionStart' / 'Stop' discussions auto-load context.

Also add a CLAUDE.md snippet (or extend an existing one): 'When you see the TaskCreate reminder, ignore it silently — bd is the tracker in this project. Do not call TaskCreate.' audit-project installs this snippet into per-project CLAUDE.md when bd is the chosen tracker.
notes:
[liza f9] sid=862dac15 ts=2026-05-18T18:26:20Z user: 'I think there's a mixup. Confirm this for me. I'm pretty sure the Stop hook only fires when claude is shutdown with /exit or similar.'

[liza f8] sid=5b3e2455 ts=2026-05-17T05:27:56Z user: 'Alright, while that runs, tell me about "Ignoring TaskCreate" is that coming from Loom or something else in claude?'

title: Worker-report sampling transparency: 'Processed: X of Y' contract clause
description:
Root cause: when a dispatched worker samples N-of-M items, that fact isn't always surfaced in the return; user has to ask explicitly. The T2 dispatch in this very retrospective (loom-z3m.1.2) DID surface sampled_of_total cleanly, but that was because the brief instructed it explicitly. Need to codify as a rule for all dispatches.

Surfaced by: loom-z3m.1 f10 (liza-base): user asked 'so you only grabbed a sample of 2 dozen?'
design: Add a worker-report-contract clause to .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md: 'Every report must include a Processed: X of Y line whenever sampling occurred (worker chose a subset of available items). Centrally, dispatchers must add a 'sampled_of_total' field requirement to any worker brief when the input set is large.' Reinforce via the worker-prompt template the dispatcher uses (loom-7p6.1 boilerplate).
notes: [liza f10] sid=862dac15 ts=2026-05-18T18:08:14Z user: 'So, if I understand correctly, you didn't do all 600, you just grabed a sample of 2 dozen, so I could look those over. When we remine, we should do the same on Option A.'

title: Phase 3: struggle taxonomy + structural fixes
description:
Phase 3 of loom-z3m. Deferred until Phase 2 drawer is filed. Shape will depend on what Phase 2 surfaces, but predictable contours:

- Classify findings into struggle-types: missing-context, wrong-default, skill-not-invoked, hook-gap, worker-brief-omission, bd-memory-gap, statusline-blindspot, etc.
- For each struggle-type that recurs ≥3× → propose a structural fix (new hook, new skill, recipe addition, bd memory entry, statusline indicator)
- File the structural fixes as fresh children of this epic with their own descriptions

Taxonomy is the design output; structural fixes are the implementation output. Epic stays open until both land.

Blocked by Phase 2 bead close.

title: Phase 2: long-form retrospective synthesis drawer
description:
Phase 2 of loom-z3m. Single-bead, single-session task: write the long-form synthesis drawer for loom/decisions covering the dig in narrative form.

Input: the ~15 Phase-1 beads + Phase-1 capture drawer + worker meta-observations + (optionally) re-reading the highest-signal transcript excerpts in full.

Output structure:
- Three-projects-three-shapes (what struggle dominated each)
- Verbatim transcript quotes (3–5 per category) with session links
- Cross-cutting patterns
- What loom got right (recovered from worker meta-observations)
- Hand-off hypotheses to Phase 3

Stop condition: if the drawer reveals no recurring patterns → file ad-hoc P3 fixes individually, skip Phase 3 taxonomy epic. Otherwise file loom-retro-p3 follow-up.

Blocked by Phase 1 epic close.

title: Constitution: staleness detection + author-and-audit how-to + CLAUDE.md mention
description:
## Goal
Final polish: catch stale constitutions, document the authoring + maintenance flow, mention the new file in loom's CLAUDE.md.

## Files
- hooks/constitution-enforce.sh (add age-skew warning logic)
- skills/audit-project/SKILL.md (confirm diff-on-re-run logic from child 2 is sufficient; extend if needed)
- docs/how-to/author-project-constitution.md (new — Diataxis how-to: when to run audit, what to author in prose body, how to evolve)
- CLAUDE.md (loom's own, mention .claude/project-constitution.md and the dogfooding example)

## Steps
1. Add age-skew check to hook: on first call per shell session, if .claude/project-constitution.md mtime is older than newest of {devbox.json, *.lock, .tool-versions, flake.nix} by > 7 days, emit one-time stderr warning suggesting /audit-project --check=constitution. Use $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR sentinel to one-shot per session.
2. Write docs/how-to/author-project-constitution.md covering: first-time audit, evolving conventions, when to add bypass_patterns, what NOT to put in the prose body
3. Update loom CLAUDE.md to mention the file (Conventions section)
4. Confirm session-startup nudge for missing constitution in loom-managed project is in place (from child 3)

## Acceptance
- Stale-fixture test: constitution older than touched devbox.json by 8 days triggers warning on first Bash call; doesn't trigger on second Bash call same session
- How-to doc reads cleanly to someone who has never seen the constitution file
- Loom's own CLAUDE.md mentions the file accurately

## Provenance
Parent epic loom-6f8. Depends on capture (child 2), surfacing (child 3), hook (child 4). Design: drawer_loom_decisions_76ec9140c47ff768735358c0.

title: session-startup: surface red CI runs from last N commits to prevent silent failures across sessions
description:
## Problem

The session-startup skill primes beads + MemPalace + diary at cold-start but never checks CI health. A red GitHub Actions workflow (Deploy docs in this case) sat unaddressed for 2+ days because nothing in the cold-start routine pointed at it. The first session of each day blissfully kicked off new work while CI was already red from the previous session's push.

Concrete instance (2026-05-13 → 2026-05-15): Deploy docs failed on every push for 10 consecutive runs across 3 sessions before being noticed. Root cause was a single broken docs link (loom-59w); CI caught it instantly, but the signal didn't propagate to any session-startup.

## Why this matters more than a docs-specific pre-push hook

Considered + rejected: pre-push 'mkdocs build --strict' hook. Catches docs breakage 24s earlier than CI but is narrow (docs only), adds a tooling dependency (mkdocs in $PATH), and assumes the failure mode that just happened will be the next one. The session-startup-surfaces-red-CI approach is:

- General: catches any workflow failure, not just docs.
- Cheap: one 'gh run list --limit N' call.
- Low-friction: info line at cold-start, not a push-time blocker.
- Mode-aware: skippable when workflow mode is 'off' or 'light' (matches existing session-startup mode behavior).

## Proposed shape — two paths, implementer picks

**Path A (skill-only, lower lift):** Add a step to skills/session-startup/SKILL.md telling the agent to run `gh run list --limit 3 --json status,conclusion,name,headBranch` at cold-start and surface any failure to the user with the commit message + workflow name. In light mode, abbreviate to a single line. Skip in off mode.

**Path B (hook-backed, more reliable):** Add a SessionStart hook that runs the gh check and prepends the result to the session's context (similar to how bd prime is wired in). More reliable (deterministic, doesn't depend on agent compliance) but harder to implement and may compete with bd-prime byte budget.

Recommend Path A as the cheap first cut; promote to Path B only if Path A misses in practice.

## Scope (Path A)

1. Edit skills/session-startup/SKILL.md to add a 'Check CI health' step between the existing 'Prime beads' (step 1) and 'Check in-progress' (step 2) steps.
2. Step prose:
   - Run `gh run list --limit 3 --json status,conclusion,name,headBranch,createdAt` (or pretty equivalent).
   - If any of the last N runs on main has conclusion=failure, surface to user: '⚠ CI: <workflow> failed on <commit short SHA> (<age>)'.
   - In light mode, condense to one line.
   - Skip in off mode entirely.
3. Behavior notes:
   - Be tolerant of 'gh' not being authenticated or not installed — degrade to no-op with a single 'gh check skipped' line, not an error.
   - Don't fail the skill if the gh call errors.

## Scope (Path B, deferred)

Defer to a follow-up bead if Path A proves insufficient in practice.

## Acceptance

- skills/session-startup/SKILL.md updated with the CI-health step.
- Manual smoke test: simulate a red CI run (or test against current state) and confirm the agent surfaces it.
- Mode behavior verified: off mode skips; light mode condenses; full mode surfaces full info.
- No regression in cold-start latency (gh call is ~1s; acceptable).

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-15 during loom-59w (P2 docs-link fix): the 2-day gap between CI going red and the user noticing was the real bug. loom-59w fixed the docs link; this bead addresses the meta-pattern.

## Anti-scope

- NOT a pre-push hook. Pre-push hooks would have caught loom-59w earlier but are narrow per-workflow. This bead is the broader fix.
- NOT a 'block session start on red CI' bead. Surfacing the failure is enough; the user decides whether to fix it or proceed.
- NOT a docs-link audit in bd preflight. Bd preflight is currently a Go-project-shaped list (per loom CLAUDE.md preflight output); cross-cutting CI awareness belongs in session-startup, not preflight.
close_reason: Shipped via commit d7c2754. Added step 1a to skills/session-startup/SKILL.md: 'gh run list --limit 3 --branch main' surfaces red CI from prior sessions; tolerant to gh-missing/unauthed; stays in light mode. 7-assertion fixture pins the contract; full suite 26/26 green; mkdocs --strict exits 0. Lineage: loom-59w surfaced the diagnostic gap, loom-97q is the broader fix. Path B (hook-backed) deferred until Path A misses in practice.

title: loom dogfood: remove duplicate bd prime hook registration from loom's own .claude/settings.json
description:
Live dogfood of loom-ann's find-hook-dups.sh against /home/frank/repos/loom surfaced two real duplicate hook registrations in loom's own .claude/settings.json:

```
WARN PreCompact "bd prime": registered in both /home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/settings.json AND /home/frank/.claude/plugins/cache/beads-marketplace/beads/0.49.3/.claude-plugin/plugin.json
WARN SessionStart "bd prime": registered in both /home/frank/repos/loom/.claude/settings.json AND /home/frank/.claude/plugins/cache/beads-marketplace/beads/0.49.3/.claude-plugin/plugin.json
```

The beads plugin's plugin.json already registers `bd prime` for both PreCompact and SessionStart. Loom's project-level settings.json re-registers them, firing each command twice per event.

## Fix

Remove the duplicate `bd prime` entries from loom's `.claude/settings.json` for both events. Keep any non-duplicate hooks in the same blocks intact (especially loom-installed wrappers like bd-prime-wrapper.sh if it's there — verify before removing).

## Acceptance

- bash scripts/find-hook-dups.sh /home/frank/repos/loom returns empty stdout
- Existing loom test suite stays green
- No regression in loom's own SessionStart / PreCompact behavior (bd prime + loom hooks still fire)

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-15 by loom-ann live dogfood. Pairs with loom-sd5 (same fix shape in liza_base, 2026-05-15).
close_reason: Removed duplicate bd prime SessionStart+PreCompact registrations from loom's .claude/settings.json per the loom-sd5 pattern. find-hook-dups detector now returns empty stdout. Commit b20a468. Drawer + KG triples filed in loom/decisions.

title: dispatched-agent pre-flight smoke battery should check base-freshness, not just rebase exit
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-15 by loom-b1l worker (ab9d792b52981371f).

## Symptom

The pre-flight rebase step in the dispatched-agent smoke battery (loom-g5k, .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md) runs:

```bash
git rebase main || { echo "FAIL: rebase failed — escalate"; exit 1; }
```

When the worker's branch has NO commits yet (fresh worktree, no work done), `git rebase main` is a NO-OP — it exits 0 even if the branch base is stale. The b1l worker's branch base was `303274d` while main had moved to `62aa08f` (4 commits ahead via loom-tat merge + loom-sd5 close). The rebase reported success; the staleness only surfaced when the worker compared `git diff --stat main HEAD` post-commit and saw UNRELATED files (loom-tat + loom-sd5 changes) showing in the diff.

The worker recovered via stash-bracketed explicit `git rebase main` post-commit. Clean recovery, but the pre-flight should have caught it.

## Root cause

`git rebase main` only does work when there are commits to replay. On a fresh worker branch with zero commits, the rebase is a no-op AND returns 0 even if the branch's merge-base trails main.

## Fix shape

Replace the rebase step in the pre-flight battery with an explicit base-freshness check + rebase combination:

```bash
# Base-freshness check (catches stale-base on empty-branch worker)
merge_base=$(git merge-base HEAD main)
main_tip=$(git rev-parse main)
if [ "$merge_base" != "$main_tip" ]; then
  echo "BASE STALE: branch base $merge_base, main $main_tip — rebasing"
  git rebase main || { echo 'FAIL: rebase failed — escalate'; exit 1; }
fi
```

This works whether the branch has commits (rebase replays them) or not (rebase is still a no-op but the message confirms the worker is now on a fresh base — and the rebase actually FORCES the branch tip to main when there are no commits).

Hmm, actually `git rebase main` on an empty branch DOES move HEAD to main when the branch has zero commits. So the issue isn't the rebase itself — it's the LACK of detection that the branch was stale to begin with. The base-freshness check tells the worker its starting point shifted, which is information the post-commit diff would otherwise hide.

## Scope

- Update `.claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md` pre-flight smoke battery section.
- Update any worker-brief templates in `skills/` / `commands/` / `agents/` that paste the smoke battery verbatim.
- Add a test case in `lib/tests/dispatched-agents-rule.test.sh` asserting the base-freshness language is present.

## Acceptance

- Rule prose updated.
- Test fixture asserts the check.
- Full loom shell-fixture suite stays green.

## Lineage

- Surfaced by loom-b1l (closed 2026-05-15) — worker's stale-base recovery
- Extends loom-g5k (closed 2026-05-15) — pre-flight smoke battery
- Complementary to loom-azt (closed 2026-05-12) — rebase WIP wrapper
- See drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<filed-for-b1l> (loom-b1l closing) for full context

Severity P3 — workers recover from this; rule discipline catches it post-commit. Mostly a hygiene improvement.
close_reason: Shipped via merge commit c251543 (worker commit 2bc8174). Adds step 4 (base-freshness check) to .claude/rules/dispatched-agents.md pre-flight smoke battery — explicit git merge-base HEAD main vs git rev-parse main comparison + auto-rebase. Catches the empty-branch git-rebase-main no-op surfaced by loom-b1l worker 2026-05-15. CLAUDE.md stale-base hygiene subsection updated to point at the battery, names the empty-branch failure mode, retains loom-rebase-worktree (loom-azt) for WIP preservation. Tests: 21 → 29 assertions in lib/tests/dispatched-agents-rule.test.sh (8 new, 0 regressions). Full loom shell-fixture suite: all test files exit 0. Lineage: extends loom-g5k (battery origin), surfaced by loom-b1l, complementary to loom-azt.

title: stale worktree base: convention + worker-brief template for rebase-onto-main
description:
Per loom-35b split (drawer pending): 'Agent({isolation: "worktree"})' doesn't always branch the worktree from latest main. In sessions with sequential dispatch waves, later worktrees may inherit a base from earlier in the session, missing intervening merges.

Observed in liza_base 2026-05-06:
- bqo's agent: worktree branched off pre-e5n; rebased onto local main to pick up VoiceSection.anchor_path; tests then GREEN.
- 9uo's retry agent: 5 commits behind main, rebased before working.
- 982's retry agent: similarly behind.

The harness behavior is opaque to loom (Claude Code's Agent tool creates the worktree at unknown ref). Loom can't directly control the base. So the fix is convention-only:

## Fix shape

1. **Update loom CLAUDE.md** with a 'Parallel worker dispatch' subsection noting the stale-base risk + the canonical mitigation: have the worker brief include a 'git rebase origin/main' (or main) as step 0.
2. **Update agent-dispatch worker-brief templates** (if any exist in skills/) to include the rebase-step boilerplate.
3. **Optionally**, ship a tiny 'scripts/loom-worktree-sync-main' helper that workers invoke at start: it rebases the worktree's branch onto current main, reporting commits picked up.

## Acceptance

- CLAUDE.md updated with the stale-base convention.
- Either: skill text references it, OR a helper script exists, OR both.
- (Optional) test fixture covers the helper script if shipped.

## Scope

Light — mostly doc + a small script. P3 because it's been worked around via per-worker rebases historically; mechanical fix prevents the next session from re-discovering.
notes: Split from loom-35b (3 symptoms). This addresses symptom 1 (stale base). Convention-only since harness behavior is not loom-controllable.
close_reason: Round 2 parallel dispatch SHIPPED. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_6c0857899831985259e2a877 covers all three. Merge commits: ee7d5e8 (0r6), 36a2ca7 (ecf), 3382360 (a82). Worker commits c8ebc42, 95fcb50, 52be361. 4 new tests for 0r6 (chained commit-then-push detection in git-push-bd-sync hook). ecf shipped CLAUDE.md stale-base hygiene convention pointing at the loom-azt wrapper. a82 trimmed 4 agent files 39% lines / 40% bytes (556→341 lines, 29473→17728 bytes); empirical savings ~11.7KB per subagent dispatch. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 18/18 files, 249 tests (was 245, +4 from 0r6), 0 failures. Two test-assertion failures during a82 trim caught and restored before commit by the worker.

title: harden bd-worktree-preseed: re-preseed when dolt is empty even if sentinel exists
description:
Per loom-35b split (drawer pending): the hooks/bd-worktree-preseed.sh hook from loom-x4m (merge 467c9e2) uses a sentinel file .beads/.loom-preseeded to memoize that pre-seed already ran. But there's a gap on rebase recovery:

If 'git stash -u' + rebase wipes the worktree's dolt DB while preserving the sentinel (e.g. stash drops untracked + rebase restores them but the dolt was in stashed-then-unstashed state), the hook sees sentinel-present and skips preseed → dolt stays empty → next bd write hits the empty-dolt bug again.

Observed in liza_base 2026-05-06 across wx7 + 982 + 9uo dispatch agents.

## Fix

Add a dolt-emptiness check to the hook's sentinel gate. Replace the simple sentinel check with: 'if sentinel exists AND dolt is non-empty → skip. Otherwise → preseed.'

Mechanism for emptiness check: 'bd ready --json' returns empty array, OR check if .beads/embeddeddolt/ has fewer than N files (e.g. <3 files = empty fresh dolt).

## Acceptance

- New test in lib/tests/bd-worktree-preseed.test.sh:
  - Sentinel present + dolt populated → no-op
  - Sentinel present + dolt EMPTY → re-preseed runs
  - Sentinel absent + dolt empty → preseed runs (existing case)
  - Sentinel absent + dolt populated → still runs preseed first time (existing case, redundant import is harmless)

## Scope

- hooks/bd-worktree-preseed.sh: ~15-line change
- lib/tests/bd-worktree-preseed.test.sh: new test cases
- docs/reference/bd-worktree-preseed.md: note re-preseed condition
notes: Split from loom-35b (3 symptoms). This addresses symptom 3 (bd db wipe on rebase) for the case where sentinel survives but dolt doesn't.
close_reason: Shipped via merge commit 00268ae on main (worker commit dee68b6 on frank/loom-8vc). Replaced sentinel-only check with sentinel+dolt-non-empty check in hooks/bd-worktree-preseed.sh — when sentinel exists but dolt has no files (the post-rebase scenario), hook falls through to the existing three-layer preseed, then re-touches the sentinel. Self-healing for the rebase-wipe path that wx7/982/9uo agents hit 2026-05-06. 2 new test cases (12/12 PASS in the suite, was 10/10). Pre-existing path-wrap bug in test stub fixed at the same time. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 17/17 files, 236 tests, 0 failures. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<filed-just-now> captures full design + remaining-cluster items. KG triples: loom-8vc -> closed_by_commit -> 00268ae; loom-8vc -> hardens -> loom-x4m. Cluster still has loom-azt (P2 WIP wrapper) and loom-ecf (P3 stale-base convention) open.

title: git-push-bd-sync hook: false positive when same Bash chains commit-then-push
description:
The hooks/git-push-bd-sync.sh PreToolUse hook fires when the Bash tool's command matches 'git push'. It checks 'git status --porcelain .beads/' at fire-time and warns if non-empty. But when the user chains 'git add .beads/issues.jsonl && git commit -m "..." && git pull --rebase=merges && bd dolt push && git push' in a single Bash call, the hook fires BEFORE the chained commit runs — so .beads/ IS dirty at fire-time, but the dirt will be committed-then-pushed within the same chain.

Observed 4x in the 2026-05-13 loom session. Misleading false positives add noise.

## Fix shape

Detect chained commit-then-push pattern: if the same CMD contains 'git commit' BEFORE 'git push' (with && or ; separators), silence the warning. Simplest implementation:

  if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|])[[:space:]]*git[[:space:]]+commit.*(&&|;)[[:space:]]*.*git[[:space:]]+push'; then
    exit 0
  fi

Place this guard after the existing --dry-run skip (line 31 of git-push-bd-sync.sh).

## Acceptance

- Single command 'git push' with dirty .beads/ still warns.
- Chained 'git add .beads/ && git commit && git push' is silent.
- Chained 'git add ... && git commit ... && bd dolt push && git push' is silent.
- Test fixture in lib/tests/git-push-bd-sync.test.sh covering both cases.
notes: Observed 4 times in 2026-05-13 session. Hook is correct at fire-time but unhelpful for the chained-commit pattern that the session-close protocol in loom CLAUDE.md actually recommends.

Worker commits c8ebc42, 95fcb50, 52be361. 4 new tests for 0r6 (chained commit-then-push detection in git-push-bd-sync hook). ecf shipped CLAUDE.md stale-base hygiene convention pointing at the loom-azt wrapper. a82 trimmed 4 agent files 39% lines / 40% bytes (556→341 lines, 29473→17728 bytes); empirical savings ~11.7KB per subagent dispatch. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 18/18 files, 249 tests (was 245, +4 from 0r6), 0 failures. Two test-assertion failures during a82 trim caught and restored before commit by the worker.

title: Audit + slim loom agent definitions to reduce per-dispatch system-prompt tokens
description:
Per loom-7zy findings (drawer pending): subagents are billed ~9-10K cached input tokens of system-prompt preamble per dispatch via the API system prompt slot (NOT via SessionStart hooks, which don't fire for sidechains in CC 2.1.112). The portion that loom CAN influence is each agent definition file in ~/.claude/agents/ — its content is included verbatim in the system prompt of every dispatch of that subagent_type.

## Current sizes (2026-05-13)

| File | Lines |
|---|---|
| bug-family-researcher.md | 97 |
| drawer-author.md | 144 |
| kg-relationship-extractor.md | 91 |
| project-onboarder.md | 224 |
| TOTAL | 556 |

project-onboarder.md is the heaviest at 224 lines (~9KB). If trimmed by 30-50%, saves ~3-4K bytes (~1K tokens) per project-onboarder dispatch.

## Scope

1. Read each agent file in agents/ and identify:
   - Redundant repetition of conventions already in loom CLAUDE.md
   - Overlong intro/preamble that doesn't shape behavior
   - Examples that exceed what's needed for the agent to do its job
2. Trim while preserving behavior — verify by dispatching test invocations and checking outputs match.
3. Measure before/after via cache_creation_input_tokens deltas in a fresh dispatch.

## Acceptance

- Each agent file reduced by 20-50% without behavioral regression.
- Empirical cache_creation_input_tokens drop measured + documented in close-reason.
- Test fixture (optional): record the system-prompt size produced by each agent for regression-pinning.
notes: Surfaced via loom-7zy investigation 2026-05-13. The 21KB SessionStart-prefix tax claim from loom-nsb was real but mis-attributed — it's the API system prompt slot (agent definitions + tools schema + harness baseline), not SessionStart hooks. Loom can only influence agent definition content.

Worker commits c8ebc42, 95fcb50, 52be361. 4 new tests for 0r6 (chained commit-then-push detection in git-push-bd-sync hook). ecf shipped CLAUDE.md stale-base hygiene convention pointing at the loom-azt wrapper. a82 trimmed 4 agent files 39% lines / 40% bytes (556→341 lines, 29473→17728 bytes); empirical savings ~11.7KB per subagent dispatch. Full loom shell-fixture suite: 18/18 files, 249 tests (was 245, +4 from 0r6), 0 failures. Two test-assertion failures during a82 trim caught and restored before commit by the worker.

title: Upstream bd PR: add --no-memories flag to bd prime
description:
loom-nc2 (2026-05-12) shipped a wrapper hook that post-processes bd prime output to cap the memories section (10.5x reduction on liza_base: 153KB → 14.6KB). Wrapper relies on text-pattern matching of section markers — brittle if bd prime output format ever changes.

Cleaner fix at the source: bd prime gains a --no-memories flag (or --memories-cap=N flag). Loom's wrapper shells out with --no-memories and skips Python post-processing entirely.

## Scope

1. File issue against the beads project (https://github.com/steveyegge/beads) requesting --no-memories flag.
2. Optionally draft + submit a PR (bd is small enough that this might be a 1-2 hour change).
3. Once shipped upstream + adopted in loom's bd install version, update hooks/bd-prime-wrapper.sh to use the flag, drop the Python post-processor.

## Acceptance

- Upstream issue filed with link.
- (Optional) PR submitted.
- Wrapper migration plan documented in docs/reference/bd-prime-wrapper.md (current doc already names this as a follow-up).
notes: Filed upstream: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3961 (2026-05-15). Body in /tmp/issue-45i.md at filing time. When upstream ships, migrate hooks/bd-prime-wrapper.sh to use the flag and drop the Python post-processor.
close_reason: Filed upstream as https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3961 (bd prime --no-memories / --memories-cap flag request). Acceptance per bead is 'upstream issue filed with link' — met. Wrapper migration deferred until upstream ships; that work is its own bead-shape (cleanup-a-bead) when triggered.

title: bd-close-capture regex widens to {3,} for 4+char bead IDs
description: Bead IDs in sibling projects (e.g. liza_base) can exceed 3 chars. The hook's regex [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3}(\.[0-9a-z]+)* fails to capture them on cross-repo bd close invocations, leading to no-evidence-check bypass behaviour for IDs that should be verified. Widen to [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3,}(\.[0-9a-z]+)* and add a regression test case.
notes: Surfaced during 2026-05-12 cold-start cleanup of loose changes on main. Frank confirmed widening is intentional ('some beads are 4 char codes'). Extends loom-8vb's regex fix (ab1a9de).
close_reason: Shipped via commit 951c6c7 on main. One-char regex widening in hooks/bd-close-capture.sh: [a-z][a-z0-9_]*-[0-9a-z]{3} -> {3,} so sibling-project bead IDs with 4+char hashes (e.g. liza_base) capture in cross-repo bd close invocations. Locked design drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628 (loom-8vb, 2026-05-06) still governs; this was an unstated gap in its 'shapes intentionally locked in' list (3-char + underscore-prefix + sub-suffix). New regression test in lib/tests/bd-close-capture.test.sh under the Bug-A scope group: 'bd close liza_base-abcd' now extracts the full ID. Test suite: 31/31 PASS (was 30/30). Bundled with two housekeeping items in the same commit: workflow.json light->full mode flip (loom is workflow-infrastructure, full earns its keep), and bd auto-export of issues.jsonl carrying loom-x4m + siblings filed on 2026-05-12.

title: trim bd prime output: cap bd memories auto-injected drawer narratives
description:
**From loom-nsb research (drawer_loom_decisions_3eec30046461f0766ac92eec).**

`bd prime` output is ~147 KB per fire in liza_base. Most of that is verbose drawer narratives auto-injected via `bd memories` keyword matches. Sample (5.4 MB liza_base transcript): bd prime fires 6× per session = ~900 KB before deduplication; even after the duplicate-registration fix is applied, each unique fire is still 147 KB.

## The cost

bd prime content sits in the SessionStart prefix and re-bills every turn until /clear. The bd workflow primer (~5 KB) is fine; the verbose memory drawer dumps are where the bloat is.

## Two paths

### A. Loom-side wrapper hook (loom scope)

Replace the direct `bd prime` SessionStart registration with a loom wrapper that:
- Calls bd prime
- Filters / caps the bd memories section (e.g. truncate each memory to 200 chars; cap total memories block at ~10 KB)
- Emits the trimmed output

Pro: doesn't depend on upstream beads cooperation. Con: brittle if bd prime output format changes.

### B. Upstream beads issue (out of loom scope)

File against the beads project: bd prime should support flags like `--memories-cap=10K` or `--no-memories-narrative` (titles only). loom installs would adopt the trimmed mode by default.

Pro: clean separation of concerns. Con: depends on beads team timeline.

## Recommendation

Start with A (loom wrapper) for immediate relief, then file B upstream and migrate to it when shipped. Document the wrapper as a temporary measure in its source.

## Estimated savings

5-10% of session bytes (post-duplicate-fix). Compounds with the duplicate-bd-prime fix.

## Acceptance

- Loom ships a bd-prime-wrapper.sh hook that produces a trimmed output.
- Test fixture: input = full bd prime output → wrapper output is ≤30 KB.
- Documented in docs/reference/.
- (Optionally) upstream issue filed against beads with link.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. Compounds with the duplicate-bd-prime liza_base fix bead — that one fixes WHERE bd prime fires, this one fixes WHAT bd prime emits.

Notable findings: w58 worker discovered CC 2.1.112 may not fire SessionStart hooks for sidechains (fix is still defensive); nc2 verified 10.5x reduction on liza_base (153KB to 14.6KB). Followups: investigate actual SessionStart-prefix injection point + file upstream bd --no-memories flag PR.

title: audit-project: detect duplicate plugin-registered hook commands across project + plugin settings
description:
**Preventive bead from loom-nsb research.**

The duplicate `bd prime` SessionStart + PreCompact registration in liza_base (sibling bead) is a config-level drift that loom's `/audit-project` should detect, so future projects don't accumulate the same waste.

## Detection logic

For each command registered in a project's `.claude/settings.json` hooks block:
- Cross-reference against commands registered by enabled plugins' `plugin.json` hooks blocks.
- If the same command (e.g. `bd prime`) is registered for the same hook event (e.g. `SessionStart`) at both layers, emit a [HOOK DUP] warning naming both registration sites and the recommended fix (drop the project-level dup).

## Scope

- Extend existing `/audit-project` SKILL.md with new check (call it Check 5 or insert into hook-related checks).
- Read project's `.claude/settings.json` hooks tree.
- Enumerate enabled plugins via `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/*/claude-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (or the plugin-resolution path Claude Code actually uses).
- Compare command + matcher + hook-event tuples; flag exact duplicates.

## Acceptance

- Check fires on liza_base (today's known case) and surfaces the bd-prime dup.
- Check is silent on a clean project (no duplicates).
- Test fixture: synthetic project with a duplicated hook → check warns.
- Documented in audit-project's SKILL.md with the dup-pattern explanation.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. Preventive: catches the duplicate-bd-prime pattern in future projects. Pairs with the liza_base-specific fix bead.
close_reason: Shipped via merge commit 0c4406a (worker commit 54831e3). Adds project-onboarder item 12 (Claude Code hook command duplicates) backed by deterministic detector scripts/find-hook-dups.sh. Project-level dups → WARN; user-level dups → INFO (machine-specific advisory). No AUTOFIX tag — content-aware JSON surgery excluded by loom-a29 Wave 2 contract. Tests: 16 new fixture cases in lib/tests/find-hook-dups.test.sh + 17 doc-presence assertions appended to lib/tests/audit-apply-flags.test.sh (57→74); full loom suite (25 files) all exit 0. Implementation detail: \x1f field separator (POSIX read+IFS=\t collapses empty matchers). Live dogfood: detects 2 real WARN dups in loom's own settings.json (PreCompact + SessionStart 'bd prime' vs beads plugin) — follow-up bead to clean up loom-side dup. Lineage: extends loom-a29 (AUTOFIX contract), surfaced by loom-nsb research, pairs with loom-sd5 (liza_base live fix).

title: LOOM_SUBAGENT_LEAN=1 env-signal opt-out for slim SessionStart
description:
Companion to the slim-SessionStart-for-subagents bead. Apps that spawn ephemeral Claude Code helpers (like liza_base's per-tool voice translator) should be able to opt explicitly into the slim shape, in case the isSidechain heuristic misclassifies their use case.

Add support for env var `LOOM_SUBAGENT_LEAN=1` which forces the loom SessionStart hooks into slim mode regardless of detected subagent context. Lets app code wrap subprocess Claude Code invocations with the env var set.

## Scope

- workflow-mode-onboarding.sh: read $LOOM_SUBAGENT_LEAN, treat as if isSidechain=true.
- Document the env var in docs/reference/ (new page or extend existing hook docs).
- Test fixture: env-var-set + non-subagent input → emits slim preamble.

Low-priority escape hatch; the main fix is heuristic-driven detection.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 via loom-nsb research. Companion to the slim-SessionStart-for-subagents bead.

title: docs-scaffold next-steps line 269 names wrong GH Pages source for mkdocs gh-deploy workflow
description:
The /docs-scaffold 'Next steps' summary at skills/docs-scaffold/SKILL.md:269 tells the user to set GitHub Pages 'Source = GitHub Actions'. But the scaffolded workflow at templates/diataxis/.github/workflows/docs.yml uses 'mkdocs gh-deploy --force --no-history', which pushes the built site to a gh-pages branch. The correct repo setting for that deploy path is 'Source = Deploy from a branch -> gh-pages', NOT 'GitHub Actions'. As written, a user following the next-steps text verbatim will end up with a non-publishing site.

Two fix options:
A. Update line 269 text to 'Source = Deploy from a branch -> gh-pages (one-time setup)'.
B. Switch the workflow to use actions/deploy-pages (which IS the 'Source = GitHub Actions' path) and keep line 269 as-is.

Option A is the smaller change. Option B aligns with GitHub's currently-recommended Pages deploy mechanism (Pages site as deployment target, no gh-pages branch sprawl). Pick one before fixing.
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-09 during a question about whether loom auto-publishes scaffolded docs. The automation IS in place — only the next-steps text is wrong.

title: dispatch operational: API 529 overload kills mid-flight + resume agents; resilience playbook needed
description:
When the Anthropic API is overloaded (529), parallel-dispatched agents hit the failure mode at three different stages:

1. **Mid-flight crash** — agent has done substantial work, hits a 529 on a Sonnet call, dies. Worktree filesystem state preserved; bd state may be in transit.
2. **Resume crash** — orchestrator dispatches a resume agent, which dies in 4 seconds with 0 tool uses because the API is too sick to even start. Resume can't begin until API health recovers.
3. **Sustained outage** — multiple agents in a wave can crash within seconds of each other. The orchestrator detects 3 crashes and stops dispatching to avoid burning context.

## Concrete case (liza_base 2026-05-06)

- All 3 Wave C agents (wx7, 982, 9uo) crashed on a 529 burst.
- The 9uo resume agent died in 4s with 0 tool uses on the next dispatch attempt.
- wx7 actually came back successfully on a subsequent independent retry.

## Implications

The harness routes ALL agent calls through the same Anthropic API backend. There is no 'go around' path; subprocess `claude` CLI from agents hits the same backend. When overload sustains, the entire parallel-dispatch flow is wedged.

## Proposed playbook (loom-level)

1. **Detect API overload** — when ≥2 agents in a wave crash with 529 within a short window, surface a 'API health pause' state to the orchestrator. Don't keep dispatching agents that immediately die.
2. **Preserve WIP** — when an agent crashes, capture its worktree filesystem state into a recovery surface (loom-managed) so resume agents can rebuild without depending on rebase choreography preserving untracked files.
3. **Resume-from-WIP recipe** — formalize the 'check what previous agent left, verify it, finish what's left' shape into a recipe that resume briefs can reference. Today this is ad-hoc per case.
4. **Decision-drawer-as-recovery-surface** — promote 'file decision drawer FIRST' from convention to recipe-mandatory. The drawer's body should be detailed enough to rebuild the implementation; it's the only crash-resilient artifact.

## Sibling concern

Once API health returns, mid-flight resume timing is delicate. Resume too early: dies again. Resume too late: untracked WIP grows stale relative to main. loom could add an exponential-backoff retry loop OR explicit 'health probe before resume' step.

## Provenance

Surfaced 2026-05-06 during liza_base FRH Wave C dispatch — API 529 burst killed 3 simultaneous agents.

title: loom-guest: how-to docs page
description:
Goal: Diataxis how-to guide for guest mode in loom's own docs site.

Files:
- docs/how-to/guest-mode.md (new)
- docs/mkdocs.yml or nav config — add the new page

Outline:
- When to use guest mode (the two scenarios from Q1: socially shouldn't pollute, or temporary engagement).
- How to activate: /loom-guest on, including the bd-mode prompt walkthrough.
- What changes (storage layout table from Section 2 of design).
- What's refused (docs-scaffold, audit AUTOFIX, bd remember in host bd).
- How to deactivate.
- Troubleshooting (statusline doesn't show [GUEST], info/exclude not picking up, etc.).

Voice: imperative how-to, not tutorial. Example sample command sequence; cross-link to the explanation page (if/when one exists).

Steps:
1. Sample sibling how-to docs in docs/how-to/ to match voice.
2. Draft the page.
3. Verify against the locked design (drawer_loom_decisions_12d7f8163e8855be037a007c).
4. Add to mkdocs nav.
5. Build docs locally if there's a build step; otherwise visual review.

Acceptance: page renders correctly; covers the design accurately; matches sibling-doc voice.

Independent — can run in parallel with implementation tasks.

title: loom-guest: drawer-author guest-project prefix
description:
Goal: When guest mode is active, drawer-author agent should prefix the leading sentence of every drawer it drafts with "[guest project: <repo>]" so future sessions can tell guest-mode decisions apart from owned-project decisions.

Files:
- agents/drawer-author.md (system prompt update)

Steps:
1. Add a check at the start of the drawer-author prompt: "If workflow-state get guest.active returns true, look up guest.repo_key and prefix the drawer's leading sentence with [guest project: <repo_key>]."
2. Add an example illustrating the prefix output.
3. Manually smoke-test: invoke drawer-author inside a guest-mode repo, verify prefix; invoke in non-guest, verify no prefix.

Acceptance: Drawer drafted in guest mode starts with the prefix; drawer drafted in non-guest mode does not.

Depends on: task 2 (workflow-state guest fields).
close_reason: Closed

title: audit-project Check 2a script: 'every bd-ID shows as dead' is a regex bug, not real drift
description:
During loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05 verification phase, the parallel agent ran '/audit-project --check=docs' as the e63 acceptance check. Check 2a (bd-ID resolution — does each bead-ID cited in docs/ resolve via 'bd show'?) initially reported every ID as 'dead'. The agent flagged this directly: 'every ID showing as "dead" looks suspicious' and re-implemented with simpler logic. Result: 0/49 dead — the original was a script bug.

The agent's commentary on the fix suggests the original script used some kind of complex regex or output-parsing that broke on the liza_base bd-prefix shape ('liza_base-XXX' instead of just 'XXX' or a project-less form). The fix used 'simpler logic' that worked.

Required investigation:
1. Find the audit-project Check 2a implementation.
2. Determine what was breaking on liza_base's bd-IDs but worked on tla-puzzles' (where the trial didn't surface this bug).
3. Replace with the agent's working approach (or write a fresh implementation that handles all bd-ID formats).
4. Add a test fixture covering at least: project-prefixed IDs ('liza_base-e63', 'tla-puzzles-bwv'), bare IDs ('77oy'), and mixed.

Severity: P3 — false-positive 'every ID dead' result is alarming but the agent caught it by sanity check ('looks suspicious'). A non-vigilant agent might have proceeded to file noise as drift. Worth fixing.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions, audit summary section). Trial: loom-wxo.
close_reason: Closed

title: bd post-commit hook re-export loop: each commit triggers another export-needing commit
description:
Extension of loom-cka. After 'bd hooks install' and the absorbing-commit pattern (which loom-cka added to project-onboarder docs), there's a deeper pattern: every git commit triggers bd's pre-commit hook to re-export .beads/issues.jsonl, which then needs ANOTHER commit to absorb. The loop:

1. User commits something logical (Commit A).
2. bd's pre-commit hook re-exports .beads/issues.jsonl (post-staging diff appears).
3. User commits the export reconcile (Commit B = 'chore(beads): post-X bd export reconcile').
4. bd's pre-commit hook re-exports AGAIN (post-staging diff appears).
5. User commits another reconcile (Commit C = 'chore(beads): post-Y bd export reconcile').
6. ... repeats until user gives up

In the loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05, the agent went 3 layers deep before stopping with the comment: 'The bd post-commit hook re-exports issues.jsonl after every commit — chasing the loop with another commit just retriggers it. Stopping here; the drift is pure metadata reordering and the next bd-related commit will fold it in naturally.'

That's the right call from the agent's POV but it leaves uncommitted state that future agents will pick up + commit, rinse, repeat across sessions.

Possible fixes:
(a) bd pre-commit hook should detect 'is this commit a pure issues.jsonl export with no semantic delta from the dolt store?' and skip the re-export when so. Stops the loop at its source.
(b) bd post-commit hook offers a 'reconcile_mode=batch' option that defers re-export to N seconds after the LAST commit in a batch (heuristic: 'session is still active'). User-side but eliminates the rapid-fire loop.
(c) Wrapping all bd-related commits in a single 'bd flush' command that does the export ONCE at the end of a logical batch. Manual but explicit.

Severity: P3 — annoyance, not correctness. The drift is pure metadata reordering; the bd database is consistent. But it makes git history noisy with 3-5 'chore(beads): post-X bd export reconcile' commits per logical session-close.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions, post-close section). Trial: loom-wxo. Pattern observed in liza_base/main commit chain: 4879035 → 8123836 → aed8096.

Extends: loom-cka (which fixed the FIRST commit case via absorbing-commit guidance; this bead is the LOOPING case).
notes: Filed upstream: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3963 (2026-05-15). Three fix options presented; downstream preference (a) silent no-semantic-delta skip.
close_reason: Filed upstream as https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3963 (post-commit re-export loop). Three fix options presented to upstream; downstream preference (a) no-semantic-delta skip. Loom-side action (file upstream) complete.

title: bd-close hook can't see just-filed drawer/KG/diary artifacts; capture-then-close fails timing race
description:
The bd-close-capture hook (PreToolUse on 'bd close') verifies that drawer + KG + diary artifacts were captured before allowing the close. In the loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-05, the parallel session:

1. Dispatched drawer-author + kg-relationship-extractor agents in parallel
2. Both returned solid artifacts (drawer + 10 KG triples + diary delta)
3. Filed all artifacts via mempalace_add_drawer / mempalace_kg_add / mempalace_diary_write
4. Ran 'bd close liza_base-e63'
5. Hook BLOCKED — couldn't see the artifacts despite them being filed seconds earlier
6. Agent bypassed with BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1

The race: the hook's freshness check probably queries MemPalace's index/cache, which hasn't refreshed in the few-second window between filing and closing.

Possible fixes:
(a) Hook explicitly waits for index refresh before checking (e.g., poll for up to 5s, or call a 'flush index' MCP method first).
(b) Hook accepts artifacts filed in the current session (use a more direct mechanism than MemPalace's search index — e.g., look at the drawer files on disk under <palace>/<wing>/<room>/).
(c) Hook accepts BD_CLOSE_FORCE=1 silently (current escape hatch) but emits a warning telling the user to retry without the override after 30s. Doesn't fix the race; just makes the override less feel-bad.

Severity: P3 — the BD_CLOSE_FORCE escape hatch works, but every well-behaved capture-then-close session hits this race. Two trials, two manual workarounds. The hook is doing the right thing (verifying capture) but its check is timing-fragile.

Source: drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (liza_base/decisions). Trial: loom-wxo.
close_reason: Superseded by loom-8vb. The race-condition diagnosis (MemPalace index/cache lag) was wrong: the old hook performed no MemPalace query at all, so there was no race — just an unconditional block. loom-8vb (drawer drawer_loom_decisions_2fbf2d5f4c0f5e50ab84e628; commits ab1a9de, f71b631) ships real 5-matcher artifact verification via direct SQLite reads (race-free by construction), which resolves the same trial symptom. See drawer_loom_decisions_791d132361dd2875f5555e71 for the close-out.

title: audit-project: extend AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees to also append .claude/workflow-state.json
description:
The Wave 2 AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees recipe (loom-a29) only appends .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore. But the loom session itself creates .claude/workflow-state.json at every session start (per-session ephemeral state); without a gitignore entry, it shows up as untracked at every audit and gets accidentally committed.

Observed pattern across BOTH customer trials:
- tla-puzzles (loom-b6o): had to add .claude/workflow-state.json to .gitignore as a separate commit (8f6df65 'chore: gitignore .claude/workflow-state.json (per-session ephemeral)')
- liza_base (loom-wxo): same need; same separate commit recommended

Both trials caught this manually. The AUTOFIX recipe should handle it.

Required fix: extend AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees to append BOTH:
- .claude/worktrees/   (existing)
- .claude/workflow-state.json   (new)

Both should be idempotent appends (skip if line already present). Consider renaming the recipe to AUTOFIX:gitignore-loom-ephemera to reflect the broader scope.

Severity: P3 ergonomics. Not a correctness bug — humans easily catch+add the missing line — but the AUTOFIX recipe exists specifically to eliminate this manual step.

Source: loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04 + loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. Both trials encountered this same gap. Drawer: drawer_loom_decisions_1101f772a4ac7660a0f45aa1 (Wave 2 close-out).
close_reason: Shipped via merge commit 10150b7 (worker 1a037c1) on main. AUTOFIX:gitignore-worktrees recipe extended to fold .claude/workflow-state.json alongside .claude/worktrees/. Per-line idempotent append. Recipe-id kept stable (rename blast radius exceeded cap). 57/0 tests pass post-merge. Closing drawer drawer_loom_decisions_<filed-this-call>.

title: audit-project: agent drafted+applied .claude/rules/tests.md despite Wave 2 explicitly excluding rules content from AUTOFIX
description:
Wave 2 (loom-a29) close-out drawer (drawer_loom_decisions_1101f772a4ac7660a0f45aa1) explicitly listed '.claude/rules/ content (content choice)' as EXCLUDED from AUTOFIX. The intent: humans should review/author rule content because it encodes project conventions.

In the loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04, the audit treated the missing .claude/rules/tests.md as a fixable item, drafted content from the project's canonical 'V0 TESTING DISCIPLINE' MemPalace drawer + phenomenology-audit lock, and wrote the file — all without explicit human authorship/review.

Two readings:
(a) Nice initiative — the agent has full context to draft well-informed rule content from the canonical source, much better than a generic template. The existing exclusion was overly conservative.
(b) Scope creep — the rule encodes project conventions; humans should at minimum review (preferably author) the text. AUTOFIX should not silently land it.

Decision needed:
1. (a) → expand AUTOFIX scope to include 'draft .claude/rules/<file>.md from canonical MemPalace source if pointed at one'. Add a recipe-id like AUTOFIX:rules-from-drawer with an explicit drawer parameter. Document.
2. (b) → tighten the audit-project skill text to forbid drafting rules content as part of AUTOFIX; make it always emit as a MISS that requires human action.

Recommendation: probably (b) for safety, with a future-bead for (a) once we have a clear pattern of which drawer-types map cleanly to which rule files.

Severity: P3 — depends on the loom-wxo AUTOFIX-bypass investigation (loom-tbd). If the per-item gate had worked, the user would have approved the draft, and this scope-creep concern would be moot. So this bead may be a no-op once the gate is fixed.

Source: loom-wxo liza_base trial 2026-05-04.

title: loom session-close: 'bd dolt push' no-remote should not read as failure
description:
Loom's session-close protocol (in loom CLAUDE.md and the bead-lifecycle-shell skill) runs 'bd dolt push' as part of finalizing work. In tla-puzzles (and presumably any solo bd workspace), 'bd dolt push' exits 1 with the message:

  Error: push to origin/main: Error 1105: fatal: remote 'origin' not found.
  Please make sure the remote exists.
  No remote is configured for this database.
  For solo use, pushing is optional — your issues are stored locally
  in .beads/ and versioned by Dolt automatically.

The error message itself confirms this is benign for solo workspaces. But the loom protocol surfaces it as a failure to fix. Observed in loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04.

Possible fixes (pick one):
(a) Loom CLAUDE.md / bead-lifecycle-shell: run 'bd dolt remote' first; if no remote configured, skip the push step with a one-line note ('solo bd workspace; skipping bd dolt push').
(b) Wrap in shell: 'bd dolt push 2>&1 | grep -v "remote .origin. not found" || true'.
(c) Add a 'bd dolt push --solo-ok' (or '--ignore-no-remote') flag and use it in loom guidance.

(a) is the most explicit; (b) is brittle (depends on error text); (c) is a bd change, not loom.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_dfb5ece53785610c83ca6619 (loom/decisions, friction signal F). Trial: loom-b6o.

Severity: P3 ergonomics — not a real failure, but creates noise in session-close output and may cause future agents to retry/fix when no action is needed.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: bd pre-commit hook: first commit after install merges issue-export churn into unrelated changes
description:
Reproduction: after running 'bd hooks install' in a project that has uncommitted bd issue-state changes, the very next 'git commit' (regardless of subject) auto-stages '.beads/issues.jsonl' (re-exported by bd's pre-commit hook) into the commit alongside whatever the user actually intended.

Observed in loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial 2026-05-04: the user split fixes into 3 logical commits (cardinality / loom-adoption / gitignore-cleanup). The first commit (cardinality, 891d2ae) silently picked up a +6-line .beads/issues.jsonl re-export that the user did not intend in that scope. Two-things-in-one-commit against intent.

Possible fixes (pick one):
(a) 'bd hooks install' should run a pre-staging sync before completing — clear the export-pending queue so the first user commit is clean.
(b) Loom workflow guidance / project-onboarder checklist warns: 'after bd hooks install, run an empty bd-only commit (or sync) before any logical commit, to absorb the auto-export'.
(c) bd's pre-commit hook should detect 'is the user actively staging non-bd files?' and defer the auto-export to a separate commit when so.

(a) is the cleanest from the user's POV. (b) is a doc-only fix. (c) is a behavior change to bd itself, not loom.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_dfb5ece53785610c83ca6619 (loom/decisions, friction signal E). Trial: loom-b6o.

Severity: P3 ergonomics — doesn't break anything, but produces commits with mixed scope that are hard to revert cleanly.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: audit-project: implement --apply-onboarding flag for one-line MISS items
description:
The /audit-project skill produces MISS items that are one-line bash to fix (bd hooks install; write {"v":1,"mode":"full"} to .claude/workflow.json; append .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore). Today the user must approve+execute each one in a follow-up message.

Implement --apply-onboarding as a flag on /audit-project that:
- only applies MISS items with deterministic one-command fixes (bd hooks install; create workflow.json with default mode=full; append known entries to .gitignore)
- prompts (or skips with --yes) for any MISS item that has a real choice (e.g. workflow.json mode=light vs full — surface both as options and let user pick)
- never touches WARN items (those imply real conflict — dirty tree, etc.)
- prints what was applied for review
- leaves git in dirty state

Analogous in spirit to the --apply-trivial flag (separate bead). Pair them so a clean fresh-project audit can land most onboarding + cardinality fixes in one approved sweep, leaving only the genuinely-judgmental items for human discussion.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #4).
Trial: loom-b6o (3 MISS items: bd hooks install + workflow.json + .gitignore append).
close_reason: Merged in wave-2 batch (commit on main 2026-05-04 via merge of frank/loom-wave2-apply-flags). Adds --apply-trivial + --apply-onboarding flags to /audit-project with 39 new bats tests. Determinism heuristics: TRIVIAL for cardinality count drifts + unambiguous dead-bead-ID supersessions; AUTOFIX for bd-hooks (with loom-cka absorbing commit), workflow-json (default mode=full, --workflow-mode override), gitignore-worktrees (idempotent append). Excluded: bd init (interactive), MemPalace wing creation, CLAUDE.md authoring, all WARN items. 73/73 total tests green. Wave-2 complete; loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now fully unblocked.

title: audit-project: implement --apply-trivial flag for deterministic doc fixes
description:
The /audit-project skill's report literally suggests '--apply-trivial' as a way to auto-apply the deterministic doc fixes it found ('With --apply-trivial I can apply the count corrections automatically'). The flag does not exist — it's referenced as a future capability that's leaking into output.

Cardinality drifts are exactly the deterministic case for this:
- skill knows the file path, line number, and exact substitution
- substitution is mechanical (105 → 106, Prelude (4) → Prelude (5))
- no human judgment needed for the change itself

Implement --apply-trivial as a flag on /audit-project that:
- only applies [DOC FIX] items the skill marks as deterministic (cardinality count drifts; trivially-resolvable broken links to renamed-but-extant files; etc.)
- skips anything ambiguous (factual claims, behavior descriptions)
- prints what was applied for review
- leaves git in dirty state for user to commit/revert

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #3).
Trial: loom-b6o (3 cardinality drifts in docs/index.md + 3 mirror drifts in README.md = 6 substitutions that should have been one flag away).
close_reason: Merged in wave-2 batch (commit on main 2026-05-04 via merge of frank/loom-wave2-apply-flags). Adds --apply-trivial + --apply-onboarding flags to /audit-project with 39 new bats tests. Determinism heuristics: TRIVIAL for cardinality count drifts + unambiguous dead-bead-ID supersessions; AUTOFIX for bd-hooks (with loom-cka absorbing commit), workflow-json (default mode=full, --workflow-mode override), gitignore-worktrees (idempotent append). Excluded: bd init (interactive), MemPalace wing creation, CLAUDE.md authoring, all WARN items. 73/73 total tests green. Wave-2 complete; loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now fully unblocked.

title: Pick canonical room for audit-project results: <wing>/decisions vs <wing>/audit_results
description:
The /audit-project skill auto-saved its findings drawer to a NEW room tla_puzzles/audit_results during loom-b6o (2026-05-04). Loom self-audits historically file findings to loom/decisions. Today the convention drifts project-by-project — the skill creates whichever room it thinks fits.

Pick one convention and enforce it in the skill:
(a) audits go to <wing>/audit_results — separates audit findings from design decisions; pro: easier to filter/sweep audits later. Con: another room to remember; doesn't reuse the decisions room which is the natural home for 'this is what we found, here's what we should do'.
(b) audits go to <wing>/decisions — reuses existing convention; pro: one less room; audits ARE decisions about project state. Con: dilutes the decisions room with state snapshots.

Once decided, update the skill to hardcode the room choice and update existing drift (the new tla_puzzles/audit_results drawer may need to migrate or be left as historical).

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #2).
Trial: loom-b6o.
design:
DECISION (2026-05-04): option (b) — audit-derived drawers go to <wing>/decisions. Reuse the existing decisions room rather than create separate audit rooms.

Rationale:
- Decisions room already exists in every loom-managed project per convention. Zero new rooms.
- Audits ARE decisions about project state; every useful audit comes with recommendations, making them functionally 'we figured out X; here is what to do' content.
- Eliminates room proliferation (tla-puzzles trial created BOTH audit_results AND findings in one session — the same skill, no convention enforcement, two rooms).
- Easier discoverability — users know to look in decisions for 'what did we figure out about this project.'

Implementation:
- Update /audit-project skill to hardcode <wing>/decisions as the destination for all audit-derived drawers.
- Drawer title/content carries the 'this is an audit' semantic (e.g., 'Project audit: <project> (<date>)'). Room name does not encode the distinction.

Migration of existing trial drawers (3 in tla_puzzles wing): file as separate hygiene bead (P4 backlog). Existing drawers in audit_results / findings rooms remain as historical artifacts; the new convention applies to all future audits.
notes: 2026-05-04 update from same loom-b6o trial: the parallel session created a SECOND room in the same wing (tla_puzzles/findings) for the docs-generation meta-finding, in addition to tla_puzzles/audit_results created earlier in the same session. Same skill, same wing, two different rooms — the convention drift is worse than first reported. Whatever decision lands needs to enforce ONE room name across all audit-derived drawers (findings, results, gaps, etc. should not all become separate rooms).
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: audit-project: formalize or scope-out [DOC FIX][MIRROR] tag for root README
description:
The /audit-project skill emits a [DOC FIX][MIRROR] tag for drift in root README.md when README mirrors docs/index.md content, even though the skill's documented scope is docs/. Surfaced during loom-b6o (tla-puzzles trial, 2026-05-04) when 3 cardinality drifts in docs/index.md had identical mirror drifts in README.md and the skill proactively reported them.

Decision needed:
(a) Formalize MIRROR support — add a configurable list of files outside docs/ that mirror docs content (README.md is the canonical case), and document the tag in the skill's reference page.
(b) Scope strictly to docs/ — strip the MIRROR proactivity from the skill, document README-as-known-blind-spot, and let users run a separate root-readme-drift check.

Today the behavior is emergent and undocumented — different invocations may or may not produce MIRROR depending on what the model notices. Pick one and make it deterministic.

Source: drawer_loom_decisions_598a609fc3e29b3c5937965f (loom/decisions, friction signal #1).
Trial: loom-b6o.
design:
DECISION (2026-05-04): option (c) reframe — drop the MIRROR tag concept entirely; expand /audit-project scope to 'all documentation files at the project root AND docs/' as one flat set. README.md is a sibling doc, not a mirror.

Default scope:
- README.md, README.rst, README.txt at project root
- docs/**/*.md (existing scope)

Out of default scope (excluded):
- AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md — agent-instructions, not user-docs (config-extendable later if needed)
- .github/*, package metadata files

Behavior change: drift in README reports as [DOC FIX] with no qualifier; the tag MIRROR is removed. Project-onboarder + audit-project skill reference docs need updating to reflect new scope.

Naturally handles loom-qp0/loom-3hb generated-docs case: when docs/ is gitignored and generated from README, audit reports drift in README (the source) without mirror-detection logic. The drift just appears there because README is in scope.

Known tradeoff (defer to a future bead, not blocking): if a project has README.md for users + docs/index.md for users with intentionally different content, audit will report 'drift' between them when there's no drift, just intentional divergence. Solve when encountered.
close_reason: Merged in wave-1 close-out batch (commits 3591eb2, d2bd6c4, 85acb1b on main 2026-05-04). All P2 correctness + P3 ergonomics fixes from loom-b6o tla-puzzles trial. 34/34 bats tests green. New shared lib/docs-generated.sh detector used by audit-project + docs-scaffold. project-onboarder.md item-3 manually composed to combine j4r structural fix with cka MISS remediation. Follow-up beads filed: loom-yxn (P4 bd upstream defer-on-mixed-stage), loom-h4u (P4 historical drawer migration). Wave 2 (loom-8hg + loom-a29) and loom-wxo (3rd-customer trial) now unblocked.

title: 3rd-customer trial: full loom setup for liza_base
description:
Full loom-managed setup + Diataxis-as-template adoption for ~/repos/liza_base, run as a fresh-session third-customer trial of the km8.6 pattern.

Source-of-truth: loom-km8.6 closing drawer (drawer_loom_decisions_4d49e7555dbbcbf52496539d in loom/decisions). Trial process learned there:

- Round 1: discover (audit + scaffold dry-run, capture friction, file follow-up beads, revert).
- Round 2: validate (re-trial after fixes, decide keep-vs-revert).

Pre-flight observations from this filing:
- ~/repos/liza_base has .beads/ but NO .claude/workflow.json — not loom-managed yet. Full setup means: bd hooks install (if missing), .claude/workflow.json (mode=full likely), .claude/rules/ scaffold for any detected source dirs, MemPalace wing check (liza_base wing already exists with 105 drawers per the round-2 wing scan), then /audit-project → /docs-scaffold.
- docs/ has 5 flat .md files (file-edit-containment.md, liza-voice.md, seed-pool-pataphor-inventory.md, setup.md, v2-design.md). No quadrants, no plans/research/ subdirs. This is the cleanest test of the km8.5 migration cheat-sheet: setup.md → how-to/, file-edit-containment.md / liza-voice.md / v2-design.md → explanation/, seed-pool-pataphor-inventory.md → reference/. Validate the cheat-sheet routes liza_base's actual filenames sensibly.
- The wing slug 'liza_base' exists alongside several 'liza' variants (liza, wing_liza, liza-live, liza_live). The loom-p1l [WING WARN] should fire (liza has 6556 drawers — vastly larger). User will need to decide whether liza_base is intentionally separate from liza or should consolidate at audit time.

Steps (per km8.6 pattern):
1. Open fresh session in ~/repos/liza_base (NOT from loom).
2. Confirm bd workspace is liza_base's own; confirm wing.
3. Run /audit-project — capture onboarding gaps + the WING WARN.
4. Apply onboarding fixes per project-onboarder checklist (with user approval).
5. Run /docs-scaffold — capture friction at M1-M6, with attention to flat-docs M3 case 3 (merge typical answer).
6. Review proposed diff. Don't commit unless user approves.
7. File loom-side follow-up beads for any new friction.
8. User decides: keep liza_base scaffold (commit) or revert.
9. Capture decision drawer in liza_base/decisions wing if kept; cross-wing tunnel to loom/decisions.
10. If user chooses to consolidate liza_base into liza wing instead, file a separate bead for the wing-merge work — out of scope for this trial.

Acceptance:
- Fresh-session execution (not driven from loom)
- Friction audit drawer landed (loom/decisions for trial drawer; liza_base/decisions for adoption drawer if kept)
- 0+ follow-up beads filed in loom for new friction
- User decision recorded
- KG triple recorded if adopted: 'liza_base → adopted → loom-diataxis-docs-template'

Lineage: this is the 3rd customer after HAW (km8.6) and tla-puzzles (parallel filing). With three customers, the trial pattern itself can be evaluated for whether it's still surfacing friction or settling into noise — a meta-observation worth capturing in the closing drawer.
notes:
2026-05-04 loom-3hb fix: step 5 ('Run /docs-scaffold') is now CONDITIONAL.

Updated trial-protocol step 5 reads:
  5. Run /docs-scaffold UNLESS docs/ is generated. /docs-scaffold itself
     now refuses with [SCAFFOLD SKIP][GENERATED] when the shared
     detector (lib/docs-generated.sh) classifies docs/ as generated
     (gitignored or built by script). In that case capture the SKIP
     output as the only friction signal for step 5 and proceed to
     step 6.

This protects against the loom-b6o tla-puzzles failure mode where
running scaffold would have written template files erased on the
next build.

Pre-flight check for liza_base: docs/ has 5 flat .md files, no
.gitignore docs/ entry visible, no scripts/build-docs.sh — the
detector should NOT classify it as generated, so step 5 will run
normally. But run the detector explicitly before assuming.
close_reason: 3rd-customer trial complete + soft-adopted via 7 commits to liza_base on 2026-05-04 and 2026-05-05; first push to github.com:fkberthold/liza_base at aed8096. Day 1: /audit-project + 3 commits + e63 bead filed for Diataxis adoption. Day 2: e63 full docs-a-bead execution → scaffold + 5 git mv + ~96-file cross-ref sweep + verify (846 tests + mkdocs --strict + audit-project --check=docs all clean) + merge + push. Adoption drawer drawer_liza_base_decisions_cbab33a93655a34b50ab0390 (canonical decisions room ✓). 11 KG triples filed by parallel session. Loom trial drawer drawer_loom_decisions_5f08db3b355976f4dd73a5cc tunneled to adoption drawer. 8 friction beads filed total this trial: loom-xcw (P2 AUTOFIX gate), loom-d50 (P3 rules.md scope creep), loom-tat (P3 workflow-state gitignore), loom-c9z (P2 _→- normalization), loom-4iu (P2 Signal 2a FP), loom-2xh (P3 bd-close timing race), loom-5b2 (P3 bd post-commit loop, extends loom-cka), loom-6m8 (P3 Check 2a script bug). Wave 1+2 fixes mostly validated end-to-end; loom-qp0/loom-3hb foundation (docs-generated detector) needs tightening per loom-4iu. KG: liza_base loom-managed true (2026-05-05). Three customer trials now complete (HAW + tla-puzzles + liza_base); friction rate decreasing per-trial as universal patterns get fixed.

title: 2nd-customer trial: full loom setup for tla-puzzles
description:
Full loom-managed setup + Diataxis-as-template adoption for ~/repos/tla-puzzles, run as a fresh-session second-customer trial of the km8.6 pattern.

Source-of-truth: loom-km8.6 closing drawer (drawer_loom_decisions_4d49e7555dbbcbf52496539d in loom/decisions). Trial process learned there:

- Round 1: discover (audit + scaffold dry-run, capture friction, file follow-up beads, revert).
- Round 2: validate (re-trial after fixes, decide keep-vs-revert).

Pre-flight observations from this filing:
- ~/repos/tla-puzzles has .beads/ but NO .claude/workflow.json — not loom-managed yet. Full setup means: bd hooks install (if missing), .claude/workflow.json (mode=full likely), .claude/rules/ scaffold for any detected source dirs, MemPalace wing check (tla_puzzles wing already exists with 197 drawers per the round-2 wing scan), then /audit-project → /docs-scaffold.
